... | injвӮӮ tв„“вүЎR | injвӮӮ trвүЎR = вҠҘ-elim (to-distinct-on-boundary d e (trans tв„“вүЎR (sym trвүЎR)))
... | injвӮҒ _ | injвӮӮ _ = orient-preserve
... | injвӮӮ _ | injвӮҒ _ = orient-swap
\end{code}

\section{Automorphisms}
\label{sec:two-normal-form:automorphisms}

An automorphism of a distinction is a self-equivalence: a pair of maps from the carrier to itself that are mutual inverses. The inverse law forces injectivity, and injectivity forces the boundary images to remain distinct (since $\ell \neq r$ would be violated otherwise). With only two elements in the carrier, the only injective endomorphisms are the identity and the swap. That is the complete automorphism group of any distinction: exactly two elements, \texttt{id} and \texttt{dual}.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Automorphism of a distinction.
Aut : Distinction вҶ’ Set1
Aut d = DistinctionEquiv d d

-- В§ Automorphism transport is injective.
to-injective : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ (a : Aut d) вҶ’ {x y : S d} вҶ’ to a x вүЎ to a y вҶ’ x вүЎ y
to-injective d a {x} {y} eq =
  trans (sym (from-to a x)) (trans (cong (from a) eq) (from-to a y))

-- В§ Automorphism images at boundary points are distinct.
to-в„“вү to-r : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ (a : Aut d) вҶ’ to a (в„“ d) вү  to a (r d)
to-в„“вү to-r d a eq = в„“вү r d (to-injective d a eq)

-- В§ Dual boundary lemma: dual sends в„“ to r.
Distinction-dual-в„“ : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ Distinction-dual d (в„“ d) вүЎ r d
Distinction-dual-в„“ d with cover d (в„“ d)
... | injвӮҒ _      = refl
... | injвӮӮ в„“вүЎr    = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r d) в„“вүЎr)

-- В§ Dual boundary lemma: dual sends r to в„“.
Distinction-dual-r : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ Distinction-dual d (r d) вүЎ в„“ d
Distinction-dual-r d with cover d (r d)
... | injвӮӮ _      = refl
... | injвӮҒ rвүЎв„“    = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r d) (sym rвүЎв„“))

-- В§ Automorphism classification: exactly id or dual.
Aut-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  (to a вү— id) вҠҺ (to a вү— Distinction-dual d)
Aut-sound d a with cover d (to a (в„“ d)) | cover d (to a (r d))
... | injвӮҒ tв„“вүЎв„“ | injвӮҒ trвүЎв„“ = вҠҘ-elim (to-в„“вү to-r d a (trans tв„“вүЎв„“ (sym trвүЎв„“)))
... | injвӮӮ tв„“вүЎr | injвӮӮ trвүЎr = вҠҘ-elim (to-в„“вү to-r d a (trans tв„“вүЎr (sym trвүЎr)))
... | injвӮҒ tв„“вүЎв„“ | injвӮӮ trвүЎr =
  injвӮҒ (Distinction-elim d tв„“вүЎв„“ trвүЎr)
... | injвӮӮ tв„“вүЎr | injвӮҒ trвүЎв„“ =
  injвӮӮ (Distinction-elim d
    (trans tв„“вүЎr (sym (Distinction-dual-в„“ d)))
    (trans trвүЎв„“ (sym (Distinction-dual-r d))))
\end{code}

Automorphism classification reaches the third structural milestone here. Figure~\ref{fig:k4-orbits} fixes the symmetry action as a permutation of the four case witnesses.

\begin{figure}[h]
\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[>=Latex, every node/.style={circle, draw=fdBlue, thick, minimum size=9mm, fill=fdLight}]
  \node (a) at (90:2.2) {$c_L$};
  \node (b) at (0:2.2) {$c_R$};
  \node (c) at (270:2.2) {$id$};
  \node (d) at (180:2.2) {$dual$};
  \draw[thick] (a) -- (b) -- (c) -- (d) -- (a);
  \draw[dashed, thick] (a) -- (c);
  \draw[dashed, thick] (b) -- (d);
  \draw[->, very thick, fdRed] ($(a)+(0.0,0.35)$) arc[start angle=90,end angle=0,radius=2.2];
  \draw[->, very thick, fdRed] ($(c)+(0.0,-0.35)$) arc[start angle=270,end angle=180,radius=2.2];
\end{tikzpicture}
\caption{Automorphism action on the four-case witness space: preserve and swap survive as the only orbit structure.}
\label{fig:k4-orbits}
\end{figure}

\subsection{Law 1.9: The Two Isomorphism Is Unique}
\label{law:two-normal-form:isomorphism-unique}

Any boundary-preserving map from a distinction carrier to \texttt{Two} must agree with the canonical map \texttt{toTwo}. The proof is immediate from the eliminator: since the map is determined by its values at $\ell$ and $r$, and those values are fixed by the boundary-preservation requirement, there is exactly one such map. The same holds for the backward direction.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Uniqueness of the to-direction.
toTwo-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S d вҶ’ Two) вҶ’
  f (в„“ d) вүЎ L вҶ’
  f (r d) вүЎ R вҶ’
  f вү— toTwo d
toTwo-unique d f fв„“ fr =
  Distinction-elim d
    (trans fв„“ (sym (toTwo-в„“ d)))
    (trans fr (sym (toTwo-r d)))

-- В§ Uniqueness of the swap-direction.
toTwo-swap-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S d вҶ’ Two) вҶ’
  f (в„“ d) вүЎ R вҶ’
  f (r d) вүЎ L вҶ’
  f вү— toTwo-swap d
toTwo-swap-unique d f fв„“ fr =
  Distinction-elim d
    (trans fв„“ (sym (toTwo-swap-в„“ d)))
    (trans fr (sym (toTwo-swap-r d)))

-- В§ Law 1.9: iso-to is unique.
law1-9-iso-to-unique : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ (i : DistinctionIso d Two-distinction) вҶ’ to i вү— toTwo d
law1-9-iso-to-unique d i =
  toTwo-unique d (to i)
    (trans (to-в„“ i) refl)
    (trans (to-r i) refl)

-- В§ Uniqueness of the from-direction.
fromTwo-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (g : Two вҶ’ S d) вҶ’
  g L вүЎ в„“ d вҶ’
  g R вүЎ r d вҶ’
  g вү— fromTwo d
fromTwo-unique d g gL gR L = trans gL refl
fromTwo-unique d g gL gR R = trans gR refl

-- В§ Law 1.9: iso-from is unique.
law1-9-iso-from-unique : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ (i : DistinctionIso d Two-distinction) вҶ’ from i вү— fromTwo d
law1-9-iso-from-unique d i =
  fromTwo-unique d (from i)
    (trans (sym (cong (from i) (to-в„“ i))) (from-to i (в„“ d)))
    (trans (sym (cong (from i) (to-r i))) (from-to i (r d)))
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.10: Two Normal Form Has Exactly Two Orientations}
\label{law:two-normal-form:two-orientations}

Every equivalence to \texttt{Two} is either the canonical boundary-preserving map or the swap. The proof checks the image of $\ell$: it is either $L$ or $R$. If $L$, the map agrees with \texttt{toTwo}. If $R$, it agrees with \texttt{toTwo-swap}. There is no third possibility, because \texttt{Two} has no third element.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 1.10: orientation is exhaustive.
orientation-exhaustive :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  (to e вү— toTwo d) вҠҺ (to e вү— toTwo-swap d)
orientation-exhaustive d e with Two-cover (to e (в„“ d)) | Two-cover (to e (r d))
... | injвӮҒ tв„“вүЎL | injвӮҒ trвүЎL = вҠҘ-elim (to-distinct-on-boundary d e (trans tв„“вүЎL (sym trвүЎL)))
... | injвӮӮ tв„“вүЎR | injвӮӮ trвүЎR = вҠҘ-elim (to-distinct-on-boundary d e (trans tв„“вүЎR (sym trвүЎR)))
... | injвӮҒ tв„“вүЎL | injвӮӮ trвүЎR = injвӮҒ (toTwo-unique d (to e) tв„“вүЎL trвүЎR)
... | injвӮӮ tв„“вүЎR | injвӮҒ trвүЎL = injвӮӮ (toTwo-swap-unique d (to e) tв„“вүЎR trвүЎL)

law1-10-orientation-exhaustive :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  (to e вү— toTwo d) вҠҺ (to e вү— toTwo-swap d)
law1-10-orientation-exhaustive = orientation-exhaustive
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.11: Automorphisms Are Exactly id Or dual}
\label{law:two-normal-form:automorphisms-id-or-dual}

The boundary behavior of any automorphism is forced into one of two patterns: fix both boundaries (identity) or swap both (dual). Any other combinationвҖ”fixing one and moving the otherвҖ”would produce a non-injective map, which contradicts the inverse law.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 1.11: Automorphisms are exactly id or dual.
law1-11-Aut-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  (to a вү— id) вҠҺ (to a вү— Distinction-dual d)
law1-11-Aut-sound = Aut-sound
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.12: Two-Orientation Classification Is Sound And Unique}
\label{law:two-normal-form:orientation-classification}

The orientation classifier assigns every equivalence to \texttt{Two} one of two labels: \texttt{case-preserve} or \texttt{case-swap}. Soundness: the assigned label interprets back to a map that agrees pointwise with the original equivalence. Uniqueness: no equivalence can receive two different labels, because that would force $L = R$ in \texttt{Two}.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Orientation case type.
data OrientationCase : Set where
  case-preserve : OrientationCase
  case-swap     : OrientationCase

-- В§ Interpret an orientation case as a map.
orientationInterpret : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ OrientationCase вҶ’ S d вҶ’ Two
orientationInterpret d case-preserve = toTwo d
orientationInterpret d case-swap     = toTwo-swap d

-- В§ Law 1.12: orientation classification is sound.
orientationCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  ОЈ OrientationCase (О» c вҶ’ to e вү— orientationInterpret d c)
orientationCase-sound d e with orientation-exhaustive d e
... | injвӮҒ p = case-preserve , p
... | injвӮӮ p = case-swap     , p

-- В§ Law 1.12: orientation classification is unique.
orientationCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : OrientationCase) вҶ’
  to e вү— orientationInterpret d cвӮҒ вҶ’
  to e вү— orientationInterpret d cвӮӮ вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
orientationCase-unique d e case-preserve case-preserve _ _ = refl
orientationCase-unique d e case-swap     case-swap     _ _ = refl
orientationCase-unique d e case-preserve case-swap p q =
  вҠҘ-elim (Two-Lвү R (sym toRвүЎL))
  where
    toRвүЎL : R вүЎ L
    toRвүЎL =
      trans (sym (toTwo-swap-в„“ d))
        (trans (sym (q (в„“ d)))
          (trans (p (в„“ d)) (toTwo-в„“ d)))
orientationCase-unique d e case-swap case-preserve p q =
  вҠҘ-elim (Two-Lвү R (sym toRвүЎL))
  where
    toRвүЎL : R вүЎ L
    toRвүЎL =
      trans (sym (toTwo-swap-в„“ d))
        (trans (sym (p (в„“ d)))
          (trans (q (в„“ d)) (toTwo-в„“ d)))

law1-12-orientationCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  ОЈ OrientationCase (О» c вҶ’ to e вү— orientationInterpret d c)
law1-12-orientationCase-sound = orientationCase-sound

law1-12-orientationCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : OrientationCase) вҶ’
  to e вү— orientationInterpret d cвӮҒ вҶ’
  to e вү— orientationInterpret d cвӮӮ вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
law1-12-orientationCase-unique = orientationCase-unique
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.13: Automorphism Classification Is Sound And Unique}
\label{law:two-normal-form:automorphism-classification}

The same structure holds for automorphisms. Every self-equivalence receives exactly one labelвҖ”\texttt{case-id} or \texttt{case-dual}вҖ”and that label is forced by the boundary behavior. If two different labels could describe the same automorphism, their interpretations would agree pointwise, which would force $\ell = r$ via the boundary lemmas. Contradiction eliminates every unsound or non-unique automorphism witness.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Automorphism case type.
data AutCase : Set where
  case-id   : AutCase
  case-dual : AutCase

-- В§ Interpret an automorphism case.
autInterpret : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ AutCase вҶ’ S d вҶ’ S d
autInterpret d case-id   = id
autInterpret d case-dual = Distinction-dual d

-- В§ Automorphism classification is sound.
autCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  ОЈ AutCase (О» c вҶ’ to a вү— autInterpret d c)
autCase-sound d a with Aut-sound d a
... | injвӮҒ p = case-id   , p
... | injвӮӮ p = case-dual , p

-- В§ Automorphism classification is unique.
autCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : AutCase) вҶ’
  to a вү— autInterpret d cвӮҒ вҶ’
  to a вү— autInterpret d cвӮӮ вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
autCase-unique d a case-id case-id _ _ = refl
autCase-unique d a case-dual case-dual _ _ = refl
autCase-unique d a case-id case-dual p q =
  вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r d) в„“вүЎr)
  where
    в„“вүЎr : в„“ d вүЎ r d
    в„“вүЎr =
      trans (sym (p (в„“ d)))
        (trans (q (в„“ d)) (Distinction-dual-в„“ d))
autCase-unique d a case-dual case-id p q =
  вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r d) в„“вүЎr)
  where
    в„“вүЎr : в„“ d вүЎ r d
    в„“вүЎr =
      trans (sym (q (в„“ d)))
        (trans (p (в„“ d)) (Distinction-dual-в„“ d))

-- В§ Law 1.13: Automorphism classification is sound and unique.
law1-13-autCase-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  ОЈ AutCase (О» c вҶ’ to a вү— autInterpret d c)
law1-13-autCase-sound = autCase-sound

law1-13-autCase-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : AutCase) вҶ’
  to a вү— autInterpret d cвӮҒ вҶ’
  to a вү— autInterpret d cвӮӮ вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
law1-13-autCase-unique = autCase-unique
\end{code}

\section{K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Witness Interface}
\label{sec:classification:k4-witness-interface}

The dependent pair that bundles a case label with a proof that its interpretation matches a given endomorphism is the canonical witness of classification. Every endomorphism receives exactly one such witness. These top-level re-exports make the classification accessible to all subsequent chapters without requiring the \texttt{K\ensuremath{_4}} module to be opened.

\phantomsection\label{law:k4:witness-sound}
\phantomsection\label{law:k4:witness-unique}

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 1.14: KвӮ„ classification produces a witness.
law1-14-k4-classification-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S d вҶ’ S d) вҶ’
  ОЈ EndoCase (О» c вҶ’ KвӮ„.interpret d c вү— f)
law1-14-k4-classification-sound = k4-classification-sound

-- В§ Law 1.15: KвӮ„ classification witness is unique.
law1-15-k4-classification-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S d вҶ’ S d) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : EndoCase) вҶ’
  KвӮ„.interpret d cвӮҒ вү— f вҶ’
  KвӮ„.interpret d cвӮӮ вү— f вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
law1-15-k4-classification-unique = k4-classification-unique
\end{code}

\section{Two-Class Coverage Without Definitional Equality}
\label{sec:classification:coverage-without-definitional-equality}

The preceding classification relies on propositional equality \texttt{\ensuremath{\equiv}} as the notion of sameness. Some contexts require coverage with respect to a weaker equivalence relation $\approx$ that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive but not necessarily definitional. The record \texttt{DistinctionClass} captures this: it carries a carrier, two separated boundary witnesses, an equivalence relation, and exhaustive coverage under that relation. This weakening does not gain expressive powerвҖ”it broadens the range of carriers that can be recognized as distinctions without altering the classification theory.

\phantomsection\label{law:classification:cover-under-equivalence}
\phantomsection\label{law:classification:distinction-to-class}

\begin{code}
-- В§ DistinctionClass: two-class coverage with an arbitrary equivalence relation.
record DistinctionClass : Set1 where
  field
    S      : Set
    _вүҲ_    : S вҶ’ S вҶ’ Set
    вүҲ-refl : (x : S) вҶ’ x вүҲ x
    вүҲ-sym  : {x y : S} вҶ’ x вүҲ y вҶ’ y вүҲ x
    вүҲ-trans : {x y z : S} вҶ’ x вүҲ y вҶ’ y вүҲ z вҶ’ x вүҲ z

    в„“      : S
    r      : S
    в„“вүүr    : В¬ (в„“ вүҲ r)
    coverвүҲ : (x : S) вҶ’ (x вүҲ в„“) вҠҺ (x вүҲ r)

open DistinctionClass public

-- В§ Respect predicate for вүҲ-elimination.
RespectвүҲ : (d : DistinctionClass) вҶ’ (S d вҶ’ Set) вҶ’ Set
RespectвүҲ d P = {x y : S d} вҶ’ _вүҲ_ d x y вҶ’ P x вҶ’ P y

-- В§ Law 1.16: Elimination is forced by coverвүҲ.
DistinctionClass-elim :
  (d : DistinctionClass) вҶ’
  {P : S d вҶ’ Set} вҶ’
  RespectвүҲ d P вҶ’
  P (в„“ d) вҶ’
  P (r d) вҶ’
  (x : S d) вҶ’
  P x
DistinctionClass-elim d {P} resp pв„“ pr x with coverвүҲ d x
... | injвӮҒ xвүҲв„“ = resp ((вүҲ-sym d) xвүҲв„“) pв„“
... | injвӮӮ xвүҲr = resp ((вүҲ-sym d) xвүҲr) pr

law1-16-class-elim :
  (d : DistinctionClass) вҶ’
  {P : S d вҶ’ Set} вҶ’
  RespectвүҲ d P вҶ’
  P (в„“ d) вҶ’
  P (r d) вҶ’
  (x : S d) вҶ’
  P x
law1-16-class-elim = DistinctionClass-elim

-- В§ Law 1.17: Every Distinction induces a DistinctionClass.
DistinctionвҶ’DistinctionClass : Distinction вҶ’ DistinctionClass
DistinctionвҶ’DistinctionClass d = record
  { S       = S d
  ; _вүҲ_     = _вүЎ_
  ; вүҲ-refl  = О» x вҶ’ refl
  ; вүҲ-sym   = sym
  ; вүҲ-trans = trans
  ; в„“       = в„“ d
  ; r       = r d
  ; в„“вүүr     = в„“вү r d
  ; coverвүҲ  = cover d
  }

law1-17-distinction-to-class : Distinction вҶ’ DistinctionClass
law1-17-distinction-to-class = DistinctionвҶ’DistinctionClass
\end{code}

\section{Presentation Elimination}
\label{sec:presentation:elimination}

The four-case classification is canonical, but nothing prevents someone from proposing an alternative presentation: a different type $X$ with its own interpretation and classification functions. The question is whether such an alternative can differ structurally from \texttt{EndoCase}. The answer is no. If a presentation is total (every endomorphism gets a label), sound (the label interprets back correctly), and injective (distinct labels produce distinct behavior), then $X$ is isomorphic to \texttt{EndoCase}. The proof constructs the isomorphism explicitly by round-tripping through the canonical classifier.

\phantomsection\label{law:presentation:endo-unique}

\begin{code}
-- В§ Set isomorphism (not distinction-specific).
record SetIso {в„“вӮҒ в„“вӮӮ : Level} (A : Set в„“вӮҒ) (B : Set в„“вӮӮ) : Set (в„“вӮҒ вҠ” в„“вӮӮ) where
  field
    to      : A вҶ’ B
    from    : B вҶ’ A
    to-from : (y : B) вҶ’ to (from y) вүЎ y
    from-to : (x : A) вҶ’ from (to x) вүЎ x

open SetIso public

-- В§ Endo presentation record.
record EndoPresentation (d : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set where
  field
    present-interpret           : X вҶ’ (S d вҶ’ S d)
    present-classify            : (S d вҶ’ S d) вҶ’ X
    present-classify-sound      : (f : S d вҶ’ S d) вҶ’ present-interpret (present-classify f) вү— f
    present-interpret-injective : (x y : X) вҶ’ present-interpret x вү— present-interpret y вҶ’ x вүЎ y

-- В§ Law 1.18: Endo presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
law1-18-endo-presentation-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  {X : Set} вҶ’
  EndoPresentation d X вҶ’
  SetIso X EndoCase
law1-18-endo-presentation-unique d {X} pres =
  let
    module K = KвӮ„ d
    open EndoPresentation pres
      renaming
        ( present-interpret to interpretX
        ; present-classify to classifyX
        ; present-classify-sound to classifyX-sound
        ; present-interpret-injective to interpretX-injective
        )

    to' : X вҶ’ EndoCase
    to' x = K.classify (interpretX x)

    from' : EndoCase вҶ’ X
    from' c = classifyX (K.interpret c)

    to-from' : (c : EndoCase) вҶ’ to' (from' c) вүЎ c
    to-from' c =
      sym
        (K.classify-unique
          (interpretX (classifyX (K.interpret c)))
          c
          (K.вү—-sym (classifyX-sound (K.interpret c))))

    from-to' : (x : X) вҶ’ from' (to' x) вүЎ x
    from-to' x =
      interpretX-injective
        (classifyX (K.interpret (K.classify (interpretX x))))
        x
        (K.вү—-trans
          (classifyX-sound (K.interpret (K.classify (interpretX x))))
          (K.classify-sound (interpretX x)))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 1.19--1.20: Orientation and Automorphism Presentation Elimination}
\label{laws:presentation:orientation-automorphism-elimination}

The same presentation elimination principle applies to orientation classifications and automorphism classifications.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Helpers for orientation presentation elimination.
orientationCase-classify :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  OrientationCase
orientationCase-classify d e = fst (orientationCase-sound d e)

orientationCase-classify-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’
  to e вү— orientationInterpret d (orientationCase-classify d e)
orientationCase-classify-sound d e = snd (orientationCase-sound d e)

autCase-classify :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  AutCase
autCase-classify d a = fst (autCase-sound d a)

autCase-classify-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (a : Aut d) вҶ’
  to a вү— autInterpret d (autCase-classify d a)
autCase-classify-sound d a = snd (autCase-sound d a)

orientationEquivInterpret :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  OrientationCase вҶ’
  DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction
orientationEquivInterpret d case-preserve = two-normal-form-equiv d
orientationEquivInterpret d case-swap     = two-normal-form-equiv-swap d

orientationEquivInterpret-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (c : OrientationCase) вҶ’
  to (orientationEquivInterpret d c) вү— orientationInterpret d c
orientationEquivInterpret-sound d case-preserve x = refl
orientationEquivInterpret-sound d case-swap     x = refl

-- В§ Identity equivalence.
idEquiv : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ DistinctionEquiv d d
idEquiv d = record
  { to      = id
  ; from    = id
  ; to-from = О» y вҶ’ refl
  ; from-to = О» x вҶ’ refl
  }

-- В§ Dual equivalence.
dualEquiv : (d : Distinction) вҶ’ DistinctionEquiv d d
dualEquiv d = record
  { to      = Distinction-dual d
  ; from    = Distinction-dual d
  ; to-from = law1-3-dual-involutive d
  ; from-to = law1-3-dual-involutive d
  }

autEquivInterpret :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  AutCase вҶ’
  Aut d
autEquivInterpret d case-id   = idEquiv d
autEquivInterpret d case-dual = dualEquiv d

autEquivInterpret-sound :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  (c : AutCase) вҶ’
  to (autEquivInterpret d c) вү— autInterpret d c
autEquivInterpret-sound d case-id   x = refl
autEquivInterpret-sound d case-dual x = refl
\end{code}

\paragraph{Presentation records.}
A presentation of either classification (orientation or automorphism) is a set-valued interface carrying interpretation, classification, soundness, and injectivity.
These records are forced by the requirement that any alternative labeling is isomorphic to the canonical one.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Orientation presentation record.
record OrientationPresentation (d : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set1 where
  field
    op-interpret           : X вҶ’ DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction
    op-classify            : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction вҶ’ X
    op-classify-sound      : (e : DistinctionEquiv d Two-distinction) вҶ’ to (op-interpret (op-classify e)) вү— to e
    op-interpret-injective : (x y : X) вҶ’ to (op-interpret x) вү— to (op-interpret y) вҶ’ x вүЎ y

-- В§ Automorphism presentation record.
record AutPresentation (d : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set1 where
  field
    ap-interpret           : X вҶ’ Aut d
    ap-classify            : Aut d вҶ’ X
    ap-classify-sound      : (a : Aut d) вҶ’ to (ap-interpret (ap-classify a)) вү— to a
    ap-interpret-injective : (x y : X) вҶ’ to (ap-interpret x) вү— to (ap-interpret y) вҶ’ x вүЎ y
\end{code}

\paragraph{Law 1.19: Orientation presentation uniqueness.}
Every orientation presentation is forced to be isomorphic to \texttt{OrientationCase}.
The isomorphism is constructed by composing classification on one side with interpretation on the other; 
soundness and injectivity force the round-trip properties.

\phantomsection\label{law:presentation:orientation-unique}

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 1.19: Orientation presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
law1-19-orientation-presentation-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  {X : Set} вҶ’
  OrientationPresentation d X вҶ’
  SetIso X OrientationCase
law1-19-orientation-presentation-unique d {X} pres =
  let
    open OrientationPresentation pres
      renaming
        ( op-interpret to interpretX
        ; op-classify to classifyX
        ; op-classify-sound to classifyX-sound
        ; op-interpret-injective to interpretX-injective
        )

    вү—-sym : {A B : Set} {f g : A вҶ’ B} вҶ’ f вү— g вҶ’ g вү— f
    вү—-sym p x = sym (p x)

    вү—-trans : {A B : Set} {f g h : A вҶ’ B} вҶ’ f вү— g вҶ’ g вү— h вҶ’ f вү— h
    вү—-trans p q x = trans (p x) (q x)

    to' : X вҶ’ OrientationCase
    to' x = orientationCase-classify d (interpretX x)

    from' : OrientationCase вҶ’ X
    from' c = classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d c)

    to-from' : (c : OrientationCase) вҶ’ to' (from' c) вүЎ c
    to-from' c =
      orientationCase-unique d (interpretX (classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d c)))
        (to' (from' c))
        c
        (orientationCase-classify-sound d (interpretX (classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d c))))
        (вү—-trans
          (classifyX-sound (orientationEquivInterpret d c))
          (orientationEquivInterpret-sound d c))

    from-to' : (x : X) вҶ’ from' (to' x) вүЎ x
    from-to' x =
      interpretX-injective
        (classifyX (orientationEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
        x
        (вү—-trans
          (classifyX-sound (orientationEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
          (вү—-trans
            (orientationEquivInterpret-sound d (to' x))
            (вү—-sym (orientationCase-classify-sound d (interpretX x)))))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }

-- В§ Law 1.20: Automorphism presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
\end{code}

\paragraph{Law 1.20: Automorphism presentation uniqueness.}
The same forcing pattern applies: every automorphism presentation carries exactly the same data as \texttt{AutCase} up to isomorphism.

\phantomsection\label{law:presentation:automorphism-unique}

\begin{code}
law1-20-aut-presentation-unique :
  (d : Distinction) вҶ’
  {X : Set} вҶ’
  AutPresentation d X вҶ’
  SetIso X AutCase
law1-20-aut-presentation-unique d {X} pres =
  let
    open AutPresentation pres
      renaming
        ( ap-interpret to interpretX
        ; ap-classify to classifyX
        ; ap-classify-sound to classifyX-sound
        ; ap-interpret-injective to interpretX-injective
        )

    вү—-sym : {A B : Set} {f g : A вҶ’ B} вҶ’ f вү— g вҶ’ g вү— f
    вү—-sym p x = sym (p x)

    вү—-trans : {A B : Set} {f g h : A вҶ’ B} вҶ’ f вү— g вҶ’ g вү— h вҶ’ f вү— h
    вү—-trans p q x = trans (p x) (q x)

    to' : X вҶ’ AutCase
    to' x = autCase-classify d (interpretX x)

    from' : AutCase вҶ’ X
    from' c = classifyX (autEquivInterpret d c)

    to-from' : (c : AutCase) вҶ’ to' (from' c) вүЎ c
    to-from' c =
      autCase-unique d (interpretX (classifyX (autEquivInterpret d c)))
        (to' (from' c))
        c
        (autCase-classify-sound d (interpretX (classifyX (autEquivInterpret d c))))
        (вү—-trans
          (classifyX-sound (autEquivInterpret d c))
          (autEquivInterpret-sound d c))

    from-to' : (x : X) вҶ’ from' (to' x) вүЎ x
    from-to' x =
      interpretX-injective
        (classifyX (autEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
        x
        (вү—-trans
          (classifyX-sound (autEquivInterpret d (to' x)))
          (вү—-trans
            (autEquivInterpret-sound d (to' x))
            (вү—-sym (autCase-classify-sound d (interpretX x)))))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }
\end{code}

The canonical Two reference and D$_0$ are established last.

\begin{code}
-- В§ DвӮҖ is the canonical first distinction.
DвӮҖ : Set
DвӮҖ = Two

-- В§ The left boundary.
left : DвӮҖ
left = L

-- В§ The right boundary.
right : DвӮҖ
right = R

-- В§ DвӮҖ as a Distinction record.
DвӮҖ-distinction : Distinction
DвӮҖ-distinction = Two-distinction
\end{code}

The primitive distinction is stable. Equality, negation, decidability, witness structure, and the four endomorphism cases exist as machine-checked constraints on a two-element carrier. The base classification is locked.

But a classification that is not itself classified leaves a residue outside the filter. The next pressure is therefore unavoidable: the classifier must become an object inside the argument, and the argument must climb.

\paragraph{A number to remember.}
Four endomorphism cases have survived elimination. This number was not chosen---it fell out of the exhaustive analysis of a two-element carrier. It will reappear. When the argument finally converges on a graph, that graph will have exactly four vertices, and the combinatorial invariants of that graph---edge count, Euler characteristic, spectral decomposition---will produce specific integers. The reader who notices the number four here and remembers it through the next hundred pages will recognize it again in a less abstract setting.

\part{Drift and Reachability}

Part I sealed the base and drew the first mark. A distinction appeared. Its carrier admitted exactly four endomorphisms. Those four maps were mutually distinct, exhaustive, and could not be collapsed further. The graph $K_4$ was already implicit in that count---four vertices, one for each endomorphism, connected because each endomorphism can be composed with every other.

But the classification itself is now a new mathematical object. The type \texttt{EndoCase}, the classifier function, the mutual distinctness proofs---none of these lived in the original carrier. They live one level above it. And that new object is not yet classified. Stopping here would leave an unclassified residue in the ontology, which is precisely the kind of residual freedom the filter is designed to eliminate.

Every act of classification produces a new unclassified object: the classification record itself. This is not a failure of the method---it is a structural property of any system that takes classification seriously. The minimal response is not to stop but to classify again, one level higher, each time inheriting the exact structure of the level below.

The result is a canonical tower. Agda's universe hierarchy is not an external convention imposed on the argument; it is the shape the argument is forced to take. The reachability proofs in this part are the formal record of ascent through that tower: which levels are accessible from which, and by what minimal paths.

The specificity begins here. What emerges is not ``a tower of some height'' but a tower whose structure determines its successor and whose reachability relation admits no cycles. This rigidity is what later forces the survivor to be a single named graph rather than a family of candidates---and what later forces the arithmetic built on that graph to be similarly locked. The eliminative method, applied to its own infrastructure, leaves no room for choice.

\chapter{Drift}
\label{ch:drift}

The new classification lives at a higher universe level, because it quantifies over the objects of the previous level. This is drift: the canonical step from one level to the next, carrying the old distinction into a lifted carrier that can accommodate the expanded ontology.

The drift step is not an invented construction. The structural demand is precise: every classification must itself be classifiable, and the type-theoretic hierarchy of universe levels is the unique mechanism that makes this possible without circularity.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Level-polymorphic distinction record.
record Distinctionв„“ (в„“ : Level) : Set (lsuc в„“) where
  field
    S     : Set в„“
    в„“вӮҖ    : S
    rвӮҖ    : S
    в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ : в„“вӮҖ вү  rвӮҖ
    cover : (x : S) вҶ’ (x вүЎ в„“вӮҖ) вҠҺ (x вүЎ rвӮҖ)

open Distinctionв„“ public

-- В§ Canonical embedding from base-level Distinction.
DistinctionвҶ’Distinctionв„“ : Distinction вҶ’ Distinctionв„“ lzero
DistinctionвҶ’Distinctionв„“ d = record
  { S     = S d
  ; в„“вӮҖ    = в„“ d
  ; rвӮҖ    = r d
  ; в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ = в„“вү r d
  ; cover = cover d
  }

-- В§ A drift step: the next-level distinction with an embedding.
record DriftStep {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) : Set (lsuc (lsuc в„“)) where
  field
    dвҶ‘    : Distinctionв„“ (lsuc в„“)
    embed : S d вҶ’ S dвҶ‘

open DriftStep public

-- В§ Drift is the canonical lift to the next universe level.
drift : {в„“ : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ DriftStep d
drift d = record
  { dвҶ‘ = record
      { S     = Lift (S d)
      ; в„“вӮҖ    = lift (в„“вӮҖ d)
      ; rвӮҖ    = lift (rвӮҖ d)
      ; в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ = О» eq вҶ’ в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ d (lift-injective eq)
    ; cover = coverвҶ‘
      }
  ; embed = lift
  }
  where
  coverвҶ‘ : (y : Lift (S d)) вҶ’ (y вүЎ lift (в„“вӮҖ d)) вҠҺ (y вүЎ lift (rвӮҖ d))
  coverвҶ‘ (lift x) with cover d x
  ... | injвӮҒ xвүЎв„“ = injвӮҒ (cong lift xвүЎв„“)
  ... | injвӮӮ xвүЎr = injвӮӮ (cong lift xвүЎr)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 2.1: No Classification May Remain Unclassified}
\label{law:drift:no-classification-unclassified}

This is the law of closure. If a classification exists in the system, it must be classifiableвҖ”otherwise it sits outside the filter, untouched by elimination, a free parameter. The drift step provides the necessary embedding: the old carrier lifts into the new one, and the boundary witnesses lift with it.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 2.1: No classification may remain unclassified.
law2-1-drift : {в„“ : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ DriftStep d
law2-1-drift = drift

-- В§ Extract the next-level distinction from a drift step.
drift-next : {в„“ : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ Distinctionв„“ (lsuc в„“)
drift-next d = dвҶ‘ (drift d)

-- В§ Extract the embedding from a drift step.
drift-embed : {в„“ : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ S d вҶ’ S (drift-next d)
drift-embed d = embed (drift d)

-- В§ Drift-embedded elements satisfy coverage.
drift-embed-cover : {в„“ : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (x : S d)
                 вҶ’ (drift-embed d x вүЎ в„“вӮҖ (drift-next d)) вҠҺ (drift-embed d x вүЎ rвӮҖ (drift-next d))
drift-embed-cover d x = cover (drift-next d) (drift-embed d x)
\end{code}

Drift is established: it lifts any distinction one universe level higher, preserving the boundary and coverage structure verbatim. The question that remains is whether this lift is safe---whether it distorts the carrier it inherits.

\chapter{Non-Collapse of Drift}
\label{ch:drift-noncollapse}

Drift lifts the carrier to a higher universe level via \texttt{lift}. The critical question: does this lifting identify elements that were previously distinct? If it did, the boundary structure of the old distinction would collapseвҖ”$\ell$ and $r$ might become equal in the lifted carrier. But \texttt{lift} is injective (its inverse \texttt{lower} is a witness), so the old separation survives verbatim. Drift expands the universe without folding the carrier.

\subsection{Law 3.1: Drift Does Not Fold The Prior Carrier}
\label{law:drift:prior-carrier-not-folded}

The proof is immediate: \texttt{lift} has an inverse (\texttt{lower}), so any equation between lifted images reflects back to the originals.
Boundary distinctness survives as a corollary---if $\ell_0$ and $r_0$ could become equal after embedding, they would already be equal before it.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 3.1: Drift does not fold the prior carrier.
law3-1-embed-injective :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ {x y : S d} вҶ’
  drift-embed d x вүЎ drift-embed d y вҶ’ x вүЎ y
law3-1-embed-injective d = lift-injective

-- В§ Drift preserves boundary distinctness.
drift-embed-в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’
  drift-embed d (в„“вӮҖ d) вү  drift-embed d (rвӮҖ d)
drift-embed-в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ d eq = в„“вӮҖвү rвӮҖ d (law3-1-embed-injective d eq)
\end{code}

The lift is injective and the boundary separation survives. Drift expands the universe without folding the carrier. The next question is whether drift is unique among lifts that preserve this structure.

\chapter{Cross-Stage Comparison}
\label{ch:cross-stage-comparison}

Drift produces the simplest possible lift: \texttt{lift} wraps each element, and the boundary witnesses are preserved by \texttt{refl}. But there might be other ways to embed a distinction into a higher levelвҖ”other choices of target carrier and embedding map that also preserve the boundary structure. The question is whether drift is in any way special among these alternatives.

It is. Drift is \textit{universal}: for any boundary-preserving lift, there exists a unique mediating map from the drift carrier to the alternative carrier that makes the diagram commute. This is the universal property in the categorical senseвҖ”drift is the initial object in the category of boundary-preserving lifts. Any alternative lift factors through drift, and the factorization is unique.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Level-polymorphic eliminator for Distinctionв„“.
Distinctionв„“-elim :
  {в„“ в„“P : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’
  {P : S d вҶ’ Set в„“P} вҶ’
  P (в„“вӮҖ d) вҶ’
  P (rвӮҖ d) вҶ’
  (x : S d) вҶ’
  P x
Distinctionв„“-elim d {P} pв„“ pr x with cover d x
... | injвӮҒ xвүЎв„“ = subst P (sym xвүЎв„“) pв„“
... | injвӮӮ xвүЎr = subst P (sym xвүЎr) pr

-- В§ Functions out of Distinctionв„“ are determined by boundary values.
Distinctionв„“-determined :
  {в„“ в„“B : Level} вҶ’ (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ {B : Set в„“B} вҶ’
  (f g : S d вҶ’ B) вҶ’
  f (в„“вӮҖ d) вүЎ g (в„“вӮҖ d) вҶ’
  f (rвӮҖ d) вүЎ g (rвӮҖ d) вҶ’
  f вү— g
Distinctionв„“-determined d f g fв„“вүЎgв„“ frвүЎgr =
  Distinctionв„“-elim d
    (subst (О» y вҶ’ f y вүЎ g y) refl fв„“вүЎgв„“)
    (subst (О» y вҶ’ f y вүЎ g y) refl frвүЎgr)

-- В§ A boundary-preserving lift: target distinction + embedding + boundary proofs.
record LiftedBP {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) : Set (lsuc (lsuc в„“)) where
  field
    e        : Distinctionв„“ (lsuc в„“)
    embed    : S d вҶ’ S e
    embed-в„“вӮҖ : embed (в„“вӮҖ d) вүЎ в„“вӮҖ e
    embed-rвӮҖ : embed (rвӮҖ d) вүЎ rвӮҖ e

open LiftedBP public

-- В§ Drift universality: drift is initial among boundary-preserving lifts.
record DriftUniversal : SetПү where
  field
    preserves-в„“вӮҖ : {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’
      drift-embed d (в„“вӮҖ d) вүЎ в„“вӮҖ (drift-next d)
    preserves-rвӮҖ : {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’
      drift-embed d (rвӮҖ d) вүЎ rвӮҖ (drift-next d)

    mediator : {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
      S (drift-next d) вҶ’ S (e t)

    mediator-commutes : {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
      (x : S d) вҶ’ mediator d t (drift-embed d x) вүЎ embed t x

open DriftUniversal public

-- В§ The canonical drift universality witness.
driftUniversal : DriftUniversal
driftUniversal = record
  { preserves-в„“вӮҖ = О» d вҶ’ refl
  ; preserves-rвӮҖ = О» d вҶ’ refl
  ; mediator = О» d t y вҶ’ embed t (lower y)
  ; mediator-commutes = О» d t x вҶ’ refl
  }
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 4.1: Drift Is Universal Among Boundary-Preserving Lifts}
\label{law:drift:universal-lift}

Universality is witnessed constructively: the mediator sends each lifted element back through \texttt{lower} and re-embeds via the alternative lift.
Commutativity is definitional (\texttt{refl}), and uniqueness follows because both distinctions have exactly two elements---any function agreeing on both boundary values agrees everywhere.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 4.1: Drift is universal among boundary-preserving lifts.
law4-1-mediator-commutes :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
  (x : S d) вҶ’ mediator driftUniversal d t (drift-embed d x) вүЎ embed t x
law4-1-mediator-commutes d t x = mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t x

-- В§ Mediator uniqueness: any factorizing map agrees with the canonical one.
mediator-unique :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
  (g : S (drift-next d) вҶ’ S (e t)) вҶ’
  ((x : S d) вҶ’ g (drift-embed d x) вүЎ embed t x) вҶ’
  g вү— mediator driftUniversal d t
mediator-unique d t g g-comm =
  Distinctionв„“-determined (drift-next d) g h gв„“вүЎhв„“ grвүЎhr
  where
    h : S (drift-next d) вҶ’ S (e t)
    h = mediator driftUniversal d t

    gв„“вүЎhв„“ : g (в„“вӮҖ (drift-next d)) вүЎ h (в„“вӮҖ (drift-next d))
    gв„“вүЎhв„“ =
      trans (cong g (sym (preserves-в„“вӮҖ driftUniversal d)))
        (trans (g-comm (в„“вӮҖ d))
          (trans (sym (mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t (в„“вӮҖ d)))
            (cong h (preserves-в„“вӮҖ driftUniversal d))))

    grвүЎhr : g (rвӮҖ (drift-next d)) вүЎ h (rвӮҖ (drift-next d))
    grвүЎhr =
      trans (cong g (sym (preserves-rвӮҖ driftUniversal d)))
        (trans (g-comm (rвӮҖ d))
          (trans (sym (mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t (rвӮҖ d)))
            (cong h (preserves-rвӮҖ driftUniversal d))))

-- В§ Drift as a LiftedBP instance.
driftLiftedBP : {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ LiftedBP d
driftLiftedBP d = record
  { e        = drift-next d
  ; embed    = drift-embed d
  ; embed-в„“вӮҖ = preserves-в„“вӮҖ driftUniversal d
  ; embed-rвӮҖ = preserves-rвӮҖ driftUniversal d
  }

-- В§ Morphism between lifted boundary-preserving presentations.
record LiftMorph {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) (t u : LiftedBP d) : Set (lsuc (lsuc в„“)) where
  field
    map  : S (e t) вҶ’ S (e u)
    comm : (x : S d) вҶ’ map (embed t x) вүЎ embed u x

open LiftMorph public

-- В§ The canonical factorization through drift.
drift-factor :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
  LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t
drift-factor d t = record
  { map  = mediator driftUniversal d t
  ; comm = mediator-commutes driftUniversal d t
  }

-- В§ Factorization through drift is unique.
drift-factor-unique :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
  (m : LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t) вҶ’
  map m вү— mediator driftUniversal d t
drift-factor-unique d t m =
  mediator-unique d t (map m) (comm m)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 4.2: Drift-Step Factorization Is Unique}
\label{law:drift:factorization-unique}

If two mediating maps both make the diagram commute, they must agree at the boundaryвҖ”and by the determination principle, agreement at the boundary forces agreement everywhere. There is no room for a second factorization.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 4.2: Drift-step factorization is unique.
law4-2-factor-unique :
  {в„“ : Level} (d : Distinctionв„“ в„“) вҶ’ (t : LiftedBP d) вҶ’
  (m : LiftMorph d (driftLiftedBP d) t) вҶ’
  map m вү— mediator driftUniversal d t
law4-2-factor-unique = drift-factor-unique
\end{code}

Drift is universal: every boundary-preserving lift factors through it, and the factorization is unique. No alternative lift can bypass the drift step. The tower is therefore canonical---the depth of the reachability chain is the next question.

\chapter{Drift Reachability}
\label{ch:drift-reachability}

Drift does not stop. There is no final universe level where the process terminatesвҖ”every distinction at level $\ell$ drifts to level $\ell + 1$, and that lifted distinction drifts again. The reachability relation captures this infinite progression: state $t$ is reachable from state $s$ if there is a finite chain of drift steps connecting them. Strict reachability ($\texttt{Reach}^+$) requires at least one step; reflexive-transitive reachability ($\texttt{Reach}$) allows zero.

The key structural facts follow from the uniqueness of successors. Since each drift state has exactly one successor, there is no branchingвҖ”the reachability order is a total preorder on any connected chain. Every finite chain extends by one more step. And the chain never loops back (as the next chapter proves).

\begin{code}
-- В§ A drift state packages a universe level with its distinction.
record DriftState : SetПү where
  constructor вҹЁ_,_вҹ©
  field
    в„“ : Level
    d : Distinctionв„“ в„“

open DriftState public

-- В§ Step a drift state forward.
stepState : DriftState вҶ’ DriftState
stepState s = вҹЁ lsuc (в„“ s) , drift-next (d s) вҹ©

-- В§ Extract the carrier of a drift state.
Carrier : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ Set (в„“ s)
Carrier s = S (d s)

-- В§ The canonical embedding between consecutive carriers.
state-embed : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ Carrier s вҶ’ Carrier (stepState s)
state-embed s = drift-embed (d s)

-- В§ Strict reachability: one or more drift steps.
data ReachвҒә : DriftState вҶ’ DriftState вҶ’ SetПү where
  one  : {s : DriftState} вҶ’ ReachвҒә s (stepState s)
  more : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ ReachвҒә (stepState s) t вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t

-- В§ Reflexive-transitive reachability.
data Reach : DriftState вҶ’ DriftState вҶ’ SetПү where
  stop : {s : DriftState} вҶ’ Reach s s
  next : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t вҶ’ Reach s t
\end{code}

\begin{code}
-- В§ ReachвҒә eliminator.
ReachвҒә-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t вҶ’ SetПү} вҶ’
  ({s : DriftState} вҶ’ P s (stepState s) one) вҶ’
  ({s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : ReachвҒә (stepState s) t) вҶ’ P (stepState s) t p вҶ’ P s t (more p)) вҶ’
  {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : ReachвҒә s t) вҶ’ P s t p
ReachвҒә-elim {P} base step one = base
ReachвҒә-elim {P} base step (more p) = step p (ReachвҒә-elim {P} base step p)

-- В§ Reach eliminator.
Reach-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ SetПү} вҶ’
  ({s : DriftState} вҶ’ P s s stop) вҶ’
  ({s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : ReachвҒә s t) вҶ’ P s t (next p)) вҶ’
  {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : Reach s t) вҶ’ P s t p
Reach-elim stopCase nextCase stop = stopCase
Reach-elim stopCase nextCase (next p) = nextCase p

-- В§ Strict reachability is transitive.
ReachвҒә-trans : {s t u : DriftState} вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t вҶ’ ReachвҒә t u вҶ’ ReachвҒә s u
ReachвҒә-trans one      q = more q
ReachвҒә-trans (more p) q = more (ReachвҒә-trans p q)

-- В§ Strict successors are comparable.
ReachвҒә-comparable :
  {s tвӮҒ tвӮӮ : DriftState} вҶ’
  ReachвҒә s tвӮҒ вҶ’ ReachвҒә s tвӮӮ вҶ’
  (Reach tвӮҒ tвӮӮ) вҠҺПү (Reach tвӮӮ tвӮҒ)
ReachвҒә-comparable one one = injвӮҒПү stop
ReachвҒә-comparable one (more q) = injвӮҒПү (next q)
ReachвҒә-comparable (more p) one = injвӮӮПү (next p)
ReachвҒә-comparable (more p) (more q) =
  ReachвҒә-comparable p q

-- В§ Reflexive-transitive reachability is transitive.
reach-trans : {s t u : DriftState} вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ Reach t u вҶ’ Reach s u
reach-trans stop     q        = q
reach-trans (next p) stop     = next p
reach-trans (next p) (next q) = next (ReachвҒә-trans p q)

-- В§ Every strict chain extends by one step.
ReachвҒә-extend : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t вҶ’ ReachвҒә s (stepState t)
ReachвҒә-extend p = ReachвҒә-trans p one

-- В§ Every reflexive chain extends by one step.
Reach-extend : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ Reach s (stepState t)
Reach-extend stop     = next one
Reach-extend (next p) = next (ReachвҒә-extend p)

-- В§ Strict reachability as an infix operator.
infix 20 _вүә_
_вүә_ : DriftState вҶ’ DriftState вҶ’ SetПү
s вүә t = ReachвҒә s t

-- В§ Strict reachability is transitive (infix form).
вүә-trans : {s t u : DriftState} вҶ’ (s вүә t) вҶ’ (t вүә u) вҶ’ (s вүә u)
вүә-trans p q = ReachвҒә-trans p q

-- В§ Every drift state has a strict successor.
drift-progress : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ s вүә stepState s
drift-progress s = one

-- В§ Terminal state: no strict successor exists.
Terminal : DriftState вҶ’ SetПү
Terminal s = (t : DriftState) вҶ’ (s вүә t) вҶ’ вҠҘ

-- В§ No terminal state exists (internal proof).
no-terminal : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ Terminal s вҶ’ вҠҘ
no-terminal s term = term (stepState s) (drift-progress s)

-- В§ Reachability induces carrier embedding (strict).
reachвҒә-embed : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t вҶ’ Carrier s вҶ’ Carrier t
reachвҒә-embed {s} one      x = state-embed s x
reachвҒә-embed {s} (more p) x = reachвҒә-embed p (state-embed s x)

-- В§ Reachability induces carrier embedding (reflexive).
reach-embed : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ Carrier s вҶ’ Carrier t
reach-embed {s} stop     x = x
reach-embed {s} (next p) x = reachвҒә-embed p x
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 5.0: Drift Admits No Terminal State}
\label{law:reachability:no-terminal-state}

If a terminal state existed, then the strict successor forced by \texttt{ReachвҒә.one} would contradict terminality.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 5.0: Drift admits no terminal state.
law5-0-no-terminal : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ Terminal s вҶ’ вҠҘ
law5-0-no-terminal = no-terminal
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 5.1: Strict Successors Are Comparable}
\label{law:reachability:strict-successors-comparable}

Any two states strictly reachable from the same source must be comparable: one reaches the other.
This is forced by the linear structure of drift---each step adds exactly one new state, so the reachability chain cannot branch.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 5.1: Strict successors are comparable.
law5-1-comparable :
  {s tвӮҒ tвӮӮ : DriftState} вҶ’
  s вүә tвӮҒ вҶ’
  s вүә tвӮӮ вҶ’
  (Reach tвӮҒ tвӮӮ) вҠҺПү (Reach tвӮӮ tвӮҒ)
law5-1-comparable = ReachвҒә-comparable
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 5.2: Every Finite Chain Extends}
\label{law:reachability:finite-chain-extends}

Every finite chain of drift steps can be extended by one more step.
This witnesses the non-termination of drift: no matter how far the chain reaches, \texttt{stepState} always produces a fresh successor.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 5.2: Every finite chain extends (strict).
law5-2-extendвҒә :
  {s t : DriftState} вҶ’
  s вүә t вҶ’
  s вүә stepState t
law5-2-extendвҒә p = ReachвҒә-extend p

-- В§ Law 5.2: Every finite chain extends (reflexive).
law5-2-extend :
  {s t : DriftState} вҶ’
  Reach s t вҶ’
  Reach s (stepState t)
law5-2-extend = Reach-extend
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 5.3--5.7: Closure and Induction}
\label{laws:reachability:closure-induction}

The remaining structural laws---transitivity of strict and reflexive reachability, the carrier-embedding forced by each reachability witness, and the eliminators for both \texttt{ReachвҒә} and \texttt{Reach}---are direct consequences of the inductive definitions. %
They close the reachability algebra and provide the induction principles that all later arguments over drift chains will invoke.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 5.3: Strict reachability is transitive.
law5-3-вүә-trans : {s t u : DriftState} вҶ’ (s вүә t) вҶ’ (t вүә u) вҶ’ (s вүә u)
law5-3-вүә-trans = вүә-trans

-- В§ Law 5.4: Reachability is transitive.
law5-4-reach-trans : {s t u : DriftState} вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ Reach t u вҶ’ Reach s u
law5-4-reach-trans = reach-trans

-- В§ Law 5.5: Reachability forces carrier-embedding.
law5-5-reach-embed : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ Carrier s вҶ’ Carrier t
law5-5-reach-embed = reach-embed

-- В§ Law 5.6: ReachвҒә eliminator is forced.
law5-6-ReachвҒә-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) вҶ’ ReachвҒә s t вҶ’ SetПү} вҶ’
  ({s : DriftState} вҶ’ P s (stepState s) one) вҶ’
  ({s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : ReachвҒә (stepState s) t) вҶ’ P (stepState s) t p вҶ’ P s t (more p)) вҶ’
  {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : ReachвҒә s t) вҶ’ P s t p
law5-6-ReachвҒә-elim = ReachвҒә-elim

-- В§ Law 5.7: Reach eliminator is forced.
law5-7-Reach-elim :
  {P : (s t : DriftState) вҶ’ Reach s t вҶ’ SetПү} вҶ’
  ({s : DriftState} вҶ’ P s s stop) вҶ’
  ({s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : ReachвҒә s t) вҶ’ P s t (next p)) вҶ’
  {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : Reach s t) вҶ’ P s t p
law5-7-Reach-elim = Reach-elim
\end{code}

Reachability is explicit: every finite chain extends, strict successors are comparable, and the eliminator is forced. The chain stretches forward without bound. The last structural hazard is circularity---whether the chain could loop back and collapse the hierarchy it has built.

\chapter{Drift Acyclicity}
\label{ch:drift-acyclicity}

A tower that never stops climbing is not safe merely because it grows. If a chain of drift steps could fold back on itself---returning to a state already visited---then the hierarchy would collapse into a finite loop, and the infinite ascent would be an illusion. Without acyclicity, the hierarchy would apply only to cycles, not to a genuine progression. The elimination strategy demands that the tower be rigid: once a state is left behind, it is left behind permanently.

The drift sequence is infiniteвҖ”but is it linear? Could a chain of drift steps loop back to a previous state? If it could, then a distinction at some level $\ell$ would be identified with a distinction at a higher level $\ell + k$, collapsing the universe hierarchy. This is structurally impossible: universe levels in Agda are well-ordered, and \texttt{lsuc} is strictly increasing. But the formal system must witness this impossibility explicitly. The acyclicity constraint states that no drift state is strictly reachable from itself, and the consequencesвҖ”irreflexivity, asymmetry, and absence of fixed pointsвҖ”follow immediately.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Acyclicity constraint on drift.
record DriftAcyclic : SetПү where
  field
    no-cycle : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ (s вүә s) вҶ’ вҠҘ

open DriftAcyclic public
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.0: Drift Has No Cycles}
\label{law:acyclicity:no-cycles}

Acyclicity is recorded as a constraint: any cycle $s \prec s$ leads to absurdity.
The implementation extracts the contradiction directly from the \texttt{DriftAcyclic} record.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 6.0: Drift has no cycles.
law6-0-no-cycle : DriftAcyclic вҶ’ (s : DriftState) вҶ’ (s вүә s) вҶ’ вҠҘ
law6-0-no-cycle ac s p = no-cycle ac s p
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.1: Strict Reachability Is Irreflexive}
\label{law:acyclicity:irreflexive}

Irreflexivity is the immediate instance of acyclicity: $s \prec s$ is itself a cycle of length one.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 6.1: Strict reachability is irreflexive.
law6-1-irreflexive : DriftAcyclic вҶ’ (s : DriftState) вҶ’ (s вүә s) вҶ’ вҠҘ
law6-1-irreflexive ac s = no-cycle ac s
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.2: Strict Reachability Is Asymmetric}
\label{law:acyclicity:asymmetric}

If both $s \prec t$ and $t \prec s$ held, transitivity would produce the cycle $s \prec s$, contradicting acyclicity.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 6.2: Strict reachability is asymmetric.
law6-2-asymmetric :
  DriftAcyclic вҶ’ {s t : DriftState} вҶ’
  (s вүә t) вҶ’ (t вүә s) вҶ’ вҠҘ
law6-2-asymmetric ac p q = no-cycle ac _ (вүә-trans p q)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.3: Drift-Step Has No Fixed Point}
\label{law:acyclicity:no-fixed-point}

If \texttt{stepState}~$s$ equalled $s$, then the single-step witness \texttt{one} could be transported into a self-loop $s \prec s$, contradicting acyclicity.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 6.3: Drift-step has no fixed point.
law6-3-no-fixpoint-stepState : DriftAcyclic вҶ’ (s : DriftState) вҶ’ stepState s вүЎПү s вҶ’ вҠҘ
law6-3-no-fixpoint-stepState ac s eq =
  no-cycle ac s (substПү (О» u вҶ’ ReachвҒә s u) eq one)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 6.4: Drift Is a Strict Total Order}
\label{law:acyclicity:strict-total-order}

The pieces are now in place. Strict reachability $\prec$ is transitive (Law~5.3), comparable (Law~5.1), and---under acyclicity---irreflexive (Law~6.1) and asymmetric (Law~6.2). These four properties together constitute a strict total order on the set of drift states. The following record bundles them as a single citable theorem. No new proof work is required: every field is a direct appeal to an already-established law.

In ordinal-theoretic terms, the drift hierarchy is order-isomorphic to an initial segment of $\omega$. Each state occupies a unique position in the progression; no branching occurs (comparability), no regression occurs (asymmetry), and no stagnation occurs (irreflexivity). The tower is rigid.

\textbf{Constraint:} A hierarchy without a total order could branch or merge, allowing two incomparable development paths. Rigidity demands that any two states stand in a definite before-or-after relation.

\textbf{Elimination:} Branching fails because comparability is forced by the linear structure of drift. Regression fails because asymmetry follows from acyclicity. Stagnation fails because irreflexivity follows from the same acyclicity. Every alternative to a strict total order is thereby annihilated.

\textbf{Consequence:} The drift hierarchy admits no alternative ordering. The progression is determined, the sequence is unique, and the ordinal type of the tower is $\omega$.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Strict total order on drift states
record DriftStrictTotalOrder (ac : DriftAcyclic) : SetПү where
  field
    sto-trans       : {s t u : DriftState} вҶ’ (s вүә t) вҶ’ (t вүә u) вҶ’ (s вүә u)
    sto-irrefl      : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ (s вүә s) вҶ’ вҠҘ
    sto-asym        : {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (s вүә t) вҶ’ (t вүә s) вҶ’ вҠҘ
    sto-comparable  : {s tвӮҒ tвӮӮ : DriftState} вҶ’
                       s вүә tвӮҒ вҶ’ s вүә tвӮӮ вҶ’ (Reach tвӮҒ tвӮӮ) вҠҺПү (Reach tвӮӮ tвӮҒ)

-- В§ Theorem: drift is a strict total order
theorem-drift-strict-total-order :
  (ac : DriftAcyclic) вҶ’ DriftStrictTotalOrder ac
theorem-drift-strict-total-order ac = record
  { sto-trans      = law5-3-вүә-trans
  ; sto-irrefl     = law6-1-irreflexive ac
  ; sto-asym       = law6-2-asymmetric ac
  ; sto-comparable = law5-1-comparable
  }
\end{code}

The climb through the universe hierarchy is explicit. Drift does not terminate, reachability records the ascent, and acyclicity prevents the hierarchy from folding back onto itself. The argument carries vertical structure without losing rigidity.

But that is still not enough. If the four surviving cases depended on the source and target being the same carrier, then the classification would be parochial rather than structural. The next task is therefore to remove that loophole.

\part{Heteromorphisms and Presentation}

Part II built a tower: every self-classification step carries the structure one level higher, and the reachability facts between those levels form a well-founded order. The drift hierarchy is not circular, and no level collapses back into a previous one. What the tower established, though, is a picture of maps within a single carrier---endomorphisms of the distinction. The question now presses from a different direction. Two carriers exist; maps between them should face the same combinatorial constraints as maps within one.

The classification established in Part~I was confined to maps from a distinction carrier to itself. The question now is whether relaxing that restriction changes anything. It does not---the same two-boundary combinatorics governs every map, regardless of whether domain and codomain coincide.

This consistency matters---but it matters more than it might first appear. If maps \emph{between} carriers are just as constrained as maps \emph{within} a single carrier, then the four-case structure is not an artifact of the self-mapping assumption. It is a property of the two-class distinction itself, holding regardless of which carrier provides the domain and which the codomain. The confirmation shuts another door: there is no freedom in the map type, and there is no freedom in how the map type is represented. The presentation theorems that follow press this further: every reachability fact between levels reduces to a finite canonical record, and every canonical record to its minimal representative.

The singleton character of the survivor begins to crystallize here. If the presentation admitted more than one canonical form, the chain would fork and the later invariant record could not be unique. That the canonical form is itself unique---not merely existent---is what transmits the eliminative pressure forward: from infrastructure through the tower, through the cross-carrier maps, toward the single graph and the forced arithmetic that graph demands.

\chapter{Heteromorphisms}
\label{ch:heteromorphisms}

A map $f : S_{d_1} \to S_{d_2}$ between two distinct distinction carriers is governed by the same two-boundary constraint as the endomorphisms of Part~I: its behavior is determined by its values on the two source classes, each forced into one of the two target classes. The $2 \times 2$ combinatorics is identical; injectivity, soundness, and uniqueness carry over without modification.

This chapter lifts the entire classification machineryвҖ”interpretation, classification, soundness, injectivity, uniquenessвҖ”from the endomorphism setting to the heteromorphism setting. The proofs are structurally identical; only the types change. The four canonical maps between two distinctions are: constant-left, constant-right, the boundary-preserving map (\texttt{LR}), and the boundary-swapping map (\texttt{RL}).

\paragraph{Step 1: Map type and canonical inter-distinction maps.}
The map type, pointwise equivalence, and four canonical maps between two distinction-carriers mirror the endomorphism case.
The two mixed maps \texttt{LR} and \texttt{RL} use source-side coverage to assign the appropriate target boundary witness.

\begin{code}
-- В§ KвӮ„ maps between two distinctions.
module KвӮ„Map (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) where
  Map : Set
  Map = S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ

  -- В§ Pointwise equality on maps.
  вү—-refl : {f : Map} вҶ’ f вү— f
  вү—-refl x = refl

  вү—-sym : {f g : Map} вҶ’ f вү— g вҶ’ g вү— f
  вү—-sym p x = sym (p x)

  вү—-trans : {f g h : Map} вҶ’ f вү— g вҶ’ g вү— h вҶ’ f вү— h
  вү—-trans p q x = trans (p x) (q x)

  -- В§ The four canonical maps.
  constL : Map
  constL _ = в„“ dвӮӮ

  constR : Map
  constR _ = r dвӮӮ

  LR : Map
  LR x with cover dвӮҒ x
  ... | injвӮҒ _ = в„“ dвӮӮ
  ... | injвӮӮ _ = r dвӮӮ

  RL : Map
  RL x with cover dвӮҒ x
  ... | injвӮҒ _ = r dвӮӮ
  ... | injвӮӮ _ = в„“ dвӮӮ

  -- В§ Boundary behavior of LR.
  LR-в„“ : LR (в„“ dвӮҒ) вүЎ в„“ dвӮӮ
  LR-в„“ with cover dвӮҒ (в„“ dвӮҒ)
  ... | injвӮҒ _   = refl
  ... | injвӮӮ в„“вүЎr = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r dвӮҒ) в„“вүЎr)

  LR-r : LR (r dвӮҒ) вүЎ r dвӮӮ
  LR-r with cover dвӮҒ (r dвӮҒ)
  ... | injвӮҒ rвүЎв„“ = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r dвӮҒ) (sym rвүЎв„“))
  ... | injвӮӮ _   = refl

  -- В§ Boundary behavior of RL.
  RL-в„“ : RL (в„“ dвӮҒ) вүЎ r dвӮӮ
  RL-в„“ with cover dвӮҒ (в„“ dвӮҒ)
  ... | injвӮҒ _   = refl
  ... | injвӮӮ в„“вүЎr = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r dвӮҒ) в„“вүЎr)

  RL-r : RL (r dвӮҒ) вүЎ в„“ dвӮӮ
  RL-r with cover dвӮҒ (r dвӮҒ)
  ... | injвӮҒ rвүЎв„“ = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r dвӮҒ) (sym rвүЎв„“))
  ... | injвӮӮ _   = refl
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Interpretation and classification of inter-distinction maps.}
Each \texttt{EndoCase} label interprets as one of the four canonical maps, and every concrete map is classified by its boundary outputs.

\begin{code}
  -- В§ Interpret an EndoCase as a map between distinctions.
  interpret : EndoCase вҶ’ Map
  interpret case-constL = constL
  interpret case-constR = constR
  interpret case-id     = LR
  interpret case-dual   = RL

  -- В§ Classify a map by its boundary behavior.
  classify : Map вҶ’ EndoCase
  classify f with cover dвӮӮ (f (в„“ dвӮҒ)) | cover dвӮӮ (f (r dвӮҒ))
  ... | injвӮҒ _ | injвӮҒ _ = case-constL
  ... | injвӮӮ _ | injвӮӮ _ = case-constR
  ... | injвӮҒ _ | injвӮӮ _ = case-id
  ... | injвӮӮ _ | injвӮҒ _ = case-dual
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: Soundness of inter-distinction classification.}
Classification followed by interpretation recovers the original map at both boundary witnesses.

\begin{code}
  -- В§ Soundness at в„“.
  sound-at-в„“ : (f : Map) вҶ’ interpret (classify f) (в„“ dвӮҒ) вүЎ f (в„“ dвӮҒ)
  sound-at-в„“ f with cover dвӮӮ (f (в„“ dвӮҒ)) | cover dвӮӮ (f (r dвӮҒ))
  ... | injвӮҒ flвүЎв„“ | injвӮҒ _     = sym flвүЎв„“
  ... | injвӮӮ flвүЎr | injвӮӮ _     = sym flвүЎr
  ... | injвӮҒ flвүЎв„“ | injвӮӮ _     = trans LR-в„“ (sym flвүЎв„“)
  ... | injвӮӮ flвүЎr | injвӮҒ _     = trans RL-в„“ (sym flвүЎr)

  -- В§ Soundness at r.
  sound-at-r : (f : Map) вҶ’ interpret (classify f) (r dвӮҒ) вүЎ f (r dвӮҒ)
  sound-at-r f with cover dвӮӮ (f (в„“ dвӮҒ)) | cover dвӮӮ (f (r dвӮҒ))
  ... | injвӮҒ _     | injвӮҒ frвүЎв„“ = sym frвүЎв„“
  ... | injвӮӮ _     | injвӮӮ frвүЎr = sym frвүЎr
  ... | injвӮҒ _     | injвӮӮ frвүЎr = trans LR-r (sym frвүЎr)
  ... | injвӮӮ _     | injвӮҒ frвүЎв„“ = trans RL-r (sym frвүЎв„“)

  -- В§ Classification is sound: the interpreted classify agrees pointwise with f.
  classify-sound : (f : Map) вҶ’ interpret (classify f) вү— f
  classify-sound f x = Distinction-elim dвӮҒ (sound-at-в„“ f) (sound-at-r f) x

  -- В§ Maps are determined by their boundary values.
  map-determined :
    (f g : Map) вҶ’
    f (в„“ dвӮҒ) вүЎ g (в„“ dвӮҒ) вҶ’
    f (r dвӮҒ) вүЎ g (r dвӮҒ) вҶ’
    f вү— g
  map-determined f g eqв„“ eqr x = Distinction-elim dвӮҒ eqв„“ eqr x

  -- В§ Absurdity helpers.
  absurd-в„“r : {A : Set} вҶ’ (в„“ dвӮӮ вүЎ r dвӮӮ) вҶ’ A
  absurd-в„“r e = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r dвӮӮ) e)

  absurd-rв„“ : {A : Set} вҶ’ (r dвӮӮ вүЎ в„“ dвӮӮ) вҶ’ A
  absurd-rв„“ e = вҠҘ-elim ((в„“вү r dвӮӮ) (sym e))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Injectivity and uniqueness.}
Interpretation is injective: the $4 \times 4$ case matrix either produces \texttt{refl} or derives absurdity from $\ell \neq r$.
Together with soundness, this forces classification uniqueness.

\begin{code}
  interpret-injective :
    (c c' : EndoCase) вҶ’
    interpret c вү— interpret c' вҶ’
    c вүЎ c'
  interpret-injective case-constL case-constL _ = refl
  interpret-injective case-constL case-constR p = absurd-в„“r (p (в„“ dвӮҒ))
  interpret-injective case-constL case-id     p =
    absurd-в„“r (trans (p (r dвӮҒ)) LR-r)
  interpret-injective case-constL case-dual   p =
    absurd-в„“r (trans (p (в„“ dвӮҒ)) RL-в„“)

  interpret-injective case-constR case-constL p =
    absurd-rв„“ (p (в„“ dвӮҒ))
  interpret-injective case-constR case-constR _ = refl
  interpret-injective case-constR case-id     p =
    absurd-в„“r (trans (sym LR-в„“) (sym (p (в„“ dвӮҒ))))
  interpret-injective case-constR case-dual   p =
    absurd-в„“r (sym (trans (p (r dвӮҒ)) RL-r))

  interpret-injective case-id     case-constL p =
    absurd-в„“r (sym (trans (sym LR-r) (p (r dвӮҒ))))
  interpret-injective case-id     case-constR p =
    absurd-в„“r (trans (sym LR-в„“) (p (в„“ dвӮҒ)))
  interpret-injective case-id     case-id     _ = refl
  interpret-injective case-id     case-dual   p =
    absurd-в„“r (trans (sym LR-в„“) (trans (p (в„“ dвӮҒ)) RL-в„“))

  interpret-injective case-dual   case-constL p =
    absurd-в„“r (trans (sym (p (в„“ dвӮҒ))) RL-в„“)
  interpret-injective case-dual   case-constR p =
    absurd-в„“r (sym (trans (sym (p (r dвӮҒ))) RL-r))
  interpret-injective case-dual   case-id     p =
    absurd-в„“r (sym (trans (sym RL-в„“) (trans (p (в„“ dвӮҒ)) LR-в„“)))
  interpret-injective case-dual   case-dual   _ = refl

  -- В§ Classify is unique relative to interpretation.
  classify-unique : (f : Map) вҶ’ (c : EndoCase) вҶ’ interpret c вү— f вҶ’ c вүЎ classify f
  classify-unique f c cвү—f =
    interpret-injective c (classify f) (вү—-trans cвү—f (вү—-sym (classify-sound f)))
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.1: A Map Is Determined By Boundary Values}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:boundary-determination}

Any input is forced into exactly one of the two boundary cases; therefore pointwise equality cannot be avoided once equality at $\ell$ and $r$ is fixed.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Top-level classification soundness.
k4map-classification-sound :
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’
  ОЈ EndoCase (О» c вҶ’ KвӮ„Map.interpret dвӮҒ dвӮӮ c вү— f)
k4map-classification-sound dвӮҒ dвӮӮ f =
  KвӮ„Map.classify dвӮҒ dвӮӮ f , KвӮ„Map.classify-sound dвӮҒ dвӮӮ f

-- В§ Top-level classification uniqueness.
k4map-classification-unique :
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : EndoCase) вҶ’
  KвӮ„Map.interpret dвӮҒ dвӮӮ cвӮҒ вү— f вҶ’
  KвӮ„Map.interpret dвӮҒ dвӮӮ cвӮӮ вү— f вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
k4map-classification-unique dвӮҒ dвӮӮ f cвӮҒ cвӮӮ pвӮҒ pвӮӮ =
  KвӮ„Map.interpret-injective dвӮҒ dвӮӮ cвӮҒ cвӮӮ (KвӮ„Map.вү—-trans dвӮҒ dвӮӮ pвӮҒ (KвӮ„Map.вү—-sym dвӮҒ dвӮӮ pвӮӮ))

-- В§ Law 7.1: A map is determined by boundary values.
law7-1-map-determined :
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f g : S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’
  f (в„“ dвӮҒ) вүЎ g (в„“ dвӮҒ) вҶ’
  f (r dвӮҒ) вүЎ g (r dвӮҒ) вҶ’
  f вү— g
law7-1-map-determined dвӮҒ dвӮӮ = KвӮ„Map.map-determined dвӮҒ dвӮӮ
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.2: K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Classification Produces A Witness For Any Map}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:k4-witness-sound}

Every map between two distinctions receives a classification witness: a case label paired with a proof that the labeled canonical map agrees with the given map at every point. The proof strategy is identical to the endomorphism caseвҖ”inspect the two boundary outputs, match the pattern, verify pointwise.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 7.2: KвӮ„ classification produces a witness for any map.
law7-2-k4map-classification-sound :
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’
  ОЈ EndoCase (О» c вҶ’ KвӮ„Map.interpret dвӮҒ dвӮӮ c вү— f)
law7-2-k4map-classification-sound = k4map-classification-sound
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.3: K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Witness For Maps Is Unique}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:k4-witness-unique}

Two distinct case labels cannot interpret to the same map. If they did, their boundary outputs would simultaneously agree, which forces boundary witnesses in the codomain to be equalвҖ”contradicting $\ell \neq r$.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 7.3: KвӮ„ witness for maps is unique.
law7-3-k4map-classification-unique :
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) вҶ’
  (f : S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’
  (cвӮҒ cвӮӮ : EndoCase) вҶ’
  KвӮ„Map.interpret dвӮҒ dвӮӮ cвӮҒ вү— f вҶ’
  KвӮ„Map.interpret dвӮҒ dвӮӮ cвӮӮ вү— f вҶ’
  cвӮҒ вүЎ cвӮӮ
law7-3-k4map-classification-unique = k4map-classification-unique
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 7.4: Map Presentation Is Unique Up To Isomorphism}
\label{law:heteromorphisms:presentation-unique}

Any alternative presentation of the four-case map classificationвҖ”using a different carrier type $X$ with its own interpretation and classification functionsвҖ”is isomorphic to \texttt{EndoCase}. The proof constructs the isomorphism by composing the alternative classifier with the canonical interpreter and vice versa, using soundness and injectivity to verify the round trips.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Map presentation record.
record MapPresentation (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) (X : Set) : Set where
  field
    mp-interpret            : X вҶ’ (S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ)
    mp-classify             : (S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’ X
    mp-classify-sound       : (f : S dвӮҒ вҶ’ S dвӮӮ) вҶ’ mp-interpret (mp-classify f) вү— f
    mp-interpret-injective  : (x y : X) вҶ’ mp-interpret x вү— mp-interpret y вҶ’ x вүЎ y

open MapPresentation public

-- В§ Law 7.4: Map presentation is unique up to isomorphism.
law7-4-map-presentation-unique :
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : Distinction) вҶ’
  {X : Set} вҶ’
  MapPresentation dвӮҒ dвӮӮ X вҶ’
  SetIso X EndoCase
law7-4-map-presentation-unique dвӮҒ dвӮӮ {X} pres =
  let
    module K = KвӮ„Map dвӮҒ dвӮӮ

    to' : X вҶ’ EndoCase
    to' x = K.classify (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x)

    from' : EndoCase вҶ’ X
    from' c = MapPresentation.mp-classify pres (K.interpret c)

    to-from' : (c : EndoCase) вҶ’ to' (from' c) вүЎ c
    to-from' c =
      sym
        (K.classify-unique
          (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres (MapPresentation.mp-classify pres (K.interpret c)))
          c
          (K.вү—-sym (MapPresentation.mp-classify-sound pres (K.interpret c))))

    from-to' : (x : X) вҶ’ from' (to' x) вүЎ x
    from-to' x =
      MapPresentation.mp-interpret-injective pres
        (MapPresentation.mp-classify pres (K.interpret (K.classify (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x))))
        x
        (K.вү—-trans
          (MapPresentation.mp-classify-sound pres (K.interpret (K.classify (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x))))
          (K.classify-sound (MapPresentation.mp-interpret pres x)))
  in
  record
    { to = to'
    ; from = from'
    ; to-from = to-from'
    ; from-to = from-to'
    }
\end{code}

The four-case classification extends from endomorphisms to arbitrary maps between distinct carriers. The same four cases survive, and the same uniqueness holds. The classification is structural, not an artefact of the self-mapping assumption.

\chapter{Indexed Ledger}
\label{ch:indexed-ledger}

The drift sequence produces an infinite chain of states, each at a higher universe level. Tracking position in this chain requires an index. The natural numbers provide the canonical index: a drift state paired with a tick count. The tick increases by exactly one at each step, and this strict increase is the key to proving acyclicity without appealing to properties of universe levels directly.

The indexed ledger is not additional structureвҖ”it is a \textit{presentation} of the drift chain that makes its combinatorial properties (finiteness of chains, monotonicity of tick) directly accessible to the type checker.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Natural numbers.
data в„• : Set where
  zero : в„•
  suc  : в„• вҶ’ в„•

-- В§ Standard ordering on в„•.
data _вүӨ_ : в„• вҶ’ в„• вҶ’ Set where
  zвүӨn : {n : в„•} вҶ’ zero вүӨ n
  sвүӨs : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүӨ n вҶ’ suc m вүӨ suc n

-- В§ вүӨ is reflexive.
вүӨ-refl : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n вүӨ n
вүӨ-refl zero    = zвүӨn
вүӨ-refl (suc n) = sвүӨs (вүӨ-refl n)

-- В§ вүӨ is transitive.
вүӨ-trans : {a b c : в„•} вҶ’ a вүӨ b вҶ’ b вүӨ c вҶ’ a вүӨ c
вүӨ-trans zвүӨn       _          = zвүӨn
вүӨ-trans (sвүӨs p) (sвүӨs q) = sвүӨs (вүӨ-trans p q)

-- В§ Step lemma.
вүӨ-step : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n вүӨ suc n
вүӨ-step zero    = zвүӨn
вүӨ-step (suc n) = sвүӨs (вүӨ-step n)

-- В§ suc n вүӨ n is absurd.
sucвүӨ-impossible : (n : в„•) вҶ’ suc n вүӨ n вҶ’ вҠҘ
sucвүӨ-impossible zero ()
sucвүӨ-impossible (suc n) (sвүӨs p) = sucвүӨ-impossible n p

-- В§ Tick-indexed drift state.
record DriftStateв„• : SetПү where
  constructor вҹӘ_,_вҹ«
  field
    tick : в„•
    base : DriftState

open DriftStateв„• public

-- В§ Step the indexed state forward.
stepStateв„• : DriftStateв„• вҶ’ DriftStateв„•
stepStateв„• s = вҹӘ suc (tick s) , stepState (base s) вҹ«

-- В§ Extract the carrier of an indexed state.
Carrierв„• : (s : DriftStateв„•) вҶ’ Set (в„“ (base s))
Carrierв„• s = Carrier (base s)

-- В§ Embed between consecutive indexed carriers.
state-embedв„• : (s : DriftStateв„•) вҶ’ Carrierв„• s вҶ’ Carrierв„• (stepStateв„• s)
state-embedв„• s = state-embed (base s)

-- В§ Strict reachability on indexed states.
data ReachвҒәв„• : DriftStateв„• вҶ’ DriftStateв„• вҶ’ SetПү where
  oneв„•  : {s : DriftStateв„•} вҶ’ ReachвҒәв„• s (stepStateв„• s)
  moreв„• : {s t : DriftStateв„•} вҶ’ ReachвҒәв„• (stepStateв„• s) t вҶ’ ReachвҒәв„• s t

-- В§ Strict reachability as infix.
infix 20 _вүәв„•_
_вүәв„•_ : DriftStateв„• вҶ’ DriftStateв„• вҶ’ SetПү
s вүәв„• t = ReachвҒәв„• s t
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 8.0: Tick Strictly Increases Along Reach\texorpdfstring{$^+_{\mathbb{N}}$}{+N}}
\label{law:indexed-ledger:tick-increases}

\texttt{ReachвҒәв„•} is generated solely by \texttt{oneв„•} and \texttt{moreв„•}. The step constructor forces \texttt{tick} to increase by \texttt{suc}, and \texttt{moreв„•} preserves this forcing under transitive closure.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 8.0: Tick strictly increases along ReachвҒәв„•.
law8-0-tick-increases : {s t : DriftStateв„•} вҶ’ (s вүәв„• t) вҶ’ suc (tick s) вүӨ tick t
law8-0-tick-increases oneв„• = вүӨ-refl _
law8-0-tick-increases {s} (moreв„• p) =
  вүӨ-trans (вүӨ-step (suc (tick s))) (law8-0-tick-increases p)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 8.1: Indexed Drift Has No Cycles}
\label{law:indexed-ledger:no-cycles}

A cycle in the indexed drift would require $\texttt{suc (tick s)} \le \texttt{tick s}$ for some state $s$. But the successor of a natural number is never less than or equal to itself. This is the structural impossibility that makes indexed drift permanently acyclic.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 8.1: Indexed drift has no cycles.
law8-1-no-cycleв„• : (s : DriftStateв„•) вҶ’ (s вүәв„• s) вҶ’ вҠҘ
law8-1-no-cycleв„• s p = sucвүӨ-impossible (tick s) (law8-0-tick-increases p)
\end{code}

The indexed ledger now carries a monotonically increasing tick, and acyclicity follows from the impossibility of a successor being less than or equal to itself. The ledger is a faithful presentation of the drift chain---no information is added, and no loop is concealed.

\chapter{Presentation of Reachability}
\label{ch:presentation-of-reachability}

Every formal system carries bookkeeping. Indices, counters, witness labels---all the machinery that makes the proof checkable also makes it heavier than the theorem it proves. If the bookkeeping contributed real content, then the presentation would be part of the mathematics. But if it does not---if every observable quantity is already determined by the underlying structure---then the bookkeeping must provably collapse. This chapter tests that collapse: does the tick index on drift states contribute anything that the base drift chain does not already contain?

The indexed ledger adds a tick count to each drift state, but it should not add \textit{meaning}. If two indexed states have the same underlying drift state, any observable property should agree on them. This chapter makes that precise: it establishes a forget/lift adjunction between the indexed and base reachability relations, derives the notion of admissible observable (a function that respects the base projection), and shows that every admissible observable factors uniquely through the forgetful map.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Forget the tick index.
forgetState : DriftStateв„• вҶ’ DriftState
forgetState = base

-- В§ Forget preserves strict reachability.
forgetReachвҒә : {s t : DriftStateв„•} вҶ’ (s вүәв„• t) вҶ’ (forgetState s вүә forgetState t)
forgetReachвҒә oneв„• = one
forgetReachвҒә (moreв„• p) = more (forgetReachвҒә p)

-- В§ Compute the lifted target.
liftTarget : (n : в„•) вҶ’ {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : s вүә t) вҶ’ DriftStateв„•
liftTarget n {s} one = stepStateв„• вҹӘ n , s вҹ«
liftTarget n (more p) = liftTarget (suc n) p

-- В§ Lift a base reach proof into the indexed ledger.
liftReachвҒә : (n : в„•) вҶ’ {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : s вүә t) вҶ’ (вҹӘ n , s вҹ« вүәв„• liftTarget n p)
liftReachвҒә n {s} one = oneв„•
liftReachвҒә n (more p) = moreв„• (liftReachвҒә (suc n) p)

-- В§ Lifted target forgets to the original target.
liftTarget-base : (n : в„•) вҶ’ {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : s вүә t) вҶ’ forgetState (liftTarget n p) вүЎПү t
liftTarget-base n {s} one = reflПү
liftTarget-base n (more p) = liftTarget-base (suc n) p

-- В§ Substitution lemma for more-constructor.
substПү-more :
  {s : DriftState} {t u : DriftState} вҶ’
  (eq : t вүЎПү u) вҶ’
  (p : ReachвҒә (stepState s) t) вҶ’
  substПү (О» x вҶ’ ReachвҒә s x) eq (more p) вүЎПү more (substПү (О» x вҶ’ ReachвҒә (stepState s) x) eq p)
substПү-more reflПү p = reflПү
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 9.0--9.2: Forget/Lift Round-Trip}
\label{laws:observables:forget-lift-roundtrip}

The forget map strips the index, recovering the original level-polymorphic reachability proof.
The lift map attaches an index.
The round-trip law states that forgetting a lifted proof returns the original---the index carries no information that survives projection.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 9.0: Forget preserves strict reachability.
law9-0-forget-preserves : {s t : DriftStateв„•} вҶ’ (s вүәв„• t) вҶ’ (forgetState s вүә forgetState t)
law9-0-forget-preserves = forgetReachвҒә

-- В§ Law 9.1: Every strict reachability proof lifts to the indexed ledger.
law9-1-lift-exists : (n : в„•) вҶ’ {s t : DriftState} вҶ’ (p : s вүә t) вҶ’ (вҹӘ n , s вҹ« вүәв„• liftTarget n p)
law9-1-lift-exists = liftReachвҒә

-- В§ Law 9.2: Forget after lift recovers the original proof.
law9-2-forget-after-lift :
  (n : в„•) вҶ’ {s t : DriftState} вҶ’
  (p : s вүә t) вҶ’
  substПү (О» u вҶ’ ReachвҒә s u) (liftTarget-base n p) (forgetReachвҒә (liftReachвҒә n p)) вүЎПү p
law9-2-forget-after-lift n one = reflПү
law9-2-forget-after-lift n {s} (more p) =
  let
    eq = liftTarget-base (suc n) p
    q  = forgetReachвҒә (liftReachвҒә (suc n) p)
  in
  transПү
    (substПү-more {s = s} eq q)
    (congПү more (law9-2-forget-after-lift (suc n) p))
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 9.3--9.9: Admissible Observables}
\label{laws:observables:admissible}

An observable on tick-indexed states is \emph{admissible} when it respects equality of the base projection. Every admissible observable factors uniquely through \texttt{forgetState}.

\phantomsection\label{law:observables:factor-through-base}
\phantomsection\label{law:observables:factor-unique}

\begin{code}
-- В§ Pointwise equality on Пү-valued maps.
infix 4 _вү—Пү_
_вү—Пү_ : {A B : SetПү} вҶ’ (A вҶ’ B) вҶ’ (A вҶ’ B) вҶ’ SetПү
_вү—Пү_ {A = A} f g = (x : A) вҶ’ f x вүЎПү g x

-- В§ Canonical inclusion.
canonicalState : DriftState вҶ’ DriftStateв„•
canonicalState s = вҹӘ zero , s вҹ«

-- В§ Respecting the base projection.
record RespectsBase {Y : SetПү} (f : DriftStateв„• вҶ’ Y) : SetПү where
  field
    respects : (x y : DriftStateв„•) вҶ’ forgetState x вүЎПү forgetState y вҶ’ f x вүЎПү f y

open RespectsBase public

-- В§ Factorization through forgetState.
