-- В§ Any non-empty cross-invariant coupling is full.
coupling-full-from-witness : (C : Coupling) вҶ’ CrossInv C вҶ’
  ОЈ EndoCase (О» a вҶ’ ОЈ EndoCase (О» b вҶ’ C a b)) вҶ’
  (a b : EndoCase) вҶ’ C a b
coupling-full-from-witness = law14F-0-edge-forces-all

-- В§ EndoCase carrier count = 4.
EndoCase-count : в„•
EndoCase-count = 4

-- В§ Full self-coupling weight = nВІ = 16.
law-self-coupling-square : EndoCase-count * EndoCase-count вүЎ 16
law-self-coupling-square = refl
\end{code}

\section{K\texorpdfstring{$_4$}{4} Product Graphs}
\label{sec:arithmetic-infrastructure:k4-product-graphs}

Two copies of K$_4$ can be coupled by a cross-relation to form an $8$-vertex product
graph. The edge relation is intra-copy (within the same K$_4$) or inter-copy (via the
coupling).

\begin{code}
-- В§ Inequality on Two.
Twoвү  : (i j : Two) вҶ’ Set
Twoвү  i j = i вүЎ j вҶ’ вҠҘ

Lвү R : Twoвү  L R
Lвү R ()

-- В§ Decidable equality on Two.
Two-decEq : (i j : Two) вҶ’ (i вүЎ j) вҠҺ (Twoвү  i j)
Two-decEq L L = injвӮҒ refl
Two-decEq R R = injвӮҒ refl
Two-decEq L R = injвӮӮ Lвү R
Two-decEq R L = injвӮӮ (О» e вҶ’ Lвү R (sym e))

-- В§ Product edge relation.
Edge2 : Coupling вҶ’ (Two Г— EndoCase) вҶ’ (Two Г— EndoCase) вҶ’ Set
Edge2 C (L , a) (L , b) = a вү  b
Edge2 C (R , a) (R , b) = a вү  b
Edge2 C (L , a) (R , b) = C a b
Edge2 C (R , a) (L , b) = C b a

-- В§ Product edge symmetry.
edge2-sym : (C : Coupling) вҶ’ {x y : Two Г— EndoCase} вҶ’ Edge2 C x y вҶ’ Edge2 C y x
edge2-sym C {x = (L , a)} {y = (L , b)} e = О» eq вҶ’ e (sym eq)
edge2-sym C {x = (R , a)} {y = (R , b)} e = О» eq вҶ’ e (sym eq)
edge2-sym C {x = (L , a)} {y = (R , b)} e = e
edge2-sym C {x = (R , a)} {y = (L , b)} e = e

-- В§ Product edge irreflexivity.
edge2-irr : (C : Coupling) вҶ’ (x : Two Г— EndoCase) вҶ’ Edge2 C x x вҶ’ вҠҘ
edge2-irr C (L , a) e = e refl
edge2-irr C (R , a) e = e refl

-- В§ KвӮ„ Г— 2 graph from coupling.
K4Г—2 : Coupling вҶ’ Graph
K4Г—2 C = record
  { V = Two Г— EndoCase
  ; Edge = Edge2 C
  ; edge-sym = О» {a} {b} e вҶ’ edge2-sym C {x = a} {y = b} e
  ; edge-irr = edge2-irr C
  }

-- В§ Empty coupling (no cross-edges).
CrossEmpty : Coupling
CrossEmpty _ _ = вҠҘ

-- В§ Full coupling (all cross-edges).
CrossFull : Coupling
CrossFull _ _ = вҠӨ
\end{code}

The raw arithmetic substrate is assembled. Finite indices, integers, iteration, couplings, and transport across the surviving K$_4$ cases are available in exactly the form the graph requires. The document can stop merely naming the surviving structures and begin imposing stable algebra on them.

Those computations are still too fragile until the algebraic laws are forced. The next stretch locks down addition, multiplication, order, and the finite summation identities needed for the spectral operators that follow.

\chapter{Forced Additive Laws}
\label{ch:forced-additive-laws}

For the Laplacian to mean anything, integer addition must behave as a group: associative, commutative, with a neutral element and inverses. These are not design choices---any failure would leave the spectral sums ambiguous, and the operator identities proven later would collapse into incoherence. Each law is forced by induction on $\mathbb{N}$ and structural recursion on the \texttt{normalizeв„Ө} definition. The result is a complete abelian-group structure on $(\mathbb{Z}, +_{\mathbb{Z}}, 0_{\mathbb{Z}}, \mathrm{neg}_{\mathbb{Z}})$, together with every permutation law that finite summation will need.

\section{Natural Addition Laws}
\label{sec:additive-laws:natural-addition}

Before $\mathbb{Z}$ can carry a group structure, the underlying $\mathbb{N}$ arithmetic must be locked down.
Diagonal normalization ($\texttt{normalizeв„Ө}\;n\;n = 0_{\mathbb{Z}}$),
right identity, successor shifting, associativity, and commutativity of $+_{\mathbb{N}}$ are each forced by structural recursion---they leave no parameter free and no alternative definition standing.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Diagonal normalization collapses to zero.
normalizeDiag0в„Ө : (n : в„•) вҶ’ normalizeв„Ө n n вүЎ 0в„Ө
normalizeDiag0в„Ө zero = refl
normalizeDiag0в„Ө (suc n) = normalizeDiag0в„Ө n

-- В§ Right identity for +в„•.
+в„•-zero-right : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n +в„• zero вүЎ n
+в„•-zero-right zero = refl
+в„•-zero-right (suc n) = cong suc (+в„•-zero-right n)

-- В§ Successor shifts right.
+в„•-suc-right : (n m : в„•) вҶ’ n +в„• suc m вүЎ suc (n +в„• m)
+в„•-suc-right zero m = refl
+в„•-suc-right (suc n) m = cong suc (+в„•-suc-right n m)

-- В§ Associativity of +в„•.
+в„•-assoc : (a b c : в„•) вҶ’ (a +в„• b) +в„• c вүЎ a +в„• (b +в„• c)
+в„•-assoc zero b c = refl
+в„•-assoc (suc a) b c = cong suc (+в„•-assoc a b c)

-- В§ Commutativity of +в„•.
+в„•-comm : (a b : в„•) вҶ’ a +в„• b вүЎ b +в„• a
+в„•-comm zero b = sym (+в„•-zero-right b)
+в„•-comm (suc a) b =
  trans
    refl
    (trans
      (cong suc (+в„•-comm a b))
      (sym (+в„•-suc-right b a)))
\end{code}

\section{Integer Addition Laws}
\label{sec:additive-laws:integer-addition}
Integer addition is defined via pair normalization: $a +_{\mathbb{Z}} b$ adds the positive and negative components pairwise and re-normalizes.
Congruence for \texttt{normalizeв„Ө}, absorption of pair-level addition,
associativity, commutativity, identity, inverse, and cancellation all follow by structural induction on the pair representation.
The outcome is a complete abelian group $(\mathbb{Z}, +_{\mathbb{Z}}, 0_{\mathbb{Z}}, \mathrm{neg}_{\mathbb{Z}})$.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Congruence for normalizeв„Ө.
normalizeв„Ө-cong : {a a' b b' : в„•} вҶ’ a вүЎ a' вҶ’ b вүЎ b' вҶ’ normalizeв„Ө a b вүЎ normalizeв„Ө a' b'
normalizeв„Ө-cong {a} {a'} {b} {b'} pa pb = trans (cong (О» t вҶ’ normalizeв„Ө t b) pa) (cong (normalizeв„Ө a') pb)

-- В§ Normalization absorbs pair-level addition.
normalize-plusRight : (a b c d : в„•) вҶ’
  normalizeв„Ө (pos (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө a b)) +в„• c) (neg (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө a b)) +в„• d)
    вүЎ
  normalizeв„Ө (a +в„• c) (b +в„• d)
normalize-plusRight zero zero c d = refl
normalize-plusRight (suc a) zero c d = refl
normalize-plusRight zero (suc b) c d = refl
normalize-plusRight (suc a) (suc b) c d = normalize-plusRight a b c d

-- В§ Commutativity of +в„Ө.
+в„Ө-comm : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ x +в„Ө y вүЎ y +в„Ө x
+в„Ө-comm x y with toPairв„Ө x | toPairв„Ө y
... | px | py =
  normalizeв„Ө-cong (+в„•-comm (pos px) (pos py)) (+в„•-comm (neg px) (neg py))

-- В§ Associativity of +в„Ө.
+в„Ө-assoc : (x y z : в„Ө) вҶ’ (x +в„Ө y) +в„Ө z вүЎ x +в„Ө (y +в„Ө z)
+в„Ө-assoc x y z with toPairв„Ө x | toPairв„Ө y | toPairв„Ө z
... | px | py | pz =
  let ax = pos px in
  let bx = neg px in
  let ay = pos py in
  let by = neg py in
  let az = pos pz in
  let bz = neg pz in
  let Axy = ax +в„• ay in
  let Bxy = bx +в„• by in
  let Ayz = ay +в„• az in
  let Byz = by +в„• bz in

  let lhsвӮҖ = normalizeв„Ө (pos (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Axy Bxy)) +в„• az)
                       (neg (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Axy Bxy)) +в„• bz) in
  let lhsвӮҒ = normalizeв„Ө (Axy +в„• az) (Bxy +в„• bz) in
  let rhsвӮҖ = normalizeв„Ө (ax +в„• pos (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Ayz Byz)))
                       (bx +в„• neg (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Ayz Byz))) in
  let rhsвӮҒ = normalizeв„Ө (pos (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Ayz Byz)) +в„• ax)
                       (neg (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Ayz Byz)) +в„• bx) in
  let rhsвӮӮ = normalizeв„Ө (Ayz +в„• ax) (Byz +в„• bx) in
  let rhsвӮғ = normalizeв„Ө (ax +в„• Ayz) (bx +в„• Byz) in

  trans
    (trans
      (cong (О» u вҶ’ u) (normalize-plusRight Axy Bxy az bz))
      (normalizeв„Ө-cong (+в„•-assoc ax ay az) (+в„•-assoc bx by bz)))
    (sym
      (trans
        (trans
          (normalizeв„Ө-cong (+в„•-comm ax (pos (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Ayz Byz))))
                           (+в„•-comm bx (neg (toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө Ayz Byz)))))
          (normalize-plusRight Ayz Byz ax bx))
        (normalizeв„Ө-cong (+в„•-comm Ayz ax) (+в„•-comm Byz bx))))

-- В§ Left identity for +в„Ө.
+в„Ө-zero-left : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ 0в„Ө +в„Ө x вүЎ x
+в„Ө-zero-left 0в„Ө = refl
+в„Ө-zero-left (+suc n) = refl
+в„Ө-zero-left (-suc n) = refl

-- В§ Right identity for +в„Ө.
+в„Ө-zero-right : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ x +в„Ө 0в„Ө вүЎ x
+в„Ө-zero-right x = trans (+в„Ө-comm x 0в„Ө) (+в„Ө-zero-left x)

-- В§ Right inverse.
+в„Ө-inv-right : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ x +в„Ө negв„Ө x вүЎ 0в„Ө
+в„Ө-inv-right 0в„Ө = refl
+в„Ө-inv-right (+suc n) =
  trans
    (cong (О» a вҶ’ normalizeв„Ө a (suc n)) (+в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
    (normalizeDiag0в„Ө (suc n))
+в„Ө-inv-right (-suc n) =
  trans
    (cong (normalizeв„Ө (suc n)) (+в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
    (normalizeDiag0в„Ө (suc n))

-- В§ Left inverse.
+в„Ө-inv-left : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө x +в„Ө x вүЎ 0в„Ө
+в„Ө-inv-left x = trans (+в„Ө-comm (negв„Ө x) x) (+в„Ө-inv-right x)

-- В§ Negation of zero.
negв„Ө-zero : negв„Ө 0в„Ө вүЎ 0в„Ө
negв„Ө-zero = refl

-- В§ Left cancellation.
+в„Ө-cancel-left : (a b : в„Ө) вҶ’ a +в„Ө b вүЎ a вҶ’ b вүЎ 0в„Ө
+в„Ө-cancel-left a b eq =
  trans
    (sym (+в„Ө-zero-left b))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„Ө b) (sym (+в„Ө-inv-left a)))
      (trans
        (+в„Ө-assoc (negв„Ө a) a b)
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ negв„Ө a +в„Ө t) eq)
          (+в„Ө-inv-left a))))

-- В§ Negation zero-test.
negв„Ө-zeroвҶ’zero : (z : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө z вүЎ 0в„Ө вҶ’ z вүЎ 0в„Ө
negв„Ө-zeroвҶ’zero 0в„Ө _ = refl
negв„Ө-zeroвҶ’zero (+suc n) ()
negв„Ө-zeroвҶ’zero (-suc n) ()
\end{code}

\section{Summation Permutation Laws}
\label{sec:additive-laws:summation-permutations}

Finite sums over three or four terms appear throughout the Laplacian and spectral proofs.
Each permutation identity---swapping the first two summands, swapping adjacent pairs, or commuting the last two---reduces to associativity and commutativity of $+_{\mathbb{Z}}$.
These lemmas eliminate every ordering ambiguity before it can infect later calculations.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Swap first two summands in a three-term sum.
swapHeadв„Ө : (a b t : в„Ө) вҶ’ a +в„Ө (b +в„Ө t) вүЎ b +в„Ө (a +в„Ө t)
swapHeadв„Ө a b t =
  trans (sym (+в„Ө-assoc a b t))
        (trans (cong (О» s вҶ’ s +в„Ө t) (+в„Ө-comm a b))
               (+в„Ө-assoc b a t))

sum3в„Ө-swap01 : (a b c : в„Ө) вҶ’ sum3в„Ө a b c вүЎ sum3в„Ө b a c
sum3в„Ө-swap01 a b c = swapHeadв„Ө a b c

sum4в„Ө-swap01 : (a b c d : в„Ө) вҶ’ sum4в„Ө a b c d вүЎ sum4в„Ө b a c d
sum4в„Ө-swap01 a b c d = swapHeadв„Ө a b (c +в„Ө d)

sum4в„Ө-swap12 : (a b c d : в„Ө) вҶ’ sum4в„Ө a b c d вүЎ sum4в„Ө a c b d
sum4в„Ө-swap12 a b c d = cong (О» t вҶ’ a +в„Ө t) (sum3в„Ө-swap01 b c d)

sum4в„Ө-swap23 : (a b c d : в„Ө) вҶ’ sum4в„Ө a b c d вүЎ sum4в„Ө a b d c
sum4в„Ө-swap23 a b c d = cong (О» t вҶ’ a +в„Ө (b +в„Ө t)) (+в„Ө-comm c d)
\end{code}

\section{Negation Laws}
\label{sec:additive-laws:negation}

The pair representation of $\mathbb{Z}$ makes negation a simple component swap: $\mathrm{neg}(a,b) = (b,a)$.
From this, involutivity ($\mathrm{neg}(\mathrm{neg}\;z) = z$) is definitional,
and distributivity of negation over sums, three-term sums, four-term sums, and the $4\times$ scaling all follow by induction on the structure.
Normalization commutes with the swap, so every identity lifts cleanly from pairs to signed integers.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Swap pair components.
swapPairв„• : Pairв„• вҶ’ Pairв„•
swapPairв„• p = mkPairв„• (neg p) (pos p)

-- В§ Negation as pair swap.
toPair-negв„Ө : (z : в„Ө) вҶ’ toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө z) вүЎ swapPairв„• (toPairв„Ө z)
toPair-negв„Ө 0в„Ө = refl
toPair-negв„Ө (+suc n) = refl
toPair-negв„Ө (-suc n) = refl

-- В§ Negation is involutive.
negв„Ө-involutive : (z : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө (negв„Ө z) вүЎ z
negв„Ө-involutive 0в„Ө = refl
negв„Ө-involutive (+suc n) = refl
negв„Ө-involutive (-suc n) = refl

-- В§ Positive component of negation.
pos-toPair-negв„Ө : (z : в„Ө) вҶ’ pos (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө z)) вүЎ neg (toPairв„Ө z)
pos-toPair-negв„Ө z = cong pos (toPair-negв„Ө z)

-- В§ Negative component of negation.
neg-toPair-negв„Ө : (z : в„Ө) вҶ’ neg (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө z)) вүЎ pos (toPairв„Ө z)
neg-toPair-negв„Ө z = cong neg (toPair-negв„Ө z)

-- В§ Normalization commutes with negation.
neg-normalizeв„Ө : (a b : в„•) вҶ’ negв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө a b) вүЎ normalizeв„Ө b a
neg-normalizeв„Ө zero zero = refl
neg-normalizeв„Ө (suc a) zero = refl
neg-normalizeв„Ө zero (suc b) = refl
neg-normalizeв„Ө (suc a) (suc b) = neg-normalizeв„Ө a b

-- В§ Negation distributes over componentwise addition.
negAdd-normalizeSwap : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’
  negв„Ө x +в„Ө negв„Ө y вүЎ
  normalizeв„Ө (neg (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• neg (toPairв„Ө y)) (pos (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• pos (toPairв„Ө y))
negAdd-normalizeSwap x y =
  let AвӮҒ = pos (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө x)) +в„• pos (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө y)) in
  let BвӮҒ = neg (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө x)) +в„• neg (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө y)) in
  let AвӮӮ = neg (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• neg (toPairв„Ө y) in
  let BвӮӮ = pos (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• pos (toPairв„Ө y) in
  let eqAвӮҒ =
        trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• pos (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө y))) (pos-toPair-negв„Ө x))
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ neg (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• t) (pos-toPair-negв„Ө y))
      in
  let eqBвӮҒ =
        trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• neg (toPairв„Ө (negв„Ө y))) (neg-toPair-negв„Ө x))
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ pos (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• t) (neg-toPair-negв„Ө y))
      in
  trans (cong (О» a вҶ’ normalizeв„Ө a BвӮҒ) eqAвӮҒ)
        (cong (normalizeв„Ө AвӮӮ) eqBвӮҒ)

-- В§ Negation distributes over +в„Ө.
neg-+в„Ө : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө (x +в„Ө y) вүЎ negв„Ө x +в„Ө negв„Ө y
neg-+в„Ө x y =
  let A = pos (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• pos (toPairв„Ө y) in
  let B = neg (toPairв„Ө x) +в„• neg (toPairв„Ө y) in
  trans (neg-normalizeв„Ө A B) (sym (negAdd-normalizeSwap x y))

-- В§ Negation distributes over sum3в„Ө.
neg-sum3в„Ө : (a b c : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө (sum3в„Ө a b c) вүЎ sum3в„Ө (negв„Ө a) (negв„Ө b) (negв„Ө c)
neg-sum3в„Ө a b c =
  trans (neg-+в„Ө a (b +в„Ө c))
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ negв„Ө a +в„Ө t) (neg-+в„Ө b c))

-- В§ Negation distributes over sum4в„Ө.
neg-sum4в„Ө : (a b c d : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө (sum4в„Ө a b c d) вүЎ sum4в„Ө (negв„Ө a) (negв„Ө b) (negв„Ө c) (negв„Ө d)
neg-sum4в„Ө a b c d =
  trans
    (neg-+в„Ө a (b +в„Ө (c +в„Ө d)))
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ negв„Ө a +в„Ө t)
          (trans
            (neg-+в„Ө b (c +в„Ө d))
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ negв„Ө b +в„Ө t) (neg-+в„Ө c d))))

-- В§ Negation distributes over fourTimesв„Ө.
neg-fourTimesв„Ө : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө (fourTimesв„Ө x) вүЎ fourTimesв„Ө (negв„Ө x)
neg-fourTimesв„Ө x = neg-sum4в„Ө x x x x

-- В§ Negation distributes over sumFin3в„Ө.
neg-sumFin3в„Ө : (f : Fin3 вҶ’ в„Ө) вҶ’ negв„Ө (sumFin3в„Ө f) вүЎ sumFin3в„Ө (О» k вҶ’ negв„Ө (f k))
neg-sumFin3в„Ө f = neg-sum3в„Ө (f f0) (f f1) (f f2)
\end{code}

\chapter{Multiplicative Structure and Integer Order}
\label{ch:multiplicative-structure-integer-order}

Spectral analysis of K$_4$ operators multiplies eigenvalues, compares magnitudes, and cancels common factors---all operations that demand a ring structure on $\mathbb{Z}$ and a total preorder compatible with it. Natural multiplication is forced by recursion on the left argument; integer multiplication lifts it through the pair representation; and the preorder $\le_{\mathbb{Z}}$ inherits reflexivity, transitivity, and antisymmetry from $\le$ on $\mathbb{N}$. Together they make $(\mathbb{Z}, +_{\mathbb{Z}}, *_{\mathbb{Z}}, \le_{\mathbb{Z}})$ a commutative ordered ring---the minimal algebraic structure in which the Laplacian's spectral decomposition can be stated.

\section{Natural Multiplication}
\label{sec:multiplicative-structure:natural-multiplication}

Multiplication on $\mathbb{N}$ is defined by recursion on the left argument: $0 \cdot n = 0$ and $(1+m) \cdot n = n + m \cdot n$.
Right identity, left and right annihilation, and left identity for addition are immediate.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Multiplication on в„• (separate from BUILTIN _*_).
infixl 7 _*в„•_

_*в„•_ : в„• вҶ’ в„• вҶ’ в„•
zero *в„• n = zero
suc m *в„• n = n +в„• (m *в„• n)

-- В§ Right identity for *в„•.
*в„•-one-right : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n *в„• suc zero вүЎ n
*в„•-one-right zero = refl
*в„•-one-right (suc n) = cong suc (*в„•-one-right n)

-- В§ Right annihilation for *в„•.
*в„•-zero-right : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n *в„• zero вүЎ zero
*в„•-zero-right zero = refl
*в„•-zero-right (suc n) = *в„•-zero-right n

-- В§ Left annihilation for *в„•.
*в„•-zero-left : (n : в„•) вҶ’ zero *в„• n вүЎ zero
*в„•-zero-left n = refl

-- В§ Left identity for +в„•.
+в„•-zero-left : (n : в„•) вҶ’ zero +в„• n вүЎ n
+в„•-zero-left n = refl
\end{code}

\section{Integer Multiplication}
\label{sec:multiplicative-structure:integer-multiplication}

Integer multiplication lifts pair-level multiplication through \texttt{normalizeв„Ө}:
$(a^+,a^-) \cdot (b^+,b^-) = (a^+ b^+ + a^- b^-, a^+ b^- + a^- b^+)$.
Left and right annihilation, right identity, and the opaque wrapper \texttt{*в„Ө} that controls definitional reduction are pinned down here.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Pair-level multiplication.
Pairв„•-mul : Pairв„• вҶ’ Pairв„• вҶ’ Pairв„•
Pairв„•-mul p q =
  let a = pos p in
  let b = neg p in
  let c = pos q in
  let d = neg q in
  mkPairв„• ((a *в„• c) +в„• (b *в„• d)) ((a *в„• d) +в„• (b *в„• c))

-- В§ Integer one.
oneв„Ө : в„Ө
oneв„Ө = +suc zero

-- В§ Natural one.
oneNat : в„•
oneNat = suc zero

-- В§ Round-trip: fromPairв„Ө вҲҳ toPairв„Ө = id.
from-toPairв„Ө : (z : в„Ө) вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (toPairв„Ө z) вүЎ z
from-toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө = refl
from-toPairв„Ө (+suc n) = refl
from-toPairв„Ө (-suc n) = refl

-- В§ Integer multiplication (opaque for reduction control).
infixl 7 _*в„Ө_

opaque
  _*в„Ө_ : в„Ө вҶ’ в„Ө вҶ’ в„Ө
  x *в„Ө y = fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y))

opaque
  unfolding _*в„Ө_
  -- В§ Left annihilation for *в„Ө.
  *в„Ө-zero-left : (y : в„Ө) вҶ’ 0в„Ө *в„Ө y вүЎ 0в„Ө
  *в„Ө-zero-left y = refl

  -- В§ Right annihilation for *в„Ө.
  *в„Ө-zero-right : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ x *в„Ө 0в„Ө вүЎ 0в„Ө
  *в„Ө-zero-right 0в„Ө = refl
  *в„Ө-zero-right (+suc n) =
    normalizeв„Ө-cong
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
  *в„Ө-zero-right (-suc n) =
    normalizeв„Ө-cong
      (*в„•-zero-right (suc n))
      (*в„•-zero-right (suc n))

  -- В§ Right identity for *в„Ө.
  *в„Ө-one-right : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ x *в„Ө oneв„Ө вүЎ x
  *в„Ө-one-right 0в„Ө = refl
  *в„Ө-one-right (+suc n) =
    normalizeв„Ө-cong
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• oneNat))
        (*в„•-one-right (suc n)))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
  *в„Ө-one-right (-suc n) =
    normalizeв„Ө-cong
      (*в„•-zero-right (suc n))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-left (suc n *в„• oneNat))
        (*в„•-one-right (suc n)))
\end{code}

\section{Integer Preorder}
\label{sec:multiplicative-structure:integer-preorder}

The preorder $\le_{\mathbb{Z}}$ inherits reflexivity, transitivity, and antisymmetry from $\le$ on $\mathbb{N}$.
Case analysis over the three constructors of $\mathbb{Z}$ (zero, positive successor, negative successor) exhausts every comparison pair.
Strict order implies weak order, and antisymmetry forces equality of the underlying natural predecessors.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Reflexivity of вүӨв„Ө.
вүӨв„Ө-refl : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ x вүӨв„Ө x
вүӨв„Ө-refl 0в„Ө = tt
вүӨв„Ө-refl (+suc n) = вүӨ-refl (suc n)
вүӨв„Ө-refl (-suc n) = вүӨ-refl (suc n)

-- В§ Transitivity of вүӨв„Ө.
вүӨв„Ө-trans : {x y z : в„Ө} вҶ’ x вүӨв„Ө y вҶ’ y вүӨв„Ө z вҶ’ x вүӨв„Ө z
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} {0в„Ө} {0в„Ө} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} {0в„Ө} {+suc n} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} {0в„Ө} { -suc n } _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} {+suc m} {0в„Ө} _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} {+suc m} {+suc n} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} {+suc m} { -suc n } _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} { -suc m } {0в„Ө} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} { -suc m } {+suc n} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans {0в„Ө} { -suc m } { -suc n } () _

вүӨв„Ө-trans {+suc m} {0в„Ө} {z} () _
вүӨв„Ө-trans {+suc m} {+suc n} {0в„Ө} p ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans {+suc m} {+suc n} {+suc k} p q = вүӨ-trans p q
вүӨв„Ө-trans {+suc m} {+suc n} { -suc k } _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans {+suc m} { -suc n } {z} () _

вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } {0в„Ө} {0в„Ө} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } {0в„Ө} {+suc k} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } {0в„Ө} { -suc k } _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } {+suc n} {0в„Ө} _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } {+suc n} {+suc k} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } {+suc n} { -suc k } _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } { -suc n } {0в„Ө} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } { -suc n } {+suc k} _ _ = tt
вүӨв„Ө-trans { -suc m } { -suc n } { -suc k } p q = вүӨ-trans q p

-- В§ Strict order implies weak order.
<в„ӨвҶ’вүӨв„Ө : {x y : в„Ө} вҶ’ x <в„Ө y вҶ’ x вүӨв„Ө y
<в„ӨвҶ’вүӨв„Ө p = fst p

-- В§ Antisymmetry of вүӨв„Ө.
вүӨв„Ө-antisym : {x y : в„Ө} вҶ’ x вүӨв„Ө y вҶ’ y вүӨв„Ө x вҶ’ x вүЎ y
вүӨв„Ө-antisym {0в„Ө} {0в„Ө} _ _ = refl
вүӨв„Ө-antisym {0в„Ө} {+suc n} _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-antisym {0в„Ө} { -suc n } () _
вүӨв„Ө-antisym {+suc m} {0в„Ө} () _
вүӨв„Ө-antisym {+suc m} {+suc n} p q = cong +suc_ (suc-injective (вүӨ-antisym p q))
вүӨв„Ө-antisym {+suc m} { -suc n } () _
вүӨв„Ө-antisym { -suc m } {0в„Ө} _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-antisym { -suc m } {+suc n} _ ()
вүӨв„Ө-antisym { -suc m } { -suc n } p q = cong -suc_ (suc-injective (вүӨ-antisym q p))

-- В§ Totality of вүӨв„Ө.
вүӨв„Ө-total : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ (x вүӨв„Ө y) вҠҺ (y вүӨв„Ө x)
вүӨв„Ө-total 0в„Ө 0в„Ө = injвӮҒ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total 0в„Ө (+suc _) = injвӮҒ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total 0в„Ө (-suc _) = injвӮӮ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total (+suc _) 0в„Ө = injвӮӮ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total (+suc m) (+suc n) with вүӨ-total (suc m) (suc n)
... | injвӮҒ p = injвӮҒ p
... | injвӮӮ p = injвӮӮ p
вүӨв„Ө-total (+suc _) (-suc _) = injвӮӮ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total (-suc _) 0в„Ө = injвӮҒ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total (-suc _) (+suc _) = injвӮҒ tt
вүӨв„Ө-total (-suc m) (-suc n) with вүӨ-total (suc n) (suc m)
... | injвӮҒ p = injвӮҒ p
... | injвӮӮ p = injвӮӮ p
\end{code}

\chapter{K$_4$ Neighborhood and Laplacian}
\label{ch:k4-neighborhood-laplacian}

Every vertex of K$_4$ is adjacent to exactly three others---completeness minus self-adjacency leaves no room for variation. The combinatorial Laplacian takes a function on vertices, computes three times the vertex value minus the sum over its neighbours, and returns the result. No parameter is chosen; the operator is dictated entirely by the graph's edge set. This chapter constructs the neighbour triples exhaustively and defines the Laplacian as degree minus adjacency sum.

\section{Neighbourhood Structure}
\label{sec:k4-laplacian:neighbourhood-structure}

\paragraph{Step 1: Adjacency and symmetric inequality witnesses.}
Adjacency on $K_4$ is edge membership.
The symmetric inequality witnesses are forced by \texttt{sym} applied to the already-established inequalities.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Adjacency on KвӮ„.
Adj : EndoCase вҶ’ EndoCase вҶ’ Set
Adj a b = Edge K4GraphCanonical a b

-- В§ Complete neighbour triple: every KвӮ„ vertex has exactly 3 neighbours.
record NeighborTriple (v : EndoCase) : Set where
  field
    nвӮҒ nвӮӮ nвӮғ : EndoCase
    adjвӮҒ     : Adj v nвӮҒ
    adjвӮӮ     : Adj v nвӮӮ
    adjвӮғ     : Adj v nвӮғ
    nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ     : nвӮҒ вү  nвӮӮ
    nвӮҒвү nвӮғ     : nвӮҒ вү  nвӮғ
    nвӮӮвү nвӮғ     : nвӮӮ вү  nвӮғ
    complete : (u : EndoCase) вҶ’ Adj v u вҶ’ (u вүЎ nвӮҒ) вҠҺ ((u вүЎ nвӮӮ) вҠҺ (u вүЎ nвӮғ))

open NeighborTriple public

-- В§ Symmetric inequality witnesses.
case-constRвү case-constL : case-constR вү  case-constL
case-constRвү case-constL eq = case-constLвү case-constR (sym eq)

case-idвү case-constL : case-id вү  case-constL
case-idвү case-constL eq = case-constLвү case-id (sym eq)

case-dualвү case-constL : case-dual вү  case-constL
case-dualвү case-constL eq = case-constLвү case-dual (sym eq)

case-idвү case-constR : case-id вү  case-constR
case-idвү case-constR eq = case-constRвү case-id (sym eq)

case-dualвү case-constR : case-dual вү  case-constR
case-dualвү case-constR eq = case-constRвү case-dual (sym eq)

case-dualвү case-id : case-dual вү  case-id
case-dualвү case-id eq = case-idвү case-dual (sym eq)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Law 14.0 --- Complete neighbour triple for each vertex.}
Each of the four vertices of $K_4$ has exactly three neighbours. The four record constructions
are forced by completeness minus self-adjacency.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 14.0: Every vertex has a complete neighbour triple.
law14-0-neighbor-triple : (v : EndoCase) вҶ’ NeighborTriple v
law14-0-neighbor-triple case-constL = record
  { nвӮҒ = case-constR
  ; nвӮӮ = case-id
  ; nвӮғ = case-dual
  ; adjвӮҒ = case-constLвү case-constR
  ; adjвӮӮ = case-constLвү case-id
  ; adjвӮғ = case-constLвү case-dual
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ = case-constRвү case-id
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮғ = case-constRвү case-dual
  ; nвӮӮвү nвӮғ = case-idвү case-dual
  ; complete = О»
      { case-constL adj вҶ’ вҠҘ-elim (adj refl)
      ; case-constR adj вҶ’ injвӮҒ refl
      ; case-id     adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮҒ refl)
      ; case-dual   adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ refl)
      }
  }
law14-0-neighbor-triple case-constR = record
  { nвӮҒ = case-constL
  ; nвӮӮ = case-id
  ; nвӮғ = case-dual
  ; adjвӮҒ = case-constRвү case-constL
  ; adjвӮӮ = case-constRвү case-id
  ; adjвӮғ = case-constRвү case-dual
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ = case-constLвү case-id
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮғ = case-constLвү case-dual
  ; nвӮӮвү nвӮғ = case-idвү case-dual
  ; complete = О»
      { case-constL adj вҶ’ injвӮҒ refl
      ; case-constR adj вҶ’ вҠҘ-elim (adj refl)
      ; case-id     adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮҒ refl)
      ; case-dual   adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ refl)
      }
  }
law14-0-neighbor-triple case-id = record
  { nвӮҒ = case-constL
  ; nвӮӮ = case-constR
  ; nвӮғ = case-dual
  ; adjвӮҒ = case-idвү case-constL
  ; adjвӮӮ = case-idвү case-constR
  ; adjвӮғ = case-idвү case-dual
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ = case-constLвү case-constR
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮғ = case-constLвү case-dual
  ; nвӮӮвү nвӮғ = case-constRвү case-dual
  ; complete = О»
      { case-constL adj вҶ’ injвӮҒ refl
      ; case-constR adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮҒ refl)
      ; case-id     adj вҶ’ вҠҘ-elim (adj refl)
      ; case-dual   adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ refl)
      }
  }
law14-0-neighbor-triple case-dual = record
  { nвӮҒ = case-constL
  ; nвӮӮ = case-constR
  ; nвӮғ = case-id
  ; adjвӮҒ = case-dualвү case-constL
  ; adjвӮӮ = case-dualвү case-constR
  ; adjвӮғ = case-dualвү case-id
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ = case-constLвү case-constR
  ; nвӮҒвү nвӮғ = case-constLвү case-id
  ; nвӮӮвү nвӮғ = case-constRвү case-id
  ; complete = О»
      { case-constL adj вҶ’ injвӮҒ refl
      ; case-constR adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮҒ refl)
      ; case-id     adj вҶ’ injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ refl)
      ; case-dual   adj вҶ’ вҠҘ-elim (adj refl)
      }
  }

-- В§ Neighbour lookup by Fin3 index.
neighborAt : (v : EndoCase) вҶ’ Fin3 вҶ’ EndoCase
neighborAt v f0 = nвӮҒ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v)
neighborAt v f1 = nвӮӮ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v)
neighborAt v f2 = nвӮғ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v)

-- В§ Neighbours are adjacent.
neighborAt-adj : (v : EndoCase) вҶ’ (i : Fin3) вҶ’ Adj v (neighborAt v i)
neighborAt-adj v f0 = adjвӮҒ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v)
neighborAt-adj v f1 = adjвӮӮ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v)
neighborAt-adj v f2 = adjвӮғ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v)

-- В§ Neighbour lookup is injective.
neighborAt-injective : (v : EndoCase) вҶ’ {i j : Fin3} вҶ’ neighborAt v i вүЎ neighborAt v j вҶ’ i вүЎ j
neighborAt-injective v {f0} {f0} _ = refl
neighborAt-injective v {f1} {f1} _ = refl
neighborAt-injective v {f2} {f2} _ = refl
neighborAt-injective v {f0} {f1} eq = вҠҘ-elim (nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v) eq)
neighborAt-injective v {f1} {f0} eq = вҠҘ-elim (nвӮҒвү nвӮӮ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v) (sym eq))
neighborAt-injective v {f0} {f2} eq = вҠҘ-elim (nвӮҒвү nвӮғ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v) eq)
neighborAt-injective v {f2} {f0} eq = вҠҘ-elim (nвӮҒвү nвӮғ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v) (sym eq))
neighborAt-injective v {f1} {f2} eq = вҠҘ-elim (nвӮӮвү nвӮғ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v) eq)
neighborAt-injective v {f2} {f1} eq = вҠҘ-elim (nвӮӮвү nвӮғ (law14-0-neighbor-triple v) (sym eq))
\end{code}

\section{Laplacian Operator}
\label{sec:k4-laplacian:laplacian-operator}

The combinatorial Laplacian $L$ takes a function $f$ on K$_4$ vertices, computes three times the vertex value minus the sum over its three neighbours, and returns the difference.
The degree term and adjacency sum are extracted from the neighbour triple, and the Laplacian is their difference.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Adjacency sum: sum of f over the three neighbours.
adjSumв„Ө : (EndoCase вҶ’ в„Ө) вҶ’ EndoCase вҶ’ в„Ө
adjSumв„Ө f v = sumFin3в„Ө (О» i вҶ’ f (neighborAt v i))

-- В§ Degree term: 3 Г— f(v).
deg3в„Ө : (EndoCase вҶ’ в„Ө) вҶ’ EndoCase вҶ’ в„Ө
deg3в„Ө f v = sum3в„Ө (f v) (f v) (f v)

-- В§ Combinatorial Laplacian: deg вҲ’ adj.
laplacianв„Ө : (EndoCase вҶ’ в„Ө) вҶ’ EndoCase вҶ’ в„Ө
laplacianв„Ө f v = deg3в„Ө f v +в„Ө negв„Ө (adjSumв„Ө f v)
\end{code}

\chapter{Simplex Invariants and Natural Multiplication Laws}
\label{ch:simplex-invariants-natural-multiplication}

The K$_4$ graph \emph{is} the $3$-simplex: four vertices, degree $3$, six edges, Euler characteristic $2$. These are not parameters but definitional equalities forced by the graph already constructed. To push further into spectral territory, natural-number arithmetic must satisfy distributivity, associativity, commutativity, and cancellation---the full semiring package. Each of these ten laws is forced by induction on $\mathbb{N}$, building on the additive laws already proven. The result is $(\mathbb{N}, +_{\mathbb{N}}, *_{\mathbb{N}})$ as a commutative semiring with monotonicity and cancellation, ready to serve as the backbone of the integer ring proofs that follow.

\section{Simplex Invariants}
\label{sec:simplex-invariants:simplex-invariants}

No number enters by hand. The carrier of any distinction has exactly two elements ($\ell$ and $r$); this is the content of the word ``distinction'' and the one irreducible datum. Every simplex constant is then forced by algebra and graph theory from that single input.

A distinction offers two boundary witnesses. The total endomorphism count on a two-element carrier is $2^2 = 4$ (each of two outputs chosen independently). This is the vertex count of the complete graph $K_4$---the same four that the four-case classification (Chapter~\ref{ch:elimination-classification}) forced by exhaustive type-theoretic analysis. The remaining simplex constants follow from the vertex count alone: degree $V - 1$, edge count $T(V)$ (the $V$th triangle number), face count of the $3$-simplex (each triple of vertices spans a face, and for four vertices there are exactly four such triples---one per omitted vertex), and Euler characteristic $V - E + F$.

\textbf{Constraint:} Every simplex constant must be derived from the carrier size of distinction. A bare numeral that does not trace back to the two-class structure of $D_0$ would introduce a free parameter and break the elimination chain.

\textbf{Construction:} Set $|S| = 2$ (the carrier size of distinction). Then $V = |S|^2 = 4$ (endomorphism count, matching the four constructors of \texttt{EndoCase} via the \texttt{vertexAt}/\texttt{vertexIndex} bijection). The complete-graph formulas $d = V - 1$, $E = T(V)$, $F = V$ (self-duality of the tetrahedron), and $\chi = V - E + F$ close the chain. Every constant reduces by \texttt{refl}---no arithmetic gap remains.

\textbf{Consequence:} The combinatorial profile $(4, 3, 6, 2)$ is not a parameter set. It is the unique output of the chain $|\{\ell, r\}| = 2 \;\to\; 2^2 = 4 \;\to\; K_4$ invariants. Changing any single entry would require changing the carrier size of distinction itself.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Distinction carrier size: |{в„“, r}| = 2.
-- В§ This is the ONE irreducible inputвҖ”the content of the word "distinction."
states-per-distinction : в„•
states-per-distinction = 2

-- В§ Triangle number: T(n) = n(nвҲ’1)/2 = 0 + 1 + вӢҜ + (nвҲ’1).
-- В§ Counts edges of the complete graph KвӮҷ without needing division.
triangle : в„• вҶ’ в„•
triangle zero    = 0
triangle (suc n) = n + triangle n

-- В§ Simplex vertex count: |End(S)| = |S|ВІ = 2ВІ = 4.
-- В§ (Four constructors of EndoCase, verified by Fin4 bijection.)
simplex-vertices : в„•
simplex-vertices = states-per-distinction * states-per-distinction

-- В§ Simplex degree: KвӮҷ is (nвҲ’1)-regular.
simplex-degree : в„•
simplex-degree = simplex-vertices вҲё 1

-- В§ Simplex edge count: T(V) = T(4) = 0+1+2+3 = 6.
simplex-edges : в„•
simplex-edges = triangle simplex-vertices

-- В§ Simplex face count: the tetrahedron is self-dual, F = V.
-- В§ (Each face is the triangle opposite one vertex; 4 vertices вҶ’ 4 faces.)
simplex-faces : в„•
simplex-faces = simplex-vertices

-- В§ Simplex Euler characteristic: ПҮ = V вҲ’ E + F.
simplex-chi : в„•
simplex-chi = (simplex-vertices + simplex-faces) вҲё simplex-edges

-- В§ Law 14C.0: Vertex count = 4 (derived from 2ВІ = 4).
law14C-0-vertices : simplex-vertices вүЎ 4
law14C-0-vertices = refl

-- В§ Law 14C.1: Degree = 3 (derived from V вҲ’ 1 = 3).
law14C-1-degree : simplex-degree вүЎ 3
law14C-1-degree = refl

-- В§ Law 14C.2: Edge count = 6 (derived from T(4) = 6).
law14C-2-edges : simplex-edges вүЎ 6
law14C-2-edges = refl

-- В§ Law 14C.2a: Face count = 4 (self-duality).
law14C-2a-faces : simplex-faces вүЎ 4
law14C-2a-faces = refl

-- В§ Law 14C.3: Euler characteristic = 2 (derived from V вҲ’ E + F = 2).
law14C-3-chi : simplex-chi вүЎ 2
law14C-3-chi = refl

-- В§ The carrier size is forced by distinction and nothing else.
law14C-4-carrier-is-2 : states-per-distinction вүЎ 2
law14C-4-carrier-is-2 = refl
\end{code}

\section{Distributivity and Associativity}
\label{sec:simplex-invariants:distributivity-associativity}

The semiring laws of $(\mathbb{N}, +_{\mathbb{N}}, *_{\mathbb{N}})$ are chain-forced from the recursive definitions.
Head-swapping, four-way shuffling, right distributivity of $*_{\mathbb{N}}$ over $+_{\mathbb{N}}$, associativity of~$*_{\mathbb{N}}$, and zero cancellation for a positive left factor are each proved by structural induction.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Left distributivity of *в„• over +в„•.
*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• : (a b c : в„•) вҶ’ (a +в„• b) *в„• c вүЎ (a *в„• c) +в„• (b *в„• c)
*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• zero b c =
  trans
    refl
    (sym (+в„•-zero-left (b *в„• c)))
*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• (suc a) b c =
  trans
    refl
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ c +в„• t) (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• a b c))
      (sym (+в„•-assoc c (a *в„• c) (b *в„• c))))

-- В§ Head swap for в„• sums.
swapHeadв„• : (a b t : в„•) вҶ’ a +в„• (b +в„• t) вүЎ b +в„• (a +в„• t)
swapHeadв„• a b t =
  trans
    (sym (+в„•-assoc a b t))
    (trans
      (cong (О» s вҶ’ s +в„• t) (+в„•-comm a b))
      (+в„•-assoc b a t))

-- В§ Four-way shuffle for в„•.
shuffleв„• : (b c x y : в„•) вҶ’ (b +в„• c) +в„• (x +в„• y) вүЎ (b +в„• x) +в„• (c +в„• y)
shuffleв„• b c x y =
  trans
    (+в„•-assoc b c (x +в„• y))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ b +в„• t) (sym (+в„•-assoc c x y)))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ b +в„• (t +в„• y)) (+в„•-comm c x))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ b +в„• t) (+в„•-assoc x c y))
          (sym (+в„•-assoc b x (c +в„• y))))))

-- В§ Right distributivity of *в„• over +в„•.
*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• : (a b c : в„•) вҶ’ a *в„• (b +в„• c) вүЎ (a *в„• b) +в„• (a *в„• c)
*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• zero b c = refl
*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• (suc a) b c =
  trans
    refl
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (b +в„• c) +в„• t) (*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• a b c))
      (trans
        (shuffleв„• b c (a *в„• b) (a *в„• c))
        refl))

-- В§ Associativity of *в„•.
*в„•-assoc : (a b c : в„•) вҶ’ (a *в„• b) *в„• c вүЎ a *в„• (b *в„• c)
*в„•-assoc zero b c = refl
*в„•-assoc (suc a) b c =
  trans
    (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• b (a *в„• b) c)
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (b *в„• c) +в„• t) (*в„•-assoc a b c))
      refl)

-- В§ Zero cancellation for positive left factor.
*в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero : (a n : в„•) вҶ’ suc a *в„• n вүЎ zero вҶ’ n вүЎ zero
*в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero a zero _ = refl
*в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero a (suc n) ()
\end{code}

\section{Commutativity and Monotonicity}
\label{sec:simplex-invariants:commutativity-monotonicity}

Commutativity of $*_{\mathbb{N}}$ is forced by the successor-right law and induction on the left argument.
Monotonicity of $+_{\mathbb{N}}$ and $*_{\mathbb{N}}$ and right cancellation by a positive factor complete the ordered-semiring structure that the integer ring proofs will lift through.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Successor-right law for *в„•.
*в„•-suc-right-+в„• : (n m : в„•) вҶ’ n *в„• (suc m) вүЎ n +в„• (n *в„• m)
*в„•-suc-right-+в„• zero m = refl
*в„•-suc-right-+в„• (suc n) m =
  trans
    refl
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (suc m) +в„• t) (*в„•-suc-right-+в„• n m))
      (cong suc (swapHeadв„• m n (n *в„• m))))

-- В§ Commutativity of *в„•.
*в„•-comm : (m n : в„•) вҶ’ m *в„• n вүЎ n *в„• m
*в„•-comm zero n = sym (*в„•-zero-right n)
*в„•-comm (suc m) n =
  trans
    refl
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ n +в„• t) (*в„•-comm m n))
      (sym (*в„•-suc-right-+в„• n m)))

-- В§ +в„• is monotone on the left.
вүӨ-+в„•-monoЛЎ : {a b : в„•} вҶ’ a вүӨ b вҶ’ (c : в„•) вҶ’ (c +в„• a) вүӨ (c +в„• b)
вүӨ-+в„•-monoЛЎ p zero = p
вүӨ-+в„•-monoЛЎ p (suc c) = sвүӨs (вүӨ-+в„•-monoЛЎ p c)

-- В§ Left cancellation for +в„• ordering.
вүӨ-+в„•-cancelЛЎ : (c a b : в„•) вҶ’ (c +в„• a) вүӨ (c +в„• b) вҶ’ a вүӨ b
вүӨ-+в„•-cancelЛЎ zero a b p = p
вүӨ-+в„•-cancelЛЎ (suc c) a b (sвүӨs p) = вүӨ-+в„•-cancelЛЎ c a b p

-- В§ *в„• is monotone in the left factor.
вүӨ-*в„•-monoКі : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүӨ n вҶ’ (t : в„•) вҶ’ (m *в„• t) вүӨ (n *в„• t)
вүӨ-*в„•-monoКі zвүӨn t = zвүӨn
вүӨ-*в„•-monoКі (sвүӨs p) t = вүӨ-+в„•-monoЛЎ (вүӨ-*в„•-monoКі p t) t

-- В§ Right cancellation by a positive (successor) factor.
вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ (k : в„•) вҶ’ (m *в„• suc k) вүӨ (n *в„• suc k) вҶ’ m вүӨ n
вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc {zero} {zero} k _ = zвүӨn
вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc {suc m'} {zero} k ()
вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc {zero} {suc n} k _ = zвүӨn
вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc {suc m} {suc n} k p =
  let
    step : (suc k +в„• (m *в„• suc k)) вүӨ (suc k +в„• (n *в„• suc k))
    step = p

    tail : (m *в„• suc k) вүӨ (n *в„• suc k)
    tail = вүӨ-+в„•-cancelЛЎ (suc k) (m *в„• suc k) (n *в„• suc k) step

    ih : m вүӨ n
    ih = вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc k tail
  in
  sвүӨs ih
\end{code}

\section{Positive Naturals}
\label{sec:simplex-invariants:positive-naturals}

The type $\mathbb{N}^+$ packages a natural number in successor form, eliminating
the possibility of a zero denominator when rationals are introduced later.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Positive natural: predecessor + implicit successor.
record в„•вҒә : Set where
  constructor mkв„•вҒә
  field
    pred : в„•

PosNat : Set
PosNat = в„•вҒә

open в„•вҒә public

-- В§ Embedding into в„• (always вүҘ 1).
вҒәtoв„• : в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„•
вҒәtoв„• n = suc (pred n)

-- В§ Positive one.
oneвҒә : в„•вҒә
oneвҒә = mkв„•вҒә zero

-- В§ Successor on в„•вҒә.
sucвҒә : в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„•вҒә
sucвҒә n = mkв„•вҒә (suc (pred n))

-- В§ Addition on в„•вҒә.
_+вҒә_ : в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„•вҒә
mkв„•вҒә a +вҒә mkв„•вҒә b = mkв„•вҒә (a +в„• suc b)

-- В§ Multiplication on в„•вҒә: (1+a)(1+b) = 1 + (aВ·(1+b) + b).
_*вҒә_ : в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„•вҒә
mkв„•вҒә a *вҒә mkв„•вҒә b = mkв„•вҒә ((a *в„• suc b) +в„• b)

-- В§ Embedding в„•вҒә into в„Ө.
вҒәtoв„Ө : в„•вҒә вҶ’ в„Ө
вҒәtoв„Ө (mkв„•вҒә k) = +suc k
\end{code}

\chapter{Integer Multiplication Laws}
\label{ch:integer-multiplication-laws}

The operator-span composition on K$_4$ eigenvectors multiplies integer scalars, distributes them over sums, and cancels common nonzero factors. These operations demand the full ring axioms: distributivity of $*_{\mathbb{Z}}$ over $+_{\mathbb{Z}}$, associativity, commutativity, and torsion-freedom---no nonzero scalar may annihilate another nonzero scalar. Every law is first proven at the \texttt{Pairв„•} level, where multiplication is componentwise and explicit, then lifted through \texttt{normalizeв„Ө} to the signed representation. The result is $(\mathbb{Z}, +_{\mathbb{Z}}, *_{\mathbb{Z}})$ as an integral domain with no free parameters.

\section{Pair-Level Addition and Normalization}
\label{sec:integer-ring:pair-addition-normalization}

The pair representation $(a^+, a^-)$ for integers admits componentwise addition and a normalization step that strips equal components.
The round-trip lemma---\texttt{fromPairв„Ө} absorbs \texttt{normalizePair}---and the factorization \texttt{toPairв„Ө} $\circ$ \texttt{normalizeв„Ө} $=$ \texttt{normalizePair} $\circ$ \texttt{mkPair$_{\mathbb{N}}$} are the bridge between signed integers and their pair-level computation.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Componentwise pair addition.
Pairв„•-add : Pairв„• вҶ’ Pairв„• вҶ’ Pairв„•
Pairв„•-add p q = (mkPairв„• (pos p +в„• pos q) (neg p +в„• neg q))

-- В§ Normalization: cancel equal components.
normalizePair : Pairв„• вҶ’ Pairв„•
normalizePair (mkPairв„• zero zero) = (mkPairв„• zero zero)
normalizePair (mkPairв„• (suc a) zero) = (mkPairв„• (suc a) zero)
normalizePair (mkPairв„• zero (suc b)) = (mkPairв„• zero (suc b))
normalizePair (mkPairв„• (suc a) (suc b)) = normalizePair (mkPairв„• a b)

-- В§ normalizePair is identity on (x , 0).
normalizePair-right0 : (x : в„•) вҶ’ normalizePair (mkPairв„• x zero) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x zero)
normalizePair-right0 zero = refl
normalizePair-right0 (suc a) = refl

-- В§ normalizePair is identity on (0 , y).
normalizePair-left0 : (y : в„•) вҶ’ normalizePair (mkPairв„• zero y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero y)
normalizePair-left0 zero = refl
normalizePair-left0 (suc b) = refl

-- В§ fromPairв„Ө absorbs normalizePair.
fromPair-normalizePair : (p : Pairв„•) вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (normalizePair p) вүЎ fromPairв„Ө p
fromPair-normalizePair (mkPairв„• zero zero) = refl
fromPair-normalizePair (mkPairв„• (suc a) zero) = refl
fromPair-normalizePair (mkPairв„• zero (suc b)) = refl
fromPair-normalizePair (mkPairв„• (suc a) (suc b)) = fromPair-normalizePair (mkPairв„• a b)

-- В§ toPairв„Ө вҲҳ normalizeв„Ө = normalizePair вҲҳ mkPairв„•.
toPair-normalizeв„Ө : (a b : в„•) вҶ’ toPairв„Ө (normalizeв„Ө a b) вүЎ normalizePair (mkPairв„• a b)
toPair-normalizeв„Ө zero zero = refl
toPair-normalizeв„Ө (suc a) zero = refl
toPair-normalizeв„Ө zero (suc b) = refl
toPair-normalizeв„Ө (suc a) (suc b) = toPair-normalizeв„Ө a b

-- В§ toPairв„Ө вҲҳ fromPairв„Ө = normalizePair.
toPair-fromPair : (p : Pairв„•) вҶ’ toPairв„Ө (fromPairв„Ө p) вүЎ normalizePair p
toPair-fromPair (mkPairв„• a b) = toPair-normalizeв„Ө a b
\end{code}

\section{Pair-Level Distributivity}
\label{sec:integer-ring:pair-distributivity}

Right distributivity of pair-level multiplication over pair-level addition is a componentwise identity involving four products and two sums.
It is verified by expanding definitions and applying commutativity and distributivity of $\mathbb{N}$.
This is the key bridge lemma: once it holds at the pair level, the signed-integer distributivity follows by normalization absorption.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Right distributivity at pair level.
Pairв„•-mul-distrib-right-add : (p q r : Pairв„•) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul p (Pairв„•-add q r) вүЎ Pairв„•-add (Pairв„•-mul p q) (Pairв„•-mul p r)
Pairв„•-mul-distrib-right-add p q r =
  let a = pos p in
  let b = neg p in
  let c = pos q in
  let d = neg q in
  let e = pos r in
  let f = neg r in
  pair-ext
    (pos-proof a b c d e f)
    (neg-proof a b c d e f)
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

    pos-proof : (a b c d e f : в„•) вҶ’
      ((a *в„• (c +в„• e)) +в„• (b *в„• (d +в„• f)))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• c) +в„• (b *в„• d)) +в„• ((a *в„• e) +в„• (b *в„• f)))
    pos-proof a b c d e f =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (b *в„• (d +в„• f))) (*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• a c e))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• c +в„• a *в„• e) +в„• t) (*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• b d f))
          (shuffleв„• (a *в„• c) (a *в„• e) (b *в„• d) (b *в„• f)))

    neg-proof : (a b c d e f : в„•) вҶ’
      ((a *в„• (d +в„• f)) +в„• (b *в„• (c +в„• e)))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• d) +в„• (b *в„• c)) +в„• ((a *в„• f) +в„• (b *в„• e)))
    neg-proof a b c d e f =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (b *в„• (c +в„• e))) (*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• a d f))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• d +в„• a *в„• f) +в„• t) (*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• b c e))
          (shuffleв„• (a *в„• d) (a *в„• f) (b *в„• c) (b *в„• e)))

-- В§ Left identity for *в„•.
*в„•-one-left : (n : в„•) вҶ’ oneNat *в„• n вүЎ n
*в„•-one-left n = +в„•-zero-right n

opaque
  unfolding _*в„Ө_
  -- В§ Left identity for *в„Ө.
  *в„Ө-one-left : (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ oneв„Ө *в„Ө x вүЎ x
  *в„Ө-one-left 0в„Ө = refl
  *в„Ө-one-left (+suc n) =
    normalizeв„Ө-cong
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (oneNat *в„• suc n))
        (*в„•-one-left (suc n)))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (oneNat *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right oneNat))
  *в„Ө-one-left (-suc n) =
    normalizeв„Ө-cong
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (oneNat *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right oneNat))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (oneNat *в„• suc n))
        (*в„•-one-left (suc n)))

-- В§ Left distributivity at pair level.
Pairв„•-mul-distrib-left-add : (p q r : Pairв„•) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul (Pairв„•-add p q) r вүЎ Pairв„•-add (Pairв„•-mul p r) (Pairв„•-mul q r)
Pairв„•-mul-distrib-left-add p q r =
  let a = pos p in
  let b = neg p in
  let c = pos q in
  let d = neg q in
  let e = pos r in
  let f = neg r in
  pair-ext
    (pos-proof a b c d e f)
    (neg-proof a b c d e f)
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

    pos-proof : (a b c d e f : в„•) вҶ’
      (((a +в„• c) *в„• e) +в„• ((b +в„• d) *в„• f))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• e) +в„• (b *в„• f)) +в„• ((c *в„• e) +в„• (d *в„• f)))
    pos-proof a b c d e f =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• ((b +в„• d) *в„• f)) (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• a c e))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• e) +в„• (c *в„• e)) +в„• t) (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• b d f))
          (shuffleв„• (a *в„• e) (c *в„• e) (b *в„• f) (d *в„• f)))

    neg-proof : (a b c d e f : в„•) вҶ’
      (((a +в„• c) *в„• f) +в„• ((b +в„• d) *в„• e))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• f) +в„• (b *в„• e)) +в„• ((c *в„• f) +в„• (d *в„• e)))
    neg-proof a b c d e f =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• ((b +в„• d) *в„• e)) (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• a c f))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• f) +в„• (c *в„• f)) +в„• t) (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• b d e))
          (shuffleв„• (a *в„• f) (c *в„• f) (b *в„• e) (d *в„• e)))
\end{code}

\section{Diagonal Cancellation and Normalization Stability}
\label{sec:integer-ring:diagonal-cancellation-normalization-stability}

Adding a diagonal pair $(k,k)$ to any pair does not change its normalized form, because
\texttt{normalizePair} cancels equal components. This is the key lemma connecting
pair-level multiplication with в„Ө-level multiplication through \texttt{normalizeв„Ө}.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Diagonal addition is absorbed by normalization.
normalizePair-addDiag : (p : Pairв„•) вҶ’ (k : в„•) вҶ’
  normalizePair (Pairв„•-add p (mkPairв„• k k)) вүЎ normalizePair p
normalizePair-addDiag (mkPairв„• a b) zero =
  cong normalizePair (pair-ext (+в„•-zero-right a) (+в„•-zero-right b))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
normalizePair-addDiag (mkPairв„• a b) (suc k) =
  trans
    (cong normalizePair (pair-ext (+в„•-suc-right a k) (+в„•-suc-right b k)))
    (trans
      refl
      (normalizePair-addDiag (mkPairв„• a b) k))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

-- В§ Successor-right via +в„• one.
+в„•-one-right : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n +в„• suc zero вүЎ suc n
+в„•-one-right n = trans (+в„•-comm n (suc zero)) refl

-- В§ *в„• successor-right.
*в„•-suc-right : (a n : в„•) вҶ’ a *в„• suc n вүЎ (a *в„• n) +в„• a
*в„•-suc-right a n =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ a *в„• t) (sym (+в„•-one-right n)))
    (trans
      (*в„•-distrib-right-+в„• a n (suc zero))
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• n) +в„• t) (*в„•-one-right a)))

-- В§ *в„• successor-left.
*в„•-suc-left : (n a : в„•) вҶ’ suc n *в„• a вүЎ (n *в„• a) +в„• a
*в„•-suc-left n a =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„• a) (sym (+в„•-one-right n)))
    (trans
      (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• n (suc zero) a)
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (n *в„• a) +в„• t) (*в„•-one-left a)))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Pair-Level Cancellation.}
The successor lemmas isolate how multiplication interacts with single increments.
Cancellation shows that normalizing after multiplying by a successor pair
$(suc\;c,\,suc\;d)$ reduces to normalizing after multiplying by $(c,d)$:
the extra diagonal component is absorbed by normalization.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Right cancellation at pair level: common successor adds a diagonal.
Pairв„•-mul-cancelRight : (p : Pairв„•) вҶ’ (c d : в„•) вҶ’
  normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul p (mkPairв„• (suc c) (suc d))) вүЎ normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul p (mkPairв„• c d))
Pairв„•-mul-cancelRight p c d =
  let a = pos p in
  let b = neg p in
  trans
    (cong normalizePair (pair-ext (pos-step a b c d) (neg-step a b c d)))
    (normalizePair-addDiag (Pairв„•-mul p (mkPairв„• c d)) (a +в„• b))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

    pos-step : (a b c d : в„•) вҶ’
      ((a *в„• suc c) +в„• (b *в„• suc d))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• c) +в„• (b *в„• d)) +в„• (a +в„• b))
    pos-step a b c d =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (b *в„• suc d)) (*в„•-suc-right a c))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• c) +в„• a) +в„• t) (*в„•-suc-right b d))
          (trans
            (shuffleв„• (a *в„• c) a (b *в„• d) b)
            refl))

    neg-step : (a b c d : в„•) вҶ’
      ((a *в„• suc d) +в„• (b *в„• suc c))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• d) +в„• (b *в„• c)) +в„• (a +в„• b))
    neg-step a b c d =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (b *в„• suc c)) (*в„•-suc-right a d))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• d) +в„• a) +в„• t) (*в„•-suc-right b c))
          (trans
            (shuffleв„• (a *в„• d) a (b *в„• c) b)
            refl))

-- В§ Left cancellation at pair level.
Pairв„•-mul-cancelLeft : (q : Pairв„•) вҶ’ (a b : в„•) вҶ’
  normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc a) (suc b)) q) вүЎ normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• a b) q)
Pairв„•-mul-cancelLeft q a b =
  let c = pos q in
  let d = neg q in
  trans
    (cong normalizePair (pair-ext (pos-step a b c d) (neg-step a b c d)))
    (normalizePair-addDiag (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• a b) q) (c +в„• d))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

    pos-step : (a b c d : в„•) вҶ’
      ((suc a *в„• c) +в„• (suc b *в„• d))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• c) +в„• (b *в„• d)) +в„• (c +в„• d))
    pos-step a b c d =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (suc b *в„• d)) (*в„•-suc-left a c))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• c) +в„• c) +в„• t) (*в„•-suc-left b d))
          (trans
            (shuffleв„• (a *в„• c) c (b *в„• d) d)
            refl))

    neg-step : (a b c d : в„•) вҶ’
      ((suc a *в„• d) +в„• (suc b *в„• c))
        вүЎ
      (((a *в„• d) +в„• (b *в„• c)) +в„• (c +в„• d))
    neg-step a b c d =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (suc b *в„• c)) (*в„•-suc-left a d))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• d) +в„• d) +в„• t) (*в„•-suc-left b c))
          (trans
            (shuffleв„• (a *в„• d) d (b *в„• c) c)
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ ((a *в„• d) +в„• (b *в„• c)) +в„• t) (+в„•-comm d c))))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: Normalization Stability.}
Left and right cancellation combine to show normalization is stable under
factor normalization: normalizing either factor before pair-multiplication
does not change the normalized product.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Normalization absorbs right factor normalization.
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right : (p q : Pairв„•) вҶ’
  normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul p (normalizePair q)) вүЎ normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul p q)
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right p (mkPairв„• zero zero) = refl
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right p (mkPairв„• (suc a) zero) = refl
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right p (mkPairв„• zero (suc b)) = refl
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right p (mkPairв„• (suc a) (suc b)) =
  trans
    (Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right p (mkPairв„• a b))
    (sym (Pairв„•-mul-cancelRight p a b))

-- В§ Normalization absorbs left factor normalization.
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left : (p q : Pairв„•) вҶ’
  normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul (normalizePair p) q) вүЎ normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul p q)
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left (mkPairв„• zero zero) q = refl
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left (mkPairв„• (suc a) zero) q = refl
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left (mkPairв„• zero (suc b)) q = refl
Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left (mkPairв„• (suc a) (suc b)) q =
  trans
    (Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left (mkPairв„• a b) q)
    (sym (Pairв„•-mul-cancelLeft q a b))
\end{code}

\section{Canonical Pair Products and в„Ө-Level Bridge}
\label{sec:integer-ring:canonical-pair-products-z-bridge}

Products of pairs coming from \texttt{toPairв„Ө} are already in normal form. This bridges
pair-level multiplication to в„Ө-level multiplication through \texttt{normalizeв„Ө}.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Products of canonical pairs are already normalized.
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’
  normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y)) вүЎ Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y)
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal 0в„Ө y = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal (+suc n) 0в„Ө =
  let mulEq : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
      mulEq =
        pair-ext
          (trans (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (zero *в„• zero)) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n))) refl)
          (trans (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (zero *в„• zero)) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n))) refl)
  in
  trans (cong normalizePair mulEq) (trans (normalizePair-right0 zero) (sym mulEq))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal (-suc n) 0в„Ө =
  let mulEq : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
      mulEq =
        pair-ext
          (trans (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (suc n *в„• zero)) refl) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
          (trans (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (suc n *в„• zero)) refl) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
  in
  trans (cong normalizePair mulEq) (trans (normalizePair-right0 zero) (sym mulEq))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal (+suc n) (+suc m) =
  let mulEq : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (+suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• (suc n *в„• suc m) zero)
      mulEq =
        pair-ext
          (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• suc m))
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (zero *в„• suc m)) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
            refl)
  in
  trans (cong normalizePair mulEq)
    (trans (normalizePair-right0 (suc n *в„• suc m)) (sym mulEq))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal (+suc n) (-suc m) =
  let mulEq : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (-suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero (suc n *в„• suc m))
      mulEq =
        pair-ext
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (zero *в„• suc m)) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
            refl)
          (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• suc m))
  in
  trans (cong normalizePair mulEq)
    (trans (normalizePair-left0 (suc n *в„• suc m)) (sym mulEq))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal (-suc n) (+suc m) =
  let mulEq : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (+suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero (suc n *в„• suc m))
      mulEq =
        pair-ext
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ (zero *в„• suc m) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
            (trans
              (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• zero) (*в„•-zero-left (suc m)))
              refl))
          (+в„•-zero-left (suc n *в„• suc m))
  in
  trans (cong normalizePair mulEq)
    (trans (normalizePair-left0 (suc n *в„• suc m)) (sym mulEq))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal (-suc n) (-suc m) =
  let mulEq : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (-suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• (suc n *в„• suc m) zero)
      mulEq =
        pair-ext
          (+в„•-zero-left (suc n *в„• suc m))
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ (zero *в„• suc m) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-right (suc n)))
            (trans
              (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• zero) (*в„•-zero-left (suc m)))
              refl))
  in
  trans (cong normalizePair mulEq)
    (trans (normalizePair-right0 (suc n *в„• suc m)) (sym mulEq))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

opaque
  unfolding _*в„Ө_
  -- В§ Bridge: toPairв„Ө of *в„Ө = Pairв„•-mul of canonical pairs.
  toPair-*в„Ө : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ toPairв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) вүЎ Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y)
  toPair-*в„Ө x y =
    trans
      (toPair-fromPair (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y)))
      (Pairв„•-mul-toPair-normal x y)

-- В§ Bridge: toPairв„Ө of +в„Ө = normalizePair of componentwise add.
toPair-+в„Ө : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ toPairв„Ө (x +в„Ө y) вүЎ normalizePair (Pairв„•-add (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y))
toPair-+в„Ө x y = toPair-fromPair (Pairв„•-add (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y))
\end{code}

\section{Ring Laws: Distributivity, Torsion-Freedom, Negation}
\label{sec:integer-multiplication:ring-laws}

Distributivity of $*_{\mathbb{Z}}$ over $+_{\mathbb{Z}}$ is the foundation of the spectral calculus: eigenvalue sums, Laplacian decompositions, and operator expansions all rely on it.
The proof lifts pair-level distributivity through normalization absorption, then symmetrizes from right to left distributivity.

\begin{code}
opaque
  unfolding _*в„Ө_
  -- В§ Right distributivity of *в„Ө over +в„Ө.
  *в„Ө-distrib-right-+в„Ө : (x y z : в„Ө) вҶ’ x *в„Ө (y +в„Ө z) вүЎ (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө z)
  *в„Ө-distrib-right-+в„Ө x y z =
    let px = toPairв„Ө x in
    let py = toPairв„Ө y in
    let pz = toPairв„Ө z in
    let q  = Pairв„•-add py pz in
    let rhs : fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-add (Pairв„•-mul px py) (Pairв„•-mul px pz)) вүЎ (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө z)
        rhs =
          trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-add t (Pairв„•-mul px pz))) (sym (toPair-*в„Ө x y)))
            (trans
              (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-add (toPairв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)) t)) (sym (toPair-*в„Ө x z)))
              refl)
    in
    trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul px t)) (toPair-+в„Ө y z))
      (trans
        (trans
          (sym (fromPair-normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul px (normalizePair q))))
          (cong fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul-normalize-right px q)))
        (trans
          (trans
            (fromPair-normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul px q))
            (cong fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul-distrib-right-add px py pz)))
          rhs))

  -- В§ Left distributivity of *в„Ө over +в„Ө.
  *в„Ө-distrib-left-+в„Ө : (x y z : в„Ө) вҶ’ (x +в„Ө y) *в„Ө z вүЎ (x *в„Ө z) +в„Ө (y *в„Ө z)
  *в„Ө-distrib-left-+в„Ө x y z =
    let px = toPairв„Ө x in
    let py = toPairв„Ө y in
    let pz = toPairв„Ө z in
    let q  = Pairв„•-add px py in
    let rhs : fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-add (Pairв„•-mul px pz) (Pairв„•-mul py pz)) вүЎ (x *в„Ө z) +в„Ө (y *в„Ө z)
        rhs =
          trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-add t (Pairв„•-mul py pz))) (sym (toPair-*в„Ө x z)))
            (trans
              (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-add (toPairв„Ө (x *в„Ө z)) t)) (sym (toPair-*в„Ө y z)))
              refl)
    in
    trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul t pz)) (toPair-+в„Ө x y))
      (trans
        (trans
          (sym (fromPair-normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul (normalizePair q) pz)))
          (cong fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul-normalize-left q pz)))
        (trans
          (trans
            (fromPair-normalizePair (Pairв„•-mul q pz))
            (cong fromPairв„Ө (Pairв„•-mul-distrib-left-add px py pz)))
          rhs))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Pair Extensionality and Commutativity.}
Distributivity at the в„Ө level follows from pair-level distributivity
plus normalization absorption. The remaining structure---commutativity
and canonical pair products---proceeds at the pair level directly.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Pair extensionality.
Pairв„•-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
Pairв„•-ext refl refl = refl

-- В§ Commutativity of Pairв„•-mul.
Pairв„•-mul-comm : (p q : Pairв„•) вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul p q вүЎ Pairв„•-mul q p
Pairв„•-mul-comm p q =
  Pairв„•-ext posEq negEq
  where
    ap = pos p
    bp = neg p
    cq = pos q
    dq = neg q

    posEq : ((ap *в„• cq) +в„• (bp *в„• dq)) вүЎ ((cq *в„• ap) +в„• (dq *в„• bp))
    posEq =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (bp *в„• dq)) (*в„•-comm ap cq))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ (cq *в„• ap) +в„• t) (*в„•-comm bp dq))
          refl)

    negEq : ((ap *в„• dq) +в„• (bp *в„• cq)) вүЎ ((cq *в„• bp) +в„• (dq *в„• ap))
    negEq =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (bp *в„• cq)) (*в„•-comm ap dq))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ (dq *в„• ap) +в„• t) (*в„•-comm bp cq))
          (+в„•-comm (dq *в„• ap) (cq *в„• bp)))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: Canonical Pair Products.}
Multiplication of canonical pairs---those coming from \texttt{toPairв„Ө}---has
four sign cases. Each reduces to a simple formula with zero in one component,
confirming that canonical pairs form a multiplicative subsystem.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Canonical pair products: (+,0) Г— (+,0).
Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos : (a b : в„•) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• a zero) (mkPairв„• b zero) вүЎ (mkPairв„• (a *в„• b) zero)
Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos a b =
  pair-ext
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• b) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-left zero))
      (+в„•-zero-right (a *в„• b)))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• zero) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-left b))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (a *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right a)))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

-- В§ Canonical pair products: (+,0) Г— (0,+).
Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg : (a b : в„•) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• a zero) (mkPairв„• zero b) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero (a *в„• b))
Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg a b =
  pair-ext
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• zero) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-left b))
      (trans
        (+в„•-zero-right (a *в„• zero))
        (*в„•-zero-right a)))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• b) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-left zero))
      (+в„•-zero-right (a *в„• b)))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

-- В§ Canonical pair products: (0,+) Г— (+,0).
Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos : (a b : в„•) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero a) (mkPairв„• b zero) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero (a *в„• b))
Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos a b =
  pair-ext
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (a *в„• zero)) (*в„•-zero-left b))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ zero +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-right a))
        refl))
    (+в„•-zero-left (a *в„• b))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

-- В§ Canonical pair products: (0,+) Г— (0,+).
Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg : (a b : в„•) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero a) (mkPairв„• zero b) вүЎ (mkPairв„• (a *в„• b) zero)
Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg a b =
  pair-ext
    (+в„•-zero-left (a *в„• b))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ (zero *в„• b) +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-right a))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• zero) (*в„•-zero-left b))
        (+в„•-zero-left zero)))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 14P.0: Positive Nonzero Scalar Has No Torsion (Left)}
\label{law:integer-multiplication:positive-no-torsion-left}

If a positive integer $1+n$ multiplied by $x$ yields zero, then $x$ must be zero.
The proof extracts the vanishing product at the pair level, applies $\mathbb{N}$ cancellation, and derives the contradiction if $x$ were nonzero.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 14P.0: positive left factor is torsion-free.
*в„Ө-pos-left-zeroвҶ’zero : (n : в„•) вҶ’ (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ (+suc n *в„Ө x вүЎ 0в„Ө) вҶ’ x вүЎ 0в„Ө
*в„Ө-pos-left-zeroвҶ’zero n 0в„Ө _ = refl
*в„Ө-pos-left-zeroвҶ’zero n (+suc m) mul0 =
  let
    eqPair : toPairв„Ө ((+suc n) *в„Ө (+suc m)) вүЎ toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө
    eqPair = cong toPairв„Ө mul0

    stepвӮҒ : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (+suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
    stepвӮҒ = trans (sym (toPair-*в„Ө (+suc n) (+suc m))) eqPair

    pos0-raw : pos (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (+suc m))) вүЎ zero
    pos0-raw = cong pos stepвӮҒ

    pos0 : (suc n *в„• suc m) вүЎ zero
    pos0 =
      trans
        (sym (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• suc m)))
        pos0-raw

    bad : suc m вүЎ zero
    bad = *в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero n (suc m) pos0
  in
  вҠҘ-elim (caseSucZero bad)
  where
    caseSucZero : {k : в„•} вҶ’ suc k вүЎ zero вҶ’ вҠҘ
    caseSucZero ()

*в„Ө-pos-left-zeroвҶ’zero n (-suc m) mul0 =
  let
    eqPair : toPairв„Ө ((+suc n) *в„Ө (-suc m)) вүЎ toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө
    eqPair = cong toPairв„Ө mul0

    stepвӮҒ : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (-suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
    stepвӮҒ = trans (sym (toPair-*в„Ө (+suc n) (-suc m))) eqPair

    neg0-raw : neg (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (+suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (-suc m))) вүЎ zero
    neg0-raw = cong neg stepвӮҒ

    neg0 : (suc n *в„• suc m) вүЎ zero
    neg0 =
      trans
        (sym (+в„•-zero-right (suc n *в„• suc m)))
        neg0-raw

    bad : suc m вүЎ zero
    bad = *в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero n (suc m) neg0
  in
  вҠҘ-elim (caseSucZero bad)
  where
    caseSucZero : {k : в„•} вҶ’ suc k вүЎ zero вҶ’ вҠҘ
    caseSucZero ()
\end{code}

\subsection{Laws 14P.1--14P.2: Multiplication Commutes With Negation}
\label{laws:integer-multiplication:commutes-with-negation}

Multiplication must commute with negation on both sides: $x \cdot (-y) = -(x \cdot y)$ and $(-x) \cdot y = -(x \cdot y)$.
Each proof uses the sum $x \cdot y + x \cdot (-y) = x \cdot 0 = 0$ to identify $x \cdot (-y)$ as the additive inverse of $x \cdot y$.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 14P.1: *в„Ө commutes with negв„Ө on the right.
*в„Ө-neg-right : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ x *в„Ө (negв„Ө y) вүЎ negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)
*в„Ө-neg-right x y =
  let
    eq0 : y +в„Ө negв„Ө y вүЎ 0в„Ө
    eq0 = +в„Ө-inv-right y

    mul0 : x *в„Ө (y +в„Ө negв„Ө y) вүЎ x *в„Ө 0в„Ө
    mul0 = cong (О» t вҶ’ x *в„Ө t) eq0

    expand : x *в„Ө (y +в„Ө negв„Ө y) вүЎ (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y)
    expand = *в„Ө-distrib-right-+в„Ө x y (negв„Ө y)

    eqSum : (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y) вүЎ 0в„Ө
    eqSum = trans (sym expand) (trans mul0 (*в„Ө-zero-right x))

    addNeg : negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө ((x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y)) вүЎ negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө 0в„Ө
    addNeg = cong (О» t вҶ’ negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө t) eqSum

    leftReduce : negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө ((x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y)) вүЎ x *в„Ө negв„Ө y
    leftReduce =
      trans
        (sym (+в„Ө-assoc (negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)) (x *в„Ө y) (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y)))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„Ө (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y)) (+в„Ө-inv-left (x *в„Ө y)))
          (+в„Ө-zero-left (x *в„Ө negв„Ө y)))

    rightReduce : negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө 0в„Ө вүЎ negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)
    rightReduce = +в„Ө-zero-right (negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y))
  in
  trans
    (sym leftReduce)
    (trans addNeg rightReduce)

-- В§ Law 14P.2: *в„Ө commutes with negв„Ө on the left.
*в„Ө-neg-left : (x y : в„Ө) вҶ’ (negв„Ө x) *в„Ө y вүЎ negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)
*в„Ө-neg-left x y =
  let
    eq0 : negв„Ө x +в„Ө x вүЎ 0в„Ө
    eq0 = +в„Ө-inv-left x

    mul0 : (negв„Ө x +в„Ө x) *в„Ө y вүЎ 0в„Ө *в„Ө y
    mul0 = cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„Ө y) eq0

    expand : (negв„Ө x +в„Ө x) *в„Ө y вүЎ (negв„Ө x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө y)
    expand = *в„Ө-distrib-left-+в„Ө (negв„Ө x) x y

    eqSum' : (negв„Ө x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө y) вүЎ 0в„Ө
    eqSum' = trans (sym expand) (trans mul0 (*в„Ө-zero-left y))

    addInv : ((negв„Ө x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө y)) +в„Ө negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) вүЎ 0в„Ө +в„Ө negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)
    addInv = cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„Ө negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)) eqSum'

    lhsReduce : ((negв„Ө x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө (x *в„Ө y)) +в„Ө negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) вүЎ negв„Ө x *в„Ө y
    lhsReduce =
      trans
        (+в„Ө-assoc (negв„Ө x *в„Ө y) (x *в„Ө y) (negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ (negв„Ө x *в„Ө y) +в„Ө t) (+в„Ө-inv-right (x *в„Ө y)))
          (+в„Ө-zero-right (negв„Ө x *в„Ө y)))

    rhsReduce : 0в„Ө +в„Ө negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y) вүЎ negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y)
    rhsReduce = +в„Ө-zero-left (negв„Ө (x *в„Ө y))
  in
  trans
    (sym lhsReduce)
    (trans addInv rhsReduce)
\end{code}

\subsection{Law 14P.3: Negative Nonzero Scalar Has No Torsion (Left)}
\label{law:integer-multiplication:negative-no-torsion-left}

The mirror case of \hyperref[law:integer-multiplication:positive-no-torsion-left]{Law~14P.0}: if a negative integer $-(1+n)$ multiplied by $x$ yields zero, then $x$ must again be zero.
The argument is identical, but applied to the opposite sign constructor.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Law 14P.3: negative left factor is torsion-free.
*в„Ө-neg-left-zeroвҶ’zero : (n : в„•) вҶ’ (x : в„Ө) вҶ’ (-suc n *в„Ө x вүЎ 0в„Ө) вҶ’ x вүЎ 0в„Ө
*в„Ө-neg-left-zeroвҶ’zero n 0в„Ө _ = refl
*в„Ө-neg-left-zeroвҶ’zero n (+suc m) mul0 =
  let
    eqPair : toPairв„Ө ((-suc n) *в„Ө (+suc m)) вүЎ toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө
    eqPair = cong toPairв„Ө mul0

    stepвӮҒ : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (+suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
    stepвӮҒ = trans (sym (toPair-*в„Ө (-suc n) (+suc m))) eqPair

    neg0-raw : neg (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (+suc m))) вүЎ zero
    neg0-raw = cong neg stepвӮҒ

    neg0 : (suc n *в„• suc m) вүЎ zero
    neg0 =
      trans
        (sym (+в„•-zero-left (suc n *в„• suc m)))
        neg0-raw

    bad : suc m вүЎ zero
    bad = *в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero n (suc m) neg0
  in
  вҠҘ-elim (caseSucZero bad)
  where
    caseSucZero : {k : в„•} вҶ’ suc k вүЎ zero вҶ’ вҠҘ
    caseSucZero ()

*в„Ө-neg-left-zeroвҶ’zero n (-suc m) mul0 =
  let
    eqPair : toPairв„Ө ((-suc n) *в„Ө (-suc m)) вүЎ toPairв„Ө 0в„Ө
    eqPair = cong toPairв„Ө mul0

    stepвӮҒ : Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (-suc m)) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
    stepвӮҒ = trans (sym (toPair-*в„Ө (-suc n) (-suc m))) eqPair

    pos0-raw : pos (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө (-suc n)) (toPairв„Ө (-suc m))) вүЎ zero
    pos0-raw = cong pos stepвӮҒ

    pos0 : (suc n *в„• suc m) вүЎ zero
    pos0 =
      trans
        (sym (+в„•-zero-left (suc n *в„• suc m)))
        pos0-raw

    bad : suc m вүЎ zero
    bad = *в„•-pos-zeroвҶ’zero n (suc m) pos0
  in
  вҠҘ-elim (caseSucZero bad)
  where
    caseSucZero : {k : в„•} вҶ’ suc k вүЎ zero вҶ’ вҠҘ
    caseSucZero ()
\end{code}

\section{Pair-Level Associativity and \texorpdfstring{$\mathbb{Z}$}{Z} Ring Closure}
\label{sec:integer-ring:pair-associativity-z-ring-closure}

Associativity of $*_{\mathbb{Z}}$ cannot be proven by a single induction---the pair representation requires zero-annihilation base cases (seven combinations of a zero factor) and then eight nonzero cases, each expanding both association trees through canonical pair products and appealing to $\mathbb{N}$ associativity.
The lifting from pairs to signed integers uses the \texttt{toPairв„Ө}/\texttt{fromPairв„Ө} round-trip.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Zero annihilation at pair level (right).
Pairв„•-mul-zero-right : (p : Pairв„•) вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul p (mkPairв„• zero zero) вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• a b) =
  pair-ext
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (b *в„• zero)) (*в„•-zero-right a))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ zero +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-right b))
        refl))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (b *в„• zero)) (*в„•-zero-right a))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ zero +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-right b))
        refl))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl

-- В§ Zero annihilation at pair level (left).
Pairв„•-mul-zero-left : (p : Pairв„•) вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero zero) p вүЎ (mkPairв„• zero zero)
Pairв„•-mul-zero-left (mkPairв„• a b) =
  pair-ext
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (zero *в„• b)) (*в„•-zero-left a))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ zero +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-left b))
        refl))
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• (zero *в„• a)) (*в„•-zero-left b))
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ zero +в„• t) (*в„•-zero-left a))
        refl))
  where
    pair-ext : {x y x' y' : в„•} вҶ’ x вүЎ x' вҶ’ y вүЎ y' вҶ’ (mkPairв„• x y) вүЎ (mkPairв„• x' y')
    pair-ext refl refl = refl
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Associativity Base Cases (Zero Factor).}
When one of the three operands is zero, associativity reduces to
zero-annihilation on both association trees. Seven base cases cover
all positions where zero can appear.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Associativity at pair level for canonical pairs (16-case proof).
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc : (x y z : в„Ө) вҶ’
  Pairв„•-mul (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (toPairв„Ө y)) (toPairв„Ө z)
    вүЎ
  Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө x) (Pairв„•-mul (toPairв„Ө y) (toPairв„Ө z))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc 0в„Ө y z = refl
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) 0в„Ө z =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (toPairв„Ө z)) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-zero-left (toPairв„Ө z))
      (sym
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-left (toPairв„Ө z)))
          (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero)))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (-suc n) 0в„Ө z =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (toPairв„Ө z)) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• zero (suc n))))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-zero-left (toPairв„Ө z))
      (sym
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) t) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-left (toPairв„Ө z)))
          (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• zero (suc n))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) (+suc m) 0в„Ө =
  trans
    (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) (mkPairв„• (suc m) zero)))
    (sym
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• (suc m) zero)))
        (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) (-suc m) 0в„Ө =
  trans
    (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) (mkPairв„• zero (suc m))))
    (sym
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• zero (suc m))))
        (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (-suc n) (+suc m) 0в„Ө =
  trans
    (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) (mkPairв„• (suc m) zero)))
    (sym
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) t) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• (suc m) zero)))
        (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (-suc n) (-suc m) 0в„Ө =
  trans
    (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) (mkPairв„• zero (suc m))))
    (sym
      (trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) t) (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• zero (suc m))))
        (Pairв„•-mul-zero-right (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)))))
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: Associativity Nonzero Cases.}
The remaining eight cases handle three nonzero operands. Each follows the
same pattern: expand both association trees using the appropriate canonical
pair product lemma, then appeal to \texttt{*в„•-assoc} for the natural number
core.

\begin{code}
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) (+suc m) (+suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• (suc k) zero)) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)) refl)
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos (suc m) (suc k)))
            (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos (suc n) ((suc m) *в„• (suc k)))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) (+suc m) (-suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• zero (suc k))) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext refl (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg (suc m) (suc k)))
            (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg (suc n) ((suc m) *в„• (suc k)))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) (-suc m) (+suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• (suc k) zero)) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext refl (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos (suc m) (suc k)))
            (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg (suc n) ((suc m) *в„• (suc k)))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (+suc n) (-suc m) (-suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• zero (suc k))) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)) refl)
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• (suc n) zero) t) (Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg (suc m) (suc k)))
            (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos (suc n) ((suc m) *в„• (suc k)))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (-suc n) (+suc m) (+suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• (suc k) zero)) (Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext refl (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) t) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos (suc m) (suc k)))
            (Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos (suc n) ((suc m) *в„• (suc k)))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (-suc n) (+suc m) (-suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• zero (suc k))) (Pairв„•-mul-neg-pos (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)) refl)
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul (mkPairв„• zero (suc n)) t) (Pairв„•-mul-pos-neg (suc m) (suc k)))
            (Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg (suc n) ((suc m) *в„• (suc k)))))))
Pairв„•-mul-toPair-assoc (-suc n) (-suc m) (+suc k) =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ Pairв„•-mul t (mkPairв„• (suc k) zero)) (Pairв„•-mul-neg-neg (suc n) (suc m)))
    (trans
      (Pairв„•-mul-pos-pos ((suc n) *в„• (suc m)) (suc k))
      (trans
        (Pairв„•-ext (*в„•-assoc (suc n) (suc m) (suc k)) refl)
        (sym
          (trans
