Reduced witnesses are forced as positive numerator/denominator presentations together with coprimality, and uniqueness is forced by the quotient fields themselves. The same mechanism extracts a canonical reduction candidate from the raw fields by dividing numerator and denominator by their computed gcd.

\textbf{Consequence:} Every rational class now admits a signed reduced normal form---proved via the \texttt{canonical-reduction-form} theorem that splits on sign, reduces the magnitude through the gcd engine, and lifts the result through negation compatibility.  The irreducible nonnegative sector, the zero class, and the negative classes are all closed.  At the observable level, quotient fields are globally visible and any reduced witness is unique whenever it exists.

\subsection{Quotient presentation and field uniqueness}

Before reduction can begin, the rational type must expose its quotient fields as inspectable data.  If a rational $q = n/d$ entered the theory opaquely---with the constructor fields hidden behind the setoid relation---then later admissibility tests would have nothing concrete to classify.  The lift into $\mathit{Set}_\omega$ makes rational observables composable with the drift framework, and the presentation record simply reads off the constructor fields that already exist.  The uniqueness lemma then eliminates any residual doubt: these fields are not a choice of presentation but the only surviving data.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Lift rationals into SetПү so admissible observables can target them.
record в„ҡПү : SetПү where
  constructor wrapв„ҡ
  field
    lowerв„ҡ : в„ҡ

open в„ҡПү public

AdmissibleRationalObservable : SetПү
AdmissibleRationalObservable = AdmissibleObservable в„ҡПү

-- В§ Quotient presentation of a rational value.
record RationalPresentation (q : в„ҡ) : Set where
  field
    pres-num : в„Ө
    pres-den : в„•вҒә
    presents-q : q вүЎ pres-num / pres-den

-- В§ The constructor fields are already the canonical quotient data.
canonical-rational-presentation : (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’ RationalPresentation q
canonical-rational-presentation (n / d) = record
  { pres-num = n
  ; pres-den = d
  ; presents-q = refl
  }

-- В§ Quotient fields are unique once a rational value is fixed.
rational-fields-unique :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  (nвӮҒ nвӮӮ : в„Ө) вҶ’
  (dвӮҒ dвӮӮ : в„•вҒә) вҶ’
  q вүЎ (nвӮҒ / dвӮҒ) вҶ’
  q вүЎ (nвӮӮ / dвӮӮ) вҶ’
  (nвӮҒ вүЎ nвӮӮ) Г— (dвӮҒ вүЎ dвӮӮ)
rational-fields-unique (n / d) nвӮҒ nвӮӮ dвӮҒ dвӮӮ refl refl = refl , refl
\end{code}

\subsection{Reduced witnesses and irreducibility}

Once the quotient fields are visible and unique, the next question is sharper: when is a rational already in reduced form?  The answer is forced by coprimality.  A quotient $n/d$ is \emph{irreducible} when $\gcd(|n|, d) = 1$---when the numerator magnitude and the denominator share no factor beyond one.

A \emph{reduced rational witness} makes this concrete.  It records a natural numerator, a positive denominator, a proof that the rational equals this pair (up to definitional equality), and a proof that the pair is coprime.  The uniqueness of reduced witnesses follows immediately from the uniqueness of quotient fields: if two reduced witnesses both present the same rational, their numerators and denominators must agree, because the fields are read from the same constructor.

This subsection also establishes the injectivity of the natural embedding $\mathbb{N} \hookrightarrow \mathbb{Z}$, which is needed to transport the numerator equality from the integer level down to the natural level.

\begin{code}
-- В§ The в„•-embedding into в„Ө is injective.
fromв„•в„Ө-injective : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ fromв„•в„Ө m вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө n вҶ’ m вүЎ n
fromв„•в„Ө-injective {zero} {zero} refl = refl
fromв„•в„Ө-injective {zero} {suc n} ()
fromв„•в„Ө-injective {suc m} {zero} ()
fromв„•в„Ө-injective {suc m} {suc n} refl = refl

-- В§ Irreducibility for rational quotients: numerator magnitude and denominator are coprime.
IrreducibleQuotient : в„ҡ вҶ’ Set
IrreducibleQuotient q = gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вүЎ 1

-- В§ Reduced witness for a rational quotient with nonnegative numerator.
record ReducedRationalWitness (q : в„ҡ) : Set where
  field
    red-num : в„•
    red-den : в„•вҒә
    red-value : q вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө red-num / red-den
    red-coprime : gcd red-num (вҒәtoв„• red-den) вүЎ 1

-- В§ Reduced witnesses are unique whenever they exist.
reduced-rational-witness-unique :
  {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’
  (rвӮҒ rвӮӮ : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’
  ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rвӮҒ вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rвӮӮ
  Г— ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rвӮҒ вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rвӮӮ
reduced-rational-witness-unique {q} rвӮҒ rвӮӮ =
  let
    pair : fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rвӮҒ)
         вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rвӮӮ)
         Г— (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rвӮҒ вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rвӮӮ)
    pair =
      rational-fields-unique q
        (fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rвӮҒ))
        (fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rвӮӮ))
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rвӮҒ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rвӮӮ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value rвӮҒ)
        (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value rвӮӮ)
  in
  fromв„•в„Ө-injective (fst pair) , snd pair
\end{code}

\subsection{Arithmetic lemmas and exact division}

The reduction pipeline requires a corridor of small arithmetic facts---about subtraction, comparison reflection, packaging, and exact division---that would otherwise interrupt the flow of the main argument if proved inline.  None of these is surprising.  Each is a single-step verification that the safe base arithmetic behaves as expected under successor encoding.

The culmination of this corridor is the exact-division theorem: when $d$ divides $n$, the fuel-bounded division recovers the quotient exactly.  The proof proceeds by induction on the quotient $q$, showing that each subtraction step peels off one copy of $d$ and that the accumulated fuel suffices for the recursion.  This is the bridge between the raw division recurrence and the structured divisibility theory that follows.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Magnitude of the в„•-embedding is the original natural number.
magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө : (n : в„•) вҶ’ magв„Ө (fromв„•в„Ө n) вүЎ n
magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө zero = refl
magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө (suc n) = refl

-- В§ Subtracting zero on the right does nothing.
вҲё-zero-right : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n вҲё zero вүЎ n
вҲё-zero-right zero = refl
вҲё-zero-right (suc n) = refl

-- В§ Subtracting one from a successor recovers the predecessor.
вҲё-one-suc : (n : в„•) вҶ’ suc n вҲё 1 вүЎ n
вҲё-one-suc n = вҲё-zero-right n

-- В§ A left summand cancels against subtraction from the whole sum.
+в„•-вҲё-cancel-left : (m n : в„•) вҶ’ (m +в„• n) вҲё m вүЎ n
+в„•-вҲё-cancel-left zero n = вҲё-zero-right n
+в„•-вҲё-cancel-left (suc m) n = +в„•-вҲё-cancel-left m n

-- В§ Every natural is вүӨв„• its own right extension.
вүӨв„•-self-+в„•-right : (m n : в„•) вҶ’ m вүӨв„• (m +в„• n) вүЎ true
вүӨв„•-self-+в„•-right zero n = refl
вүӨв„•-self-+в„•-right (suc m) n = вүӨв„•-self-+в„•-right m n

-- В§ Boolean вүӨв„• reflects propositional вүӨ.
вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүӨв„• n вүЎ true вҶ’ m вүӨ n
вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ {zero} {n} _ = zвүӨn
вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ {suc m} {zero} ()
вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ {suc m} {suc n} eq = sвүӨs (вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ eq)

-- В§ Boolean failure of вүӨв„• forces the reverse propositional order.
вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүӨв„• n вүЎ false вҶ’ n вүӨ m
вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ {zero} {n} ()
вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ {suc m} {zero} _ = zвүӨn
вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ {suc m} {suc n} eq = sвүӨs (вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ eq)

-- В§ Ordered subtraction splits the larger number into base plus remainder.
вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүӨ n вҶ’ m +в„• (n вҲё m) вүЎ n
вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split {zero} {n} zвүӨn = вҲё-zero-right n
вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split {suc m} {suc n} (sвүӨs p) = cong suc (вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split p)

-- В§ Packing the predecessor of a positive natural recovers the same в„•вҒә.
mkв„•вҒә-вҒәtoв„•-minus-one : (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ mkв„•вҒә (вҒәtoв„• d вҲё 1) вүЎ d
mkв„•вҒә-вҒәtoв„•-minus-one (mkв„•вҒә p) = cong mkв„•вҒә (вҲё-zero-right p)

-- В§ Division by one leaves naturals unchanged.
div-oneвҒә : (n : в„•) вҶ’ n div oneвҒә вүЎ n
div-oneвҒә zero = refl
div-oneвҒә (suc n) =
  cong suc
    (trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ div-fuel n t oneвҒә) (вҲё-zero-right n))
      (div-oneвҒә n))

\end{code}

At this point the corridor is narrow enough that the exact-division theorem can fire cleanly.  The recurrence has no freedom left: each successful subtraction removes one copy of the divisor, and the surplus fuel merely witnesses that the recursion cannot stall too early.

\begin{code}

-- В§ Division recovers the quotient on exact multiples, even with extra fuel.
div-fuel-exact-multiple :
  (q slack : в„•) вҶ’ (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’
  div-fuel ((q *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) +в„• slack) (q *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) d вүЎ q
div-fuel-exact-multiple zero zero (mkв„•вҒә p) = refl
div-fuel-exact-multiple zero (suc slack) (mkв„•вҒә p) = refl
div-fuel-exact-multiple (suc q) slack (mkв„•вҒә p) =
  let
    qk : в„•
    qk = q *в„• suc p

    fuelShape : ((suc q *в„• suc p) +в„• slack) вүЎ suc (p +в„• (qk +в„• slack))
    fuelShape = cong suc (+в„•-assoc p qk slack)

    cmpEq : suc p вүӨв„• (suc q *в„• suc p) вүЎ true
    cmpEq = вүӨв„•-self-+в„•-right p qk

    subEq : (suc q *в„• suc p) вҲё suc p вүЎ qk
    subEq = +в„•-вҲё-cancel-left (suc p) qk

    fuelEq : p +в„• (qk +в„• slack) вүЎ qk +в„• (p +в„• slack)
    fuelEq =
      trans
        (sym (+в„•-assoc p qk slack))
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• slack) (+в„•-comm p qk))
          (+в„•-assoc qk p slack))
  in
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ div-fuel t (suc q *в„• suc p) (mkв„•вҒә p)) fuelShape)
    (trans
      (div-fuel-suc (p +в„• (qk +в„• slack)) (suc q *в„• suc p) (mkв„•вҒә p))
      (trans
        (cong (div-fuel-step (p +в„• (qk +в„• slack)) (suc q *в„• suc p) (mkв„•вҒә p)) cmpEq)
        (cong suc
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ div-fuel (p +в„• (qk +в„• slack)) t (mkв„•вҒә p)) subEq)
            (trans
              (cong (О» t вҶ’ div-fuel t qk (mkв„•вҒә p)) fuelEq)
              (div-fuel-exact-multiple q (p +в„• slack) (mkв„•вҒә p)))))))

-- В§ Division by a positive factor recovers the quotient on exact multiples.
div-exact-multiple : (q : в„•) вҶ’ (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ (q *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) div d вүЎ q
div-exact-multiple q d =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ div-fuel t (q *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) d) (sym (+в„•-zero-right (q *в„• вҒәtoв„• d))))
    (div-fuel-exact-multiple q zero d)
\end{code}

\subsection{Positive divisibility theory}

Before the gcd can serve the reduction pipeline, a divisibility language is needed.  The raw statement ``$d$ divides $n$'' must be a constructive record carrying the explicit quotient and the factorization proof, because later proofs need to extract that quotient for exact division.  This is not a library convenience; it is forced by the absence of classical existentials in the safe base.

The \texttt{DividesвҒә} record captures exactly this: a positive divisor~$d$, a quotient, and a proof that $n = q \cdot d$.  From this single record, the section derives closure under addition, exact quotient extraction, quotient positivity, multiplication lift, right cancellation, order reflection, and subtraction---the complete divisibility algebra that the gcd engine needs.

\textbf{Constraint:} The gcd proofs require divisibility witnesses that carry computational content (the quotient), not merely propositional existence.

\textbf{Construction:} Any divisibility witness that lacks computational content---carrying only propositional existence without an explicit quotient---is eliminated by the downstream gcd proofs. The only surviving form is a record \texttt{DividesвҒә d n} with field \texttt{quotientвҒә} and factorization proof.  Closure under $+$, $-$, multiplication, and cancellation follows.

\textbf{Consequence:} Every later divisibility argument reduces to record operations on \texttt{DividesвҒә}.  No ad hoc arithmetic witness is needed downstream.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Positive divisibility packages an explicit quotient witness.
record DividesвҒә (d : в„•вҒә) (n : в„•) : Set where
  field
    quotientвҒә : в„•
    factorвҒә : n вүЎ quotientвҒә *в„• вҒәtoв„• d

open DividesвҒә public

-- В§ Every exact multiple yields a positive divisibility witness.
dividesвҒә-multiple : (q : в„•) вҶ’ (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ DividesвҒә d (q *в„• вҒәtoв„• d)
dividesвҒә-multiple q d = record
  { quotientвҒә = q
  ; factorвҒә = refl
  }

-- В§ The divisor divides itself.
dividesвҒә-self : (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ DividesвҒә d (вҒәtoв„• d)
dividesвҒә-self d = record
  { quotientвҒә = oneNat
  ; factorвҒә = sym (*в„•-one-left (вҒәtoв„• d))
  }

-- В§ Zero is divisible by every positive natural.
dividesвҒә-zero : (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ DividesвҒә d zero
dividesвҒә-zero d = record
  { quotientвҒә = zero
  ; factorвҒә = refl
  }

-- В§ Positive divisibility is closed under addition.
dividesвҒә-+в„• : {d : в„•вҒә} {m n : в„•} вҶ’ DividesвҒә d m вҶ’ DividesвҒә d n вҶ’ DividesвҒә d (m +в„• n)
dividesвҒә-+в„• {d} {m} {n} dm dn = record
  { quotientвҒә = quotientвҒә dm +в„• quotientвҒә dn
  ; factorвҒә =
      trans
        (congвӮӮ _+в„•_ (factorвҒә dm) (factorвҒә dn))
        (sym (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• (quotientвҒә dm) (quotientвҒә dn) (вҒәtoв„• d)))
  }

-- В§ Exact division extracts the stored quotient from a positive divisibility witness.
dividesвҒәвҶ’div : {d : в„•вҒә} {n : в„•} вҶ’ (dvd : DividesвҒә d n) вҶ’ n div d вүЎ quotientвҒә dvd
dividesвҒәвҶ’div {d} {n} dvd =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ t div d) (factorвҒә dvd))
    (div-exact-multiple (quotientвҒә dvd) d)

-- В§ A positive divisible natural has a positive stored quotient.
dividesвҒә-positive-quotient : {d : в„•вҒә} {n : в„•} вҶ’ (dvd : DividesвҒә d (suc n)) вҶ’ ОЈ в„• (О» k вҶ’ quotientвҒә dvd вүЎ suc k)
dividesвҒә-positive-quotient {d} {n} dvd with quotientвҒә dvd | factorвҒә dvd
... | zero | ()
... | suc k | _ = k , refl

-- В§ Built-in addition and manuscript addition coincide on naturals.
+-to-+в„• : (m n : в„•) вҶ’ (m + n) вүЎ (m +в„• n)
+-to-+в„• zero n = refl
+-to-+в„• (suc m) n = cong suc (+-to-+в„• m n)

-- В§ The в„•-view of в„•вҒә multiplication is ordinary natural multiplication.
вҒәtoв„•-*вҒә : (x y : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ вҒәtoв„• (x *вҒә y) вүЎ вҒәtoв„• x *в„• вҒәtoв„• y
вҒәtoв„•-*вҒә (mkв„•вҒә a) (mkв„•вҒә b) = cong suc (+в„•-comm (a *в„• suc b) b)

-- В§ Divisibility lifts through multiplication by a positive factor on the right.
dividesвҒә-mul-right : {e : в„•вҒә} {n : в„•} вҶ’ DividesвҒә e n вҶ’ (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ DividesвҒә (e *вҒә d) (n *в„• вҒәtoв„• d)
dividesвҒә-mul-right {e} {n} dvd d = record
  { quotientвҒә = quotientвҒә dvd
  ; factorвҒә =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) (factorвҒә dvd))
        (trans
          (*в„•-assoc (quotientвҒә dvd) (вҒәtoв„• e) (вҒәtoв„• d))
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ quotientвҒә dvd *в„• t) (sym (вҒәtoв„•-*вҒә e d))))
  }

-- В§ Equality after multiplying by the same positive factor cancels on the right.
*в„•-cancelКі-suc : {a b : в„•} вҶ’ (k : в„•) вҶ’ a *в„• suc k вүЎ b *в„• suc k вҶ’ a вүЎ b
*в„•-cancelКі-suc {a} {b} k eq =
  вүӨ-antisym
    (вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc k (subst (О» t вҶ’ (a *в„• suc k) вүӨ t) eq (вүӨ-refl (a *в„• suc k))))
    (вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc k (subst (О» t вҶ’ (b *в„• suc k) вүӨ t) (sym eq) (вүӨ-refl (b *в„• suc k))))

-- В§ A product of two positive naturals equals one only in the trivial case.
positive-product-one : (a b : в„•) вҶ’ suc a *в„• suc b вүЎ oneNat вҶ’ (a вүЎ zero) Г— (b вүЎ zero)
positive-product-one a zero eq =
  let
    aEq : suc a вүЎ oneNat
    aEq = trans (sym (*в„•-one-right (suc a))) eq
  in
  suc-injective aEq , refl
positive-product-one a (suc b) ()

-- В§ If a product divisor of d also divides d, the extra factor must be one.
dividesвҒә-product-factor-left-one : (e d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ DividesвҒә (e *вҒә d) (вҒәtoв„• d) вҶ’ e вүЎ oneвҒә
dividesвҒә-product-factor-left-one e d dvd =
  let
    qPos : ОЈ в„• (О» k вҶ’ quotientвҒә dvd вүЎ suc k)
    qPos = dividesвҒә-positive-quotient dvd

    k : в„•
    k = fst qPos

    qEq : quotientвҒә dvd вүЎ suc k
    qEq = snd qPos

    factored : вҒәtoв„• d вүЎ ((suc k *в„• вҒәtoв„• e) *в„• вҒәtoв„• d)
    factored =
      trans
        (factorвҒә dvd)
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„• вҒәtoв„• (e *вҒә d)) qEq)
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ suc k *в„• t) (вҒәtoв„•-*вҒә e d))
            (sym (*в„•-assoc (suc k) (вҒәtoв„• e) (вҒәtoв„• d)))))

    prodOne : suc k *в„• вҒәtoв„• e вүЎ oneNat
    prodOne =
      *в„•-cancelКі-suc
        (pred d)
        (trans
          (sym factored)
          (sym (*в„•-one-left (вҒәtoв„• d))))

    ePredZero : pred e вүЎ zero
    ePredZero = snd (positive-product-one k (pred e) prodOne)
  in
  cong mkв„•вҒә ePredZero
\end{code}

The factor constraints above show that divisibility by a product forces each factor to be trivial when the product divides a single copy of one factor. This is the positive-integer analogue of the familiar number-theoretic fact that if $ab \mid b$, then $a = 1$. With this in hand, the machinery compares quotients: if $d$ divides both $m$ and $n$ and $m \le n$, then the stored quotient for $m$ is at most the stored quotient for $n$.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Order on divisible multiples reflects to the stored quotients.
dividesвҒә-quotient-вүӨ :
  {d : в„•вҒә} {m n : в„•} вҶ’
  m вүӨ n вҶ’
  (dm : DividesвҒә d m) вҶ’
  (dn : DividesвҒә d n) вҶ’
  DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm вүӨ DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn
dividesвҒә-quotient-вүӨ {mkв„•вҒә p} {m} {n} mвүӨn dm dn =
  let
    stepвӮҒ : m вүӨ (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn *в„• (suc p))
    stepвӮҒ = subst (О» t вҶ’ m вүӨ t) (DividesвҒә.factorвҒә dn) mвүӨn

    stepвӮӮ : (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm *в„• (suc p)) вүӨ (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn *в„• (suc p))
    stepвӮӮ = subst (О» t вҶ’ t вүӨ (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn *в„• (suc p))) (DividesвҒә.factorвҒә dm) stepвӮҒ
  in
  вүӨ-*в„•-cancelКі-suc p stepвӮӮ

-- В§ Positive divisibility survives subtraction of a smaller divisible multiple.
dividesвҒә-вҲё-вүӨ :
  {d : в„•вҒә} {m n : в„•} вҶ’
  m вүӨ n вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d m вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d n вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d (n вҲё m)
dividesвҒә-вҲё-вүӨ {d} {m} {n} mвүӨn dm dn = record
  { quotientвҒә = DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm
  ; factorвҒә = factor
  }
  where
    qвүӨ : DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm вүӨ DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn
    qвүӨ = dividesвҒә-quotient-вүӨ mвүӨn dm dn

    qSplit : DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm +в„• (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) вүЎ DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn
    qSplit = вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split qвүӨ

    nAsSum : n вүЎ m +в„• ((DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) *в„• вҒәtoв„• d)
    nAsSum =
      trans
        (DividesвҒә.factorвҒә dn)
        (trans
          (sym (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) qSplit))
          (trans
            (*в„•-distrib-left-+в„• (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) (вҒәtoв„• d))
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ t +в„• ((DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) *в„• вҒәtoв„• d)) (sym (DividesвҒә.factorвҒә dm)))))

    factor : n вҲё m вүЎ (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) *в„• вҒәtoв„• d
    factor = subst (О» t вҶ’ t вҲё m вүЎ (DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) *в„• вҒәtoв„• d) (sym nAsSum)
      (+в„•-вҲё-cancel-left m ((DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dn вҲё DividesвҒә.quotientвҒә dm) *в„• вҒәtoв„• d))

-- В§ Adding back a divisible remainder recovers divisibility of the larger number.
dividesвҒә-restore-вҲё :
  {d : в„•вҒә} {m n : в„•} вҶ’
  m вүӨ n вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d m вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d (n вҲё m) вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d n
dividesвҒә-restore-вҲё {d} {m} {n} mвүӨn dm dnm =
  subst
    (DividesвҒә d)
    (вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split mвүӨn)
    (dividesвҒә-+в„• dm dnm)

-- В§ The в„•-view of sucToв„•вҒә is the expected successor.
вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә : (n : в„•) вҶ’ вҒәtoв„• (sucToв„•вҒә n) вүЎ suc n
вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә zero = refl
вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә (suc n) = cong suc (вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә n)

-- В§ The successor-coded positive natural divides its own в„•-view.
dividesвҒә-suc-self : (n : в„•) вҶ’ DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә n) (suc n)
dividesвҒә-suc-self n =
  subst
    (DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә n))
    (вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә n)
    (dividesвҒә-self (sucToв„•вҒә n))

-- В§ Zero/zero is a stable fixed point of gcd-fuel.
gcd-fuel-zero-zero : (f : в„•) вҶ’ gcd-fuel f zero zero вүЎ zero
gcd-fuel-zero-zero zero = refl
gcd-fuel-zero-zero (suc f) = refl

-- В§ Positive fuel with a zero left input returns the right input.
gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc : (f n : в„•) вҶ’ gcd-fuel (suc f) zero (suc n) вүЎ suc n
gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc f n = refl

-- В§ Positive fuel with a zero right input returns the left input.
gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc : (f m : в„•) вҶ’ gcd-fuel (suc f) (suc m) zero вүЎ suc m
gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc f m = refl

-- В§ One Euclidean subtraction step preserves the total positive mass in the true branch.
euclid-sum-true : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүӨ n вҶ’ (suc m) +в„• (n вҲё m) вүЎ suc n
euclid-sum-true p = cong suc (вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split p)

-- В§ One Euclidean subtraction step preserves the total positive mass in the false branch.
euclid-sum-false : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ n вүӨ m вҶ’ (m вҲё n) +в„• (suc n) вүЎ suc m
euclid-sum-false {m} {n} p =
  trans
    (+в„•-comm (m вҲё n) (suc n))
    (cong suc (вүӨвҶ’+в„•-вҲё-split p))
\end{code}

\subsection{GCD engine: common-divisor preservation, output divisibility, and positivity}

The Euclidean gcd is defined, but a bare recurrence carries no force.  Three properties must be forged before the gcd can serve the reduction pipeline:

\emph{Common-divisor preservation.}  Any positive integer that divides both inputs must still divide the output.  This is the ``upward'' direction: known divisors survive the algorithm.  The proof proceeds by mutual structural recursion, tracking divisibility through each subtraction step via the already established \texttt{dividesвҒә-вҲё-вүӨ} lemma.

\emph{Output divisibility.}  Conversely, if the gcd output is positive, it divides both inputs.  This is the ``downward'' direction: the output itself is a common divisor.  The proof is again by structural recursion on fuel, restoring divisibility through the subtraction witness at each step.

\emph{Positivity.}  Whenever at least one input is positive, the gcd output is also positive.  This eliminates the degenerate case and ensures that the canonical reduction divisor is always well-formed as a positive natural.

Together, these three properties force the gcd to behave as the \emph{greatest} common divisor in the usual sense: it divides both inputs, and every other common divisor divides it.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Every natural is bounded by its own right extension.
вүӨ-self+в„• : (m n : в„•) вҶ’ m вүӨ (m +в„• n)
вүӨ-self+в„• zero n = zвүӨn
вүӨ-self+в„• (suc m) n = sвүӨs (вүӨ-self+в„• m n)

mutual
  -- В§ One recursive Euclidean step preserves any common positive divisor.
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step :
    {d : в„•вҒә} вҶ’
    (f m n : в„•) вҶ’
    (b : Bool) вҶ’
    m вүӨв„• n вүЎ b вҶ’
    DividesвҒә d (suc m) вҶ’
    DividesвҒә d (suc n) вҶ’
    DividesвҒә d (gcd-fuel-step f (suc m) (suc n) b)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n true eq dm dn =
    gcd-fuel-common-divisor f (suc m) (n вҲё m) dm
      (dividesвҒә-вҲё-вүӨ (sвүӨs (вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ eq)) dm dn)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n false eq dm dn =
    gcd-fuel-common-divisor f (m вҲё n) (suc n)
      (dividesвҒә-вҲё-вүӨ (sвүӨs (вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ eq)) dn dm)
      dn

  -- В§ Any common positive divisor survives every Euclidean gcd-fuel step.
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor :
    {d : в„•вҒә} вҶ’
    (f m n : в„•) вҶ’
    DividesвҒә d m вҶ’
    DividesвҒә d n вҶ’
    DividesвҒә d (gcd-fuel f m n)
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor zero m n dm dn = dm
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor (suc f) zero zero dm dn = dividesвҒә-zero _
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor (suc f) zero (suc n) dm dn = dn
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor (suc f) (suc m) zero dm dn = dm
  gcd-fuel-common-divisor {d} (suc f) (suc m) (suc n) dm dn =
    subst
      (DividesвҒә d)
      (sym (gcd-fuel-suc-suc f m n))
      (gcd-fuel-common-divisor-step f m n (m вүӨв„• n) refl dm dn)

    -- В§ Phase 2: Positive gcd outputs divide both original inputs

-- В§ Under sufficient fuel, every positive gcd-fuel output divides both inputs.
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ :
  (fuel m n g : в„•) вҶ’
  ((m +в„• n) вүӨ fuel) вҶ’
  (gcd-fuel fuel m n вүЎ suc g) вҶ’
  ((DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) m) Г— (DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) n))
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ fuel zero zero g enough eq with trans (sym (gcd-fuel-zero-zero fuel)) eq
... | ()
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ zero zero (suc n) g () eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ (suc fuel) zero (suc n) g enough eq =
  dividesвҒә-zero (sucToв„•вҒә g) ,
  subst
    (DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g))
    (sym eq')
    (dividesвҒә-suc-self g)
  where
    eq' : suc n вүЎ suc g
    eq' = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc fuel n)) eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ zero (suc m) zero g () eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ (suc fuel) (suc m) zero g enough eq =
  subst
    (DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g))
    (sym eq')
    (dividesвҒә-suc-self g)
  , dividesвҒә-zero (sucToв„•вҒә g)
  where
    eq' : suc m вүЎ suc g
    eq' = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc fuel m)) eq
gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ (suc fuel) (suc m) (suc n) g (sвүӨs enough') eq with inspect (m вүӨв„• n)
... | reveal true bEq =
  let
    p : m вүӨ n
    p = вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ bEq

    sucnвүӨsum : suc n вүӨ (m +в„• suc n)
    sucnвүӨsum = subst (О» t вҶ’ suc n вүӨ t) (+в„•-comm (suc n) m) (вүӨ-self+в„• (suc n) m)

    subEnough : ((suc m) +в„• (n вҲё m)) вүӨ fuel
    subEnough = subst (О» t вҶ’ t вүӨ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-true p)) (вүӨ-trans sucnвүӨsum enough')

    stepEqвӮҖ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m вүӨв„• n) вүЎ suc g
    stepEqвӮҖ = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n)) eq

    stepEq : gcd-fuel fuel (suc m) (n вҲё m) вүЎ suc g
    stepEq = trans (sym (cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq)) stepEqвӮҖ

    rec : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (suc m) Г— DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (n вҲё m)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ fuel (suc m) (n вҲё m) g subEnough stepEq

    dm : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (suc m)
    dm = fst rec

    dn-rest : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (n вҲё m)
    dn-rest = snd rec
  in
  dm , dividesвҒә-restore-вҲё (sвүӨs p) dm dn-rest
... | reveal false bEq =
  let
    p : n вүӨ m
    p = вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ bEq

    sucmвүӨsum : suc m вүӨ (m +в„• suc n)
    sucmвүӨsum = subst (О» t вҶ’ suc m вүӨ t) (sym (+в„•-suc-right m n)) (вүӨ-self+в„• (suc m) n)

    subEnough : ((m вҲё n) +в„• (suc n)) вүӨ fuel
    subEnough = subst (О» t вҶ’ t вүӨ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-false p)) (вүӨ-trans sucmвүӨsum enough')

    stepEqвӮҖ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m вүӨв„• n) вүЎ suc g
    stepEqвӮҖ = trans (sym (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n)) eq

    stepEq : gcd-fuel fuel (m вҲё n) (suc n) вүЎ suc g
    stepEq = trans (sym (cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq)) stepEqвӮҖ

    rec : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (m вҲё n) Г— DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (suc n)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ fuel (m вҲё n) (suc n) g subEnough stepEq

    dm-rest : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (m вҲё n)
    dm-rest = fst rec

    dn : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (suc n)
    dn = snd rec
  in
  dividesвҒә-restore-вҲё (sвүӨs p) dn dm-rest , dn
\end{code}

The inductive core of the gcd engine is established: for any fuel level sufficient to reach termination, the positive output divides both inputs. The remaining step generalises this to the canonical fuel formula $\texttt{slack} + (m + n)$, which always provides enough iterations. This is the interface consumed by the rational reduction machinery above.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Canonical-or-greater fuel is enough to turn any positive output into a common divisor.
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides :
  (slack m n g : в„•) вҶ’
  (gcd-fuel (slack +в„• (m +в„• n)) m n вүЎ suc g) вҶ’
  ((DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) m) Г— (DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) n))
gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides slack m n g eq =
  gcd-fuel-positive-output-dividesвүӨ
    (slack +в„• (m +в„• n))
    m
    n
    g
    (subst
      (О» t вҶ’ (m +в„• n) вүӨ t)
      (+в„•-comm (m +в„• n) slack)
      (вүӨ-self+в„• (m +в„• n) slack))
    eq

  -- В§ Phase 3: Positive total mass forces a positive gcd

-- В§ Any common positive divisor of m and n divides gcd m n.
common-divisor-divides-gcd :
  {d : в„•вҒә} вҶ’
  (m n : в„•) вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d m вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d n вҶ’
  DividesвҒә d (gcd m n)
common-divisor-divides-gcd {d} m n dm dn =
  subst
    (DividesвҒә d)
    (sym (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd-fuel t m n) (+-to-+в„• m n)))
    (gcd-fuel-common-divisor (m +в„• n) m n dm dn)

-- В§ Under sufficient fuel, a positive total input mass forces a positive gcd-fuel output.
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ :
  (fuel m n s : в„•) вҶ’
  (m +в„• n вүЎ suc s) вҶ’
  ((m +в„• n) вүӨ fuel) вҶ’
  ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ gcd-fuel fuel m n вүЎ suc g)
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ zero zero zero s () enough
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ zero zero (suc n) s sumEq ()
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ zero (suc m) zero s sumEq ()
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ zero (suc m) (suc n) s sumEq ()
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ (suc fuel) zero zero s () enough
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ (suc fuel) zero (suc n) s sumEq enough = n , gcd-fuel-zero-left-suc fuel n
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ (suc fuel) (suc m) zero s sumEq enough = m , gcd-fuel-zero-right-suc fuel m
gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ (suc fuel) (suc m) (suc n) s sumEq (sвүӨs enough') with inspect (m вүӨв„• n)
... | reveal true bEq =
  let
    p : m вүӨ n
    p = вүӨв„•-trueвҶ’вүӨ bEq

    sucnвүӨsum : suc n вүӨ (m +в„• suc n)
    sucnвүӨsum = subst (О» t вҶ’ suc n вүӨ t) (+в„•-comm (suc n) m) (вүӨ-self+в„• (suc n) m)

    subEnough : ((suc m) +в„• (n вҲё m)) вүӨ fuel
    subEnough = subst (О» t вҶ’ t вүӨ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-true p)) (вүӨ-trans sucnвүӨsum enough')

    subSum : (suc m) +в„• (n вҲё m) вүЎ suc n
    subSum = euclid-sum-true p

    stepEqвӮҖ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m вүӨв„• n) вүЎ gcd-fuel fuel (suc m) (n вҲё m)
    stepEqвӮҖ = cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq

    rec : ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ gcd-fuel fuel (suc m) (n вҲё m) вүЎ suc g)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ fuel (suc m) (n вҲё m) n subSum subEnough
  in
  fst rec , trans (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n) (trans stepEqвӮҖ (snd rec))
... | reveal false bEq =
  let
    p : n вүӨ m
    p = вүӨв„•-falseвҶ’вүӨ bEq

    sucmвүӨsum : suc m вүӨ (m +в„• suc n)
    sucmвүӨsum = subst (О» t вҶ’ suc m вүӨ t) (sym (+в„•-suc-right m n)) (вүӨ-self+в„• (suc m) n)

    subEnough : ((m вҲё n) +в„• (suc n)) вүӨ fuel
    subEnough = subst (О» t вҶ’ t вүӨ fuel) (sym (euclid-sum-false p)) (вүӨ-trans sucmвүӨsum enough')

    subSum : (m вҲё n) +в„• (suc n) вүЎ suc m
    subSum = euclid-sum-false p

    stepEqвӮҖ : gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n) (m вүӨв„• n) вүЎ gcd-fuel fuel (m вҲё n) (suc n)
    stepEqвӮҖ = cong (gcd-fuel-step fuel (suc m) (suc n)) bEq

    rec : ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ gcd-fuel fuel (m вҲё n) (suc n) вүЎ suc g)
    rec = gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ fuel (m вҲё n) (suc n) m subSum subEnough
  in
  fst rec , trans (gcd-fuel-suc-suc fuel m n) (trans stepEqвӮҖ (snd rec))

-- В§ The canonical gcd of a positive denominator is itself positive.
gcd-positive-right : (m : в„•) вҶ’ (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ gcd m (вҒәtoв„• d) вүЎ suc g)
gcd-positive-right m d =
  let
    pack : ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ gcd-fuel (m +в„• вҒәtoв„• d) m (вҒәtoв„• d) вүЎ suc g)
    pack =
      gcd-fuel-positive-sumвүӨ
        (m +в„• вҒәtoв„• d)
        m
        (вҒәtoв„• d)
        (m +в„• pred d)
        (+в„•-suc-right m (pred d))
        (вүӨ-refl (m +в„• вҒәtoв„• d))
  in
  fst pack ,
    trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd-fuel t m (вҒәtoв„• d)) (+-to-+в„• m (вҒәtoв„• d)))
      (snd pack)
\end{code}

\subsection{Canonical reduction candidate and divisibility}

The gcd engine and the divisibility theory are both in place.  The canonical reduction candidate is therefore forced: given a rational $q = n/d$, compute $g = \gcd(|n|, d)$, and define the reduced numerator as $|n|/g$ and the reduced denominator as $d/g$.  The ``candidate'' is not a guess; it is the unique pair obtained by dividing out the maximal common factor.

Three properties must survive.  First, the canonical divisor $g$ is itself positive---forced by the gcd-positivity theorem on the positive denominator.  Second, the в„•-view of the divisor equals the computed gcd---a bookkeeping identity that eliminates the successor-encoding detour.  Third, the divisor divides both the numerator magnitude and the denominator---forced by the output-divisibility theorem already established for the gcd engine.

\textbf{Constraint:} The reduction candidate must be computable from the raw fields and must preserve the rational class up to~$\simeq_{\mathbb{Q}}$.  Any alternative decomposition would reintroduce presentation freedom.

\textbf{Construction:} Any reduction candidate not computable from the raw fields, or failing to preserve the rational class up to~$\simeq_{\mathbb{Q}}$, is eliminated. The unique surviving candidate is \texttt{canonical-reduction-divisor} $= \texttt{mkв„•вҒә}(\gcd(|n|,d) - 1)$.
The numerator and denominator are then forced by exact division.
Positivity, value agreement, and divisibility follow from the candidate's definition.

\textbf{Consequence:} The candidate is well-defined and the gcd divides both fields.  The factorization and coprimality proofs that follow proceed from concrete divisibility witnesses.

\begin{code}
-- В§ The successor-coded positive natural is definitionally mkв„•вҒә.
sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә : (n : в„•) вҶ’ sucToв„•вҒә n вүЎ mkв„•вҒә n
sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә zero = refl
sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә (suc n) = cong sucвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә n)

-- В§ The gcd used by the canonical reduction candidate.
canonical-reduction-divisor : в„ҡ вҶ’ в„•вҒә
canonical-reduction-divisor q = mkв„•вҒә (gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вҲё 1)

-- В§ Phase 2: Divide both fields by the forced gcd

-- В§ Candidate reduced numerator obtained by dividing out the computed gcd.
canonical-reduction-num : в„ҡ вҶ’ в„•
canonical-reduction-num q = magв„Ө (num q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q

-- В§ Candidate reduced denominator obtained by dividing out the computed gcd.
canonical-reduction-den : в„ҡ вҶ’ в„•вҒә
canonical-reduction-den q = mkв„•вҒә ((вҒәtoв„• (den q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q) вҲё 1)

-- В§ Package the raw gcd/division reduction candidate.
record CanonicalReductionCandidate (q : в„ҡ) : Set where
  field
    cand-divisor : в„•вҒә
    cand-num : в„•
    cand-den : в„•вҒә

canonical-reduction-candidate : (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’ CanonicalReductionCandidate q
canonical-reduction-candidate q = record
  { cand-divisor = canonical-reduction-divisor q
  ; cand-num = canonical-reduction-num q
  ; cand-den = canonical-reduction-den q
  }

-- В§ The canonical reduction divisor is the positive gcd itself.
canonical-reduction-divisor-positive :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ canonical-reduction-divisor q вүЎ sucToв„•вҒә g)
canonical-reduction-divisor-positive q =
  let
    gPack : ОЈ в„• (О» g вҶ’ gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вүЎ suc g)
    gPack = gcd-positive-right (magв„Ө (num q)) (den q)

    g : в„•
    g = fst gPack

    gcdEq : gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вүЎ suc g
    gcdEq = snd gPack
  in
  g ,
    trans
      (cong (О» t вҶ’ mkв„•вҒә (t вҲё 1)) gcdEq)
      (trans
        (cong mkв„•вҒә (вҲё-one-suc g))
        (sym (sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә g)))

-- В§ The в„•-view of the canonical reduction divisor is the computed gcd.
canonical-reduction-divisor-value :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q) вүЎ gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q))
canonical-reduction-divisor-value q with gcd-positive-right (magв„Ө (num q)) (den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    divEq : canonical-reduction-divisor q вүЎ sucToв„•вҒә g
    divEq =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ mkв„•вҒә (t вҲё 1)) gcdEq)
        (trans
          (cong mkв„•вҒә (вҲё-one-suc g))
          (sym (sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә g)))
  in
  trans
    (cong вҒәtoв„• divEq)
    (trans
      (вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә g)
      (sym gcdEq))

-- В§ The canonical reduction divisor divides the visible numerator magnitude.
canonical-reduction-divides-num :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  DividesвҒә (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (magв„Ө (num q))
canonical-reduction-divides-num q with gcd-positive-right (magв„Ө (num q)) (den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    divEq : canonical-reduction-divisor q вүЎ sucToв„•вҒә g
    divEq =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ mkв„•вҒә (t вҲё 1)) gcdEq)
        (trans
          (cong mkв„•вҒә (вҲё-one-suc g))
          (sym (sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә g)))

    gcdEq+в„• : gcd-fuel (magв„Ө (num q) +в„• вҒәtoв„• (den q)) (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вүЎ suc g
    gcdEq+в„• =
      trans
        (sym (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd-fuel t (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q))) (+-to-+в„• (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)))))
        gcdEq
  in
  subst
    (О» t вҶ’ DividesвҒә t (magв„Ө (num q)))
    (sym divEq)
    (fst
      (gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides
        zero
        (magв„Ө (num q))
        (вҒәtoв„• (den q))
        g
        gcdEq+в„•))

-- В§ The canonical reduction divisor divides the visible denominator.
canonical-reduction-divides-den :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  DividesвҒә (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (вҒәtoв„• (den q))
canonical-reduction-divides-den q with gcd-positive-right (magв„Ө (num q)) (den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    divEq : canonical-reduction-divisor q вүЎ sucToв„•вҒә g
    divEq =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ mkв„•вҒә (t вҲё 1)) gcdEq)
        (trans
          (cong mkв„•вҒә (вҲё-one-suc g))
          (sym (sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә g)))

    gcdEq+в„• : gcd-fuel (magв„Ө (num q) +в„• вҒәtoв„• (den q)) (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вүЎ suc g
    gcdEq+в„• =
      trans
        (sym (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd-fuel t (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q))) (+-to-+в„• (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)))))
        gcdEq
  in
  subst
    (О» t вҶ’ DividesвҒә t (вҒәtoв„• (den q)))
    (sym divEq)
    (snd
      (gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides
        zero
        (magв„Ө (num q))
        (вҒәtoв„• (den q))
        g
        gcdEq+в„•))
\end{code}

\subsection{Factorization, coprimality, and witness stabilization}

The canonical reduction divisor now divides both the numerator magnitude and the denominator.  The next forced step is to show that dividing out the gcd produces a coprime pair---so that the candidate is genuinely reduced---and that the entire candidate stabilizes on any input that is already reduced.

Factorization is read directly from the exact-division infrastructure: since the gcd divides both fields, exact division recovers the quotients, and the original fields are the product of quotient times divisor.  Coprimality then follows by contradiction: any common divisor of the reduced pair would lift, through multiplication by the gcd, to a common divisor of the \emph{original} pair that exceeds the gcd itself---which contradicts the maximality of the gcd.

Stabilization completes the picture.  If the input is already irreducible, the gcd is~$1$, division by one leaves the fields unchanged, and the candidate collapses to the original witness.  This guarantees that the reduction pipeline is idempotent: applying it twice yields the same result as applying it once.

\textbf{Constraint:} Without coprimality, the ``reduced'' candidate might still hide common factors, and the uniqueness guarantee downstream would fail.

\textbf{Elimination:} Factor both fields through exact division; eliminate any putative common divisor by lifting it back through the gcd---where it is absorbed; eliminate instability by specializing to divisor $= 1$. The candidate survives all three tests.

\textbf{Consequence:} The canonical candidate is a genuine reduced pair.  The reduced witness is idempotent.  Every later reference to ``the reduced form'' is unambiguous.

\begin{code}
-- В§ The candidate numerator factorization follows from exact division.
canonical-reduction-num-factor :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  magв„Ө (num q)
  вүЎ canonical-reduction-num q *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
canonical-reduction-num-factor q =
  let
    dvd : DividesвҒә (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (magв„Ө (num q))
    dvd = canonical-reduction-divides-num q
  in
  trans
    (DividesвҒә.factorвҒә dvd)
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)) (sym (dividesвҒәвҶ’div dvd)))

-- В§ The candidate denominator factorization follows from exact division.
canonical-reduction-den-factor :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  вҒәtoв„• (den q)
  вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q) *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
canonical-reduction-den-factor q =
  let
    dvd : DividesвҒә (canonical-reduction-divisor q) (вҒәtoв„• (den q))
    dvd = canonical-reduction-divides-den q

    qPos : ОЈ в„• (О» k вҶ’ quotientвҒә dvd вүЎ suc k)
    qPos = dividesвҒә-positive-quotient dvd

    k : в„•
    k = fst qPos

    qEq : quotientвҒә dvd вүЎ suc k
    qEq = snd qPos

    denQuotEq : вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q) вүЎ quotientвҒә dvd
    denQuotEq =
      let
        denEq : canonical-reduction-den q вүЎ sucToв„•вҒә k
        denEq =
          trans
            (cong mkв„•вҒә (cong (О» t вҶ’ t вҲё 1) (trans (dividesвҒәвҶ’div dvd) qEq)))
            (trans
              (cong mkв„•вҒә (вҲё-one-suc k))
              (sym (sucToв„•вҒә-mkв„•вҒә k)))
      in
      trans
        (cong вҒәtoв„• denEq)
        (trans
          (вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә k)
          (sym qEq))
  in
  trans
    (DividesвҒә.factorвҒә dvd)
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)) (sym denQuotEq))

-- В§ Any common positive divisor of the canonical reduced pair is forced to be one.
canonical-reduction-common-divisor-one :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  {e : в„•вҒә} вҶ’
  DividesвҒә e (canonical-reduction-num q) вҶ’
  DividesвҒә e (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)) вҶ’
  e вүЎ oneвҒә
canonical-reduction-common-divisor-one q {e} dnum dden =
  let
    liftedNum : DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q) (magв„Ө (num q))
    liftedNum =
      subst
        (DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q))
          (sym (canonical-reduction-num-factor q))
        (dividesвҒә-mul-right dnum (canonical-reduction-divisor q))

    liftedDen : DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q) (вҒәtoв„• (den q))
    liftedDen =
      subst
        (DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q))
          (sym (canonical-reduction-den-factor q))
        (dividesвҒә-mul-right dden (canonical-reduction-divisor q))

    gcdDiv : DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q) (gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)))
    gcdDiv = common-divisor-divides-gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) liftedNum liftedDen

    divisorDiv : DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q))
    divisorDiv =
      subst
        (DividesвҒә (e *вҒә canonical-reduction-divisor q))
          (sym (canonical-reduction-divisor-value q))
        gcdDiv
  in
  dividesвҒә-product-factor-left-one e (canonical-reduction-divisor q) divisorDiv

-- В§ Phase 2: Eliminate every leftover common factor

-- В§ The canonical reduced numerator and denominator are coprime.
canonical-reduction-candidate-coprime :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  gcd (canonical-reduction-num q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)) вүЎ 1
canonical-reduction-candidate-coprime q with gcd-positive-right (canonical-reduction-num q) (canonical-reduction-den q)
... | g , gcdEq =
  let
    gcdEq+в„• : gcd-fuel (canonical-reduction-num q +в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)) (canonical-reduction-num q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)) вүЎ suc g
    gcdEq+в„• =
      trans
        (sym (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd-fuel t (canonical-reduction-num q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q))) (+-to-+в„• (canonical-reduction-num q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)))))
        gcdEq

    divisorPair : DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (canonical-reduction-num q) Г— DividesвҒә (sucToв„•вҒә g) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q))
    divisorPair =
      gcd-fuel-positive-output-divides
        zero
        (canonical-reduction-num q)
        (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q))
        g
        gcdEq+в„•

    oneEq : sucToв„•вҒә g вүЎ oneвҒә
    oneEq = canonical-reduction-common-divisor-one q (fst divisorPair) (snd divisorPair)
  in
  trans
    gcdEq
    (trans
      (sym (вҒәtoв„•-sucToв„•вҒә g))
      (cong вҒәtoв„• oneEq))

-- В§ A reduced witness fixes the visible quotient fields of q.
reduced-witness-fields :
  {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’
  num q вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r)
  Г— den q вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
reduced-witness-fields {n / d} r =
  rational-fields-unique (n / d)
    n
    (fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r))
    d
    (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    refl
    (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r)
\end{code}

The gcd machinery has confirmed that the canonical divisor is one, and the field-extraction lemma locks the numerator and denominator of the reduced witness to the original quotient fields. These are bookkeeping results---they do not change the mathematical content, but they allow every subsequent proof to refer to the reduced form without re-deriving the field correspondence each time.

The next step converts a reduced witness into a full irreducibility certificate. Once this is in hand, any rational that admits a reduced witness is provably in lowest terms, and no further simplification is possible.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Every reduced witness certifies irreducibility of the represented quotient.
reduced-witness-irred : {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’ (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’ IrreducibleQuotient q
reduced-witness-irred {q} r =
  let
    numEq = fst (reduced-witness-fields r)
    denEq = snd (reduced-witness-fields r)
    magEq : magв„Ө (num q) вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
    magEq = trans (cong magв„Ө numEq) (magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r))

    denNatEq : вҒәtoв„• (den q) вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    denNatEq = cong вҒәtoв„• denEq

    gcdEq : gcd (magв„Ө (num q)) (вҒәtoв„• (den q)) вүЎ gcd (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r) (вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))
    gcdEq =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd t (вҒәtoв„• (den q))) magEq)
        (cong (gcd (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r)) denNatEq)
  in
  trans gcdEq (ReducedRationalWitness.red-coprime r)

-- В§ On a reduced witness, the canonical gcd collapses to one.
canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced :
  {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’
  canonical-reduction-divisor q вүЎ oneвҒә
canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r =
  trans
    (cong (О» t вҶ’ mkв„•вҒә (t вҲё 1)) (reduced-witness-irred r))
    refl

-- В§ Phase 3: Stabilization on already reduced input

-- В§ The canonical candidate numerator stabilizes on any reduced witness.
canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced :
  {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’
  canonical-reduction-num q вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced {q} r =
  let
    numEq = fst (reduced-witness-fields r)
    magEq : magв„Ө (num q) вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
    magEq = trans (cong magв„Ө numEq) (magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r))

    divEq :
      magв„Ө (num q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q
      вүЎ magв„Ө (num q) div oneвҒә
    divEq = cong (О» t вҶ’ magв„Ө (num q) div t) (canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r)
  in
  trans divEq (trans (div-oneвҒә (magв„Ө (num q))) magEq)

-- В§ The canonical candidate denominator stabilizes on any reduced witness.
canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced :
  {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’
  canonical-reduction-den q вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced {q} r =
  let
    denEq = snd (reduced-witness-fields r)
    denNatEq : вҒәtoв„• (den q) вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    denNatEq = cong вҒәtoв„• denEq

    step :
      (вҒәtoв„• (den q) div canonical-reduction-divisor q)
      вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r)
    step =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ вҒәtoв„• (den q) div t) (canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r))
        (trans (div-oneвҒә (вҒәtoв„• (den q))) denNatEq)
  in
  trans
    (cong mkв„•вҒә (cong (О» t вҶ’ t вҲё 1) step))
    (mkв„•вҒә-вҒәtoв„•-minus-one (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))

-- В§ The full canonical reduction candidate stabilizes on any reduced witness.
canon-red-candidate-from-reduced :
  {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’
  (r : ReducedRationalWitness q) вҶ’
  CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-divisor
    (canonical-reduction-candidate q) вүЎ oneвҒә
  Г— CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-num
      (canonical-reduction-candidate q) вүЎ
      ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
  Г— CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-den
      (canonical-reduction-candidate q) вүЎ
      ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
canon-red-candidate-from-reduced r =
  canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced r
  , ( canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced r
    , canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced r
    )

-- В§ Nonnegative rationals expose a natural numerator presentation.
nonnegative-rational-presentation :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q вҶ’
  ОЈ в„• (О» n вҶ’ q вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө n / den q)
nonnegative-rational-presentation (a / d) aвүҘ0 with 0вүӨв„ӨвҶ’fromв„•в„Ө a aвүҘ0
... | n , aвүЎ = n , cong (О» t вҶ’ t / d) aвүЎ

-- В§ Irreducible nonnegative quotients already carry a global reduced witness.
irreducible-nonnegative-witness :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  IrreducibleQuotient q вҶ’
  0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q вҶ’
  ReducedRationalWitness q
irreducible-nonnegative-witness (a / d) irred aвүҘ0 with 0вүӨв„ӨвҶ’fromв„•в„Ө a aвүҘ0
... | n , aвүЎ = record
  { red-num = n
  ; red-den = d
  ; red-value = cong (О» t вҶ’ t / d) aвүЎ
  ; red-coprime =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd t (вҒәtoв„• d)) (sym (trans (cong magв„Ө aвүЎ) (magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө n))))
        irred
  }

-- В§ The canonical candidate becomes a concrete reduced witness on irreducible nonnegative input.
canonical-reduction-on-irreducible-nonnegative :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  (irred : IrreducibleQuotient q) вҶ’
  (qвүҘ0 : 0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q) вҶ’
  CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-divisor (canonical-reduction-candidate q) вүЎ oneвҒә
  Г— CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-num (canonical-reduction-candidate q)
      вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num (irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred qвүҘ0)
  Г— CanonicalReductionCandidate.cand-den (canonical-reduction-candidate q)
      вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-den (irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred qвүҘ0)
canonical-reduction-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred qвүҘ0 =
  canon-red-candidate-from-reduced
    (irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred qвүҘ0)
\end{code}

\subsection{Normal forms and sign handling}

The canonical reduction machinery so far operates on magnitudes: it divides a natural numerator and a positive denominator by their gcd.  But a rational number is not a magnitude---it carries a sign.  The \texttt{ReducedRationalForm} record captures the quotient-level normal form for nonnegative classes, where the numerator is still a natural number and the equivalence is up to~$\simeq_{\mathbb{Q}}$ rather than raw definitional equality.  This weaker formulation is forced because gcd-reduction may change the concrete numerator and denominator while preserving the rational class.

For genuinely arbitrary classes including negative ones, a \texttt{SignedReducedRationalForm} extends the nonneg form by allowing the numerator to be an integer.  The bridge between the two is the sign-transport infrastructure: negation preserves rational equivalence (\texttt{-в„ҡ-resp-вүғ}), and the magnitude of a negated embedded natural is still the original natural (\texttt{magв„Ө-neg-fromв„•в„Ө}).  These two facts together allow a negative class $(-n)/d$ to be reduced by first reducing $(+n)/d$ and then transporting the result through negation.

\textbf{Constraint:} Negative rationals cannot be reduced by the nonneg pipeline directly; their sign must be factored out, the magnitude reduced, and the sign restored.

\textbf{Construction:} Any normal form that fails to cover the negative sector is eliminated by the sign-transport constraint. The only surviving forms are \texttt{ReducedRationalForm} for the nonneg sector; \texttt{SignedReducedRationalForm} as the signed generalisation; \texttt{reduced-formвҶ’signed} as the canonical embedding; and \texttt{-в„ҡ-resp-вүғ} plus \texttt{magв„Ө-neg-fromв„•в„Ө} as the sign-transport lemmas.

\textbf{Consequence:} Every nonneg form lifts to a signed form.  Every negative class is reduced by negation transport.  The zero class is trivially nonneg.  Together, these three cases cover all of~$\mathbb{Q}$.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Positive denominators are exactly natural embeddings.
вҒәtoв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө : (d : в„•вҒә) вҶ’ вҒәtoв„Ө d вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө (вҒәtoв„• d)
вҒәtoв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө (mkв„•вҒә p) = refl

-- В§ Quotient-level reduced normal form for an arbitrary rational class.
record ReducedRationalForm (q : в„ҡ) : Set where
  field
    form-num : в„•
    form-den : в„•вҒә
    form-value : q вүғв„ҡ (fromв„•в„Ө form-num / form-den)
    form-coprime : gcd form-num (вҒәtoв„• form-den) вүЎ 1

-- В§ Signed quotient-level reduced normal form for a genuinely arbitrary rational class.
record SignedReducedRationalForm (q : в„ҡ) : Set where
  field
    signed-num : в„Ө
    signed-den : в„•вҒә
    signed-value : q вүғв„ҡ (signed-num / signed-den)
    signed-coprime : gcd (magв„Ө signed-num) (вҒәtoв„• signed-den) вүЎ 1

-- В§ Every strict reduced witness yields a quotient-level reduced form.
reduced-witnessвҶ’form : {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’ ReducedRationalWitness q вҶ’ ReducedRationalForm q
reduced-witnessвҶ’form {q} r = record
  { form-num = ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r
  ; form-den = ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r
  ; form-value =
      subst
        (О» t вҶ’ t вүғв„ҡ (fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r) / ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))
      (sym (ReducedRationalWitness.red-value r))
        (вүғв„ҡ-refl (fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num r) / ReducedRationalWitness.red-den r))
  ; form-coprime = ReducedRationalWitness.red-coprime r
  }

-- В§ Negation preserves rational equivalence.
-в„ҡ-resp-вүғ : {p q : в„ҡ} вҶ’ p вүғв„ҡ q вҶ’ (-в„ҡ p) вүғв„ҡ (-в„ҡ q)
-в„ҡ-resp-вүғ {a / b} {c / d} eq =
  trans
    (*в„Ө-neg-left a (вҒәtoв„Ө d))
    (trans
      (cong negв„Ө eq)
      (sym (*в„Ө-neg-left c (вҒәtoв„Ө b))))

-- В§ Magnitude ignores the transported sign on embedded naturals.
magв„Ө-neg-fromв„•в„Ө : (n : в„•) вҶ’ magв„Ө (negв„Ө (fromв„•в„Ө n)) вүЎ n
magв„Ө-neg-fromв„•в„Ө zero = refl
magв„Ө-neg-fromв„•в„Ө (suc n) = refl

-- В§ Any nonnegative quotient-level reduced form is already a signed one.
reduced-formвҶ’signed : {q : в„ҡ} вҶ’ ReducedRationalForm q вҶ’ SignedReducedRationalForm q
reduced-formвҶ’signed {q} r = record
  { signed-num = fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalForm.form-num r)
  ; signed-den = ReducedRationalForm.form-den r
  ; signed-value = ReducedRationalForm.form-value r
  ; signed-coprime =
      trans
        (cong
          (О» t вҶ’ gcd t (вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalForm.form-den r)))
          (magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalForm.form-num r)))
        (ReducedRationalForm.form-coprime r)
  }
\end{code}

\subsection{Global reduction theorems}

The individual components---coprimality, factorization, sign handling, nonnegative witnesses---are all in place.  What remains is to forge them into the global theorem: every rational class, regardless of sign, admits a canonical signed reduced form.

The route is forced.  For nonnegative numerators, the factorization data is already available: the gcd divides both numerator magnitude and denominator, the candidate quotients are coprime, and the factorization record can be assembled outright.  For negative numerators, the argument proceeds by negation transport: the positive representative $|n|/d$ is reduced first, and the result is then carried back through \texttt{-в„ҡ-resp-вүғ} and magnitude-invariance of negation on embedded naturals.  The zero class collapses immediately.

\textbf{Constraint:} Without a global theorem covering all three sign cases, the bridge chapter would need ad hoc case splits at every correction fraction.

\textbf{Construction:} Each sign case is handled and eliminated in turn: the nonnegative factorization is packaged into \texttt{CanonicalReductionFactorization}, the quotient-level \texttt{ReducedRationalForm} is derived, the lift to \texttt{SignedReducedRationalForm} closes the nonneg case, and the negative case is eliminated by reducing the negation and transporting the result. No sign case escapes.

\textbf{Consequence:} \texttt{canonical-reduction-form} is total on all of~$\mathbb{Q}$: zero, positive, and negative classes.  The downstream companion modules may now invoke reduced quotient data without case analysis.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Explicit factorization data needed to make the canonical candidate sound globally.
record CanonicalReductionFactorization (q : в„ҡ) : Set where
  field
    num-nonnegative : 0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q
    num-factor :
      magв„Ө (num q)
      вүЎ canonical-reduction-num q *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    den-factor :
      вҒәtoв„• (den q)
      вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q) *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    candidate-coprime :
      gcd (canonical-reduction-num q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)) вүЎ 1

-- В§ With gcd-factorization laws established, the canonical candidate is the quotient-level reduced form.
canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  CanonicalReductionFactorization q вҶ’
  ReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization (a / dq) f = record
  { form-num = canonical-reduction-num (a / dq)
  ; form-den = canonical-reduction-den (a / dq)
  ; form-value = valueProof
  ; form-coprime = CanonicalReductionFactorization.candidate-coprime f
  }
  where
    divr : в„•вҒә
    divr = canonical-reduction-divisor (a / dq)

    cnum : в„•
    cnum = canonical-reduction-num (a / dq)

    cden : в„•вҒә
    cden = canonical-reduction-den (a / dq)

    aNatPack : ОЈ в„• (О» n вҶ’ a вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө n)
    aNatPack = 0вүӨв„ӨвҶ’fromв„•в„Ө a (CanonicalReductionFactorization.num-nonnegative f)

    aNat : в„•
    aNat = fst aNatPack

    aвүЎ : a вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө aNat
    aвүЎ = snd aNatPack

    numFactorEq : aNat вүЎ cnum *в„• вҒәtoв„• divr
    numFactorEq =
      trans
        (sym (magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө aNat))
        (trans
          (cong magв„Ө (sym aвүЎ))
          (CanonicalReductionFactorization.num-factor f))

    lhsNum : a *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө cden вүЎ (fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө divr) *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө cden
    lhsNum =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө cden) aвүЎ)
        (trans
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ fromв„•в„Ө t *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө cden) numFactorEq)
          (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө cden) (sym (fromв„•в„Ө-mul-вҒә cnum divr))))

    lhsRegroup : a *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө cden вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө ((вҒәtoв„Ө divr) *в„Ө (вҒәtoв„Ө cden))
    lhsRegroup =
      trans lhsNum (*в„Ө-assoc (fromв„•в„Ө cnum) (вҒәtoв„Ө divr) (вҒәtoв„Ө cden))

    rhsDen : вҒәtoв„Ө dq вүЎ (вҒәtoв„Ө cden) *в„Ө (вҒәtoв„Ө divr)
    rhsDen =
      trans
        (вҒәtoв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө dq)
        (trans
          (cong fromв„•в„Ө (CanonicalReductionFactorization.den-factor f))
          (trans
            (sym (fromв„•в„Ө-mul-вҒә (вҒәtoв„• cden) divr))
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ t *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө divr) (вҒәtoв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө cden))))

    rhsRegroup : fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө вҒәtoв„Ө dq вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө ((вҒәtoв„Ө cden) *в„Ө (вҒәtoв„Ө divr))
    rhsRegroup = cong (О» t вҶ’ fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө t) rhsDen

    middleComm : fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө ((вҒәtoв„Ө divr) *в„Ө (вҒәtoв„Ө cden)) вүЎ fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө ((вҒәtoв„Ө cden) *в„Ө (вҒәtoв„Ө divr))
    middleComm = cong (О» t вҶ’ fromв„•в„Ө cnum *в„Ө t) (*в„Ө-comm (вҒәtoв„Ө divr) (вҒәtoв„Ө cden))

    valueProof : (a / dq) вүғв„ҡ (fromв„•в„Ө cnum / cden)
    valueProof = trans lhsRegroup (trans middleComm (sym rhsRegroup))

-- В§ The canonical reduction candidate carries full factorization data for nonnegative rationals.
canonical-reduction-factorization-nonnegative :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q вҶ’
  CanonicalReductionFactorization q
canonical-reduction-factorization-nonnegative q qвүҘ0 = record
  { num-nonnegative = qвүҘ0
  ; num-factor = canonical-reduction-num-factor q
  ; den-factor = canonical-reduction-den-factor q
  ; candidate-coprime = canonical-reduction-candidate-coprime q
  }

-- В§ Every nonnegative rational class has a canonical quotient-level reduced form.
canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q вҶ’
  ReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative q qвүҘ0 =
  canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization q (canonical-reduction-factorization-nonnegative q qвүҘ0)
\end{code}

The nonnegative case is fully resolved: every nonnegative rational has a unique factorization through the canonical candidate, and that candidate yields a quotient-level reduced form. What remains is the sign barrier---negative rationals must be handled by reducing their magnitude and then transporting the result through negation.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Canonical reduction yields a signed quotient-level reduced form for every rational.
canonical-reduction-form :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  SignedReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form (0в„Ө / d) =
  reduced-formвҶ’signed (canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative (0в„Ө / d) tt)
canonical-reduction-form ((+suc n) / d) =
  reduced-formвҶ’signed (canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative ((+suc n) / d) tt)
canonical-reduction-form ((-suc n) / d) = record
  { signed-num = negв„Ө (fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalForm.form-num negForm))
  ; signed-den = ReducedRationalForm.form-den negForm
  ; signed-value = -в„ҡ-resp-вүғ (ReducedRationalForm.form-value negForm)
  ; signed-coprime =
      trans
        (cong
          (О» t вҶ’ gcd t (вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalForm.form-den negForm)))
          (magв„Ө-neg-fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalForm.form-num negForm)))
        (ReducedRationalForm.form-coprime negForm)
  }
  where
    negForm : ReducedRationalForm ((+suc n) / d)
    negForm = canonical-reduction-form-nonnegative ((+suc n) / d) tt

-- В§ In the irreducible nonnegative sector, the conditional factorization data is already forced.
canonical-reduction-factorization-on-irreducible-nonnegative :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  (irred : IrreducibleQuotient q) вҶ’
  (qвүҘ0 : 0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q) вҶ’
  CanonicalReductionFactorization q
canonical-reduction-factorization-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred qвүҘ0 = record
  { num-nonnegative = qвүҘ0
  ; num-factor = numFactor
  ; den-factor = denFactor
  ; candidate-coprime = candidateCoprime
  }
  where
    rw : ReducedRationalWitness q
    rw = irreducible-nonnegative-witness q irred qвүҘ0

    numWitnessEq : magв„Ө (num q) вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw
    numWitnessEq =
      trans
        (cong magв„Ө (fst (reduced-witness-fields rw)))
        (magв„Ө-fromв„•в„Ө (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw))

    denWitnessEq : вҒәtoв„• (den q) вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rw)
    denWitnessEq = cong вҒәtoв„• (snd (reduced-witness-fields rw))

    divNatEq : вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q) вүЎ 1
    divNatEq = cong вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor-from-reduced rw)

    numCandEq : canonical-reduction-num q вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw
    numCandEq = canonical-reduction-num-from-reduced rw

    denCandNatEq : вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q) вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (ReducedRationalWitness.red-den rw)
    denCandNatEq = cong вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den-from-reduced rw)

    numFactor : magв„Ө (num q) вүЎ canonical-reduction-num q *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    numFactor =
      trans
        (trans numWitnessEq (sym numCandEq))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ canonical-reduction-num q *в„• t) divNatEq)
            (*в„•-one-right (canonical-reduction-num q))))

    denFactor : вҒәtoв„• (den q) вүЎ вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q) *в„• вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-divisor q)
    denFactor =
      trans
        (trans denWitnessEq (sym denCandNatEq))
        (sym
          (trans
            (cong (О» t вҶ’ вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q) *в„• t) divNatEq)
            (*в„•-one-right (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)))))

    candidateCoprime : gcd (canonical-reduction-num q) (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q)) вүЎ 1
    candidateCoprime =
      trans
        (cong (О» t вҶ’ gcd t (вҒәtoв„• (canonical-reduction-den q))) numCandEq)
        (trans
          (cong (gcd (ReducedRationalWitness.red-num rw)) denCandNatEq)
          (ReducedRationalWitness.red-coprime rw))

-- В§ The canonical candidate already yields a quotient-level reduced form on irreducible nonnegative input.
canonical-reduction-form-on-irreducible-nonnegative :
  (q : в„ҡ) вҶ’
  (irred : IrreducibleQuotient q) вҶ’
  (qвүҘ0 : 0в„Ө вүӨв„Ө num q) вҶ’
  ReducedRationalForm q
canonical-reduction-form-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred qвүҘ0 =
  canonical-reduction-form-from-factorization q
    (canonical-reduction-factorization-on-irreducible-nonnegative q irred qвүҘ0)
\end{code}

\subsection{Observable quotient layer}

The class-level reduction is complete: every rational carries a signed reduced form.  But the invariant chain does not touch rationals in isolation.  It touches \emph{admissible rational observables}---functions from drift states into $\mathbb{Q}$ that already survive the admissibility filter.  If quotient data were not lifted pointwise along these observables, the bridge chapter would be forced to re-derive the presentation at every call site, and the uniqueness guarantee would dissolve into ad hoc casework.

The lift is immediate.  An admissible rational observable already assigns a rational value at each drift state.  Reading off the constructor fields at each state gives the canonical pointwise presentation; the uniqueness of those fields then propagates pointwise.  Reduced witnesses are similarly lifted: a reduced observable carries a reduced witness at every state, and whenever two such witnesses exist, they agree pointwise because the class-level uniqueness theorem already forces agreement field by field.

\textbf{Constraint:} If quotient fields were not globally visible on admissible observables, the correction fractions would lack a canonical numerator/denominator decomposition at each measurement point.

\textbf{Construction:} Any observable layer that fails to expose quotient data pointwise is eliminated by the admissibility constraint. The only surviving form lifts \texttt{RationalPresentation} and \texttt{ReducedRationalWitness} pointwise along \texttt{AdmissibleRationalObservable}, inheriting uniqueness from the class-level theorems.

\textbf{Consequence:} The bridge chapter may now invoke quotient data and reduced witnesses at any drift state without re-deriving them.  The observable quotient layer is closed.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Pointwise quotient presentation for an admissible rational observable.
record RationalObservablePresentation (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) : SetПү where
  field
    presented-num : DriftState вҶ’ в„Ө
    presented-den : DriftState вҶ’ в„•вҒә
    presents-observable :
      (s : DriftState) вҶ’
      lowerв„ҡ (observe a s) вүЎ presented-num s / presented-den s

-- В§ Canonical pointwise presentation reads off the quotient fields directly.
canonical-rational-observable-presentation :
  (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) вҶ’ RationalObservablePresentation a
canonical-rational-observable-presentation a = record
  { presented-num = О» s вҶ’ num (lowerв„ҡ (observe a s))
  ; presented-den = О» s вҶ’ den (lowerв„ҡ (observe a s))
  ; presents-observable = О» s вҶ’ refl
  }

-- В§ Pointwise quotient fields are unique for every admissible rational observable.
observable-quotient-fields-unique :
  (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) вҶ’
  (PвӮҒ PвӮӮ : RationalObservablePresentation a) вҶ’
  (s : DriftState) вҶ’
  RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num PвӮҒ s вүЎ RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num PвӮӮ s
  Г— RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den PвӮҒ s вүЎ RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den PвӮӮ s
observable-quotient-fields-unique a PвӮҒ PвӮӮ s =
  rational-fields-unique (lowerв„ҡ (observe a s))
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num PвӮҒ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-num PвӮӮ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den PвӮҒ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presented-den PвӮӮ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presents-observable PвӮҒ s)
    (RationalObservablePresentation.presents-observable PвӮӮ s)

-- В§ Pointwise reduced witnesses on an admissible rational observable.
record ReducedRationalObservable (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) : SetПү where
  field
    reduced-at : (s : DriftState) вҶ’ ReducedRationalWitness (lowerв„ҡ (observe a s))

-- В§ Pointwise reduced witnesses are unique whenever they exist.
reduced-rational-observable-unique :
  (a : AdmissibleRationalObservable) вҶ’
  (RвӮҒ RвӮӮ : ReducedRationalObservable a) вҶ’
  (s : DriftState) вҶ’
  ReducedRationalWitness.red-num (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at RвӮҒ s)
    вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-num (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at RвӮӮ s)
  Г— ReducedRationalWitness.red-den (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at RвӮҒ s)
    вүЎ ReducedRationalWitness.red-den (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at RвӮӮ s)
reduced-rational-observable-unique a RвӮҒ RвӮӮ s =
  reduced-rational-witness-unique
    (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at RвӮҒ s)
    (ReducedRationalObservable.reduced-at RвӮӮ s)
\end{code}


%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
\chapter{K\textsubscript{4} Forced Constants Layer}
\label{ch:k4-unit-constants}
%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

Once the simplex cardinality is fixed at $V = 4$, every combinatorial constant attached to $K_4$ is already determined. This chapter eliminates parameter freedom by computing edge counts, face counts, spectral constants, and cycle data directly from that forced cardinality, with each law collapsing to definitional equality.

That rigidity must not be overstated. Two different kinds of sentence appear in what follows, and they answer to different standards.

The first kind is theorem. A theorem here is an internal statement of the formal development: a count, spectrum, or equality that Agda closes by reduction. When this chapter proves that a quantity on $K_4$ is $4$, or $6$, or $137$, the claim is purely structural. It says only that these numbers are forced by the previously derived object.

The second kind is interpretation. Interpretation begins only when one asks whether a formally forced invariant should be read as a quantity in an external theory. That move is not part of the theorem. It is a separate act of comparison between the formal survivor and an external framework.

\textbf{Constraint:} The numerology objection gains force precisely when theorem and interpretation are allowed to blur. If a computed invariant is described as externally significant before the formal status of the computation is fixed, the reader can rightly suspect that the target meaning guided the construction.

\textbf{Construction:} Any methodology that blurs the boundary between theorem and interpretation is eliminated: it reintroduces the numerology objection. The only surviving discipline keeps the registers separate. First the invariant is computed as a theorem internal to the $K_4$ record. Only afterward may a companion volume state the possible external reading of that already-fixed value. In this chapter, proofs close in the first register; external comparisons belong to \textit{Form}.

\textbf{Consequence:} The formal content of the chapter stands or falls independently of any downstream interpretation. If an interpretation later fails, the theorem remains proved. If an interpretation later succeeds, it succeeds because it reads a pre-existing invariant rather than because the invariant was engineered to fit it.

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Simplex invariants}
\label{sec:k4-constants:simplex-invariants}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The first layer fixes the bare tetrahedral invariants forced by $V = 4$. Edge count, face count, degree, and Euler characteristic are not introduced independently; each is the unique value surviving the standard simplex formulas.

\phantomsection\label{law:k4:simplex-degree-match}

\begin{code}
-- В§ Vertex count of KвӮ„ (= simplex-vertices from Tier 5)
vertexCountK4 : в„•
vertexCountK4 = simplex-vertices

-- В§ Edge count: E = V(VвҲ’1)/2
edgeCountK4 : в„•
edgeCountK4 = (vertexCountK4 * (vertexCountK4 вҲё 1)) divв„• 2

-- В§ Face count: F = V(VвҲ’1)(VвҲ’2)/6
faceCountK4 : в„•
faceCountK4 = (vertexCountK4 * (vertexCountK4 вҲё 1) * (vertexCountK4 вҲё 2)) divв„• 6

-- В§ Vertex degree: deg = V вҲ’ 1
degree-K4 : в„•
degree-K4 = vertexCountK4 вҲё 1

-- В§ Euler characteristic: ПҮ = V + F вҲ’ E
eulerChar-computed : в„•
eulerChar-computed = (vertexCountK4 + faceCountK4) вҲё edgeCountK4

-- В§ Law 16B-0: E = 6
law16B-0-edges : edgeCountK4 вүЎ 6
law16B-0-edges = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-1: F = 4
law16B-1-faces : faceCountK4 вүЎ 4
law16B-1-faces = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-2: deg = 3
law16B-2-degree : degree-K4 вүЎ 3
law16B-2-degree = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-3: ПҮ = 2
law16B-3-euler : eulerChar-computed вүЎ 2
law16B-3-euler = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-4: edge count matches simplex definition
law16B-4-edges-match : edgeCountK4 вүЎ simplex-edges
law16B-4-edges-match = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-5: degree matches simplex definition
law16B-5-degree-match : degree-K4 вүЎ simplex-degree
law16B-5-degree-match = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-6: Euler characteristic matches simplex definition
law16B-6-euler-match : eulerChar-computed вүЎ simplex-chi
law16B-6-euler-match = refl
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Clifford and spectral constants}
\label{sec:k4-constants:clifford-spectral-constants}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

These constants are forced from the same combinatorial core, so no separate parameter enters. Clifford dimension, hierarchy exponent, and the discrete evaluation data are computed as rigid consequences of the already fixed $K_4$ profile.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Clifford algebra dimension: 2^V
clifford-dimension : в„•
clifford-dimension = 2 ^ vertexCountK4

-- В§ Law 16B-7: Cl(в„қ^4) has dimension 16
law16B-7-clifford : clifford-dimension вүЎ 16
law16B-7-clifford = refl

-- В§ Clifford mode count (alias)
clifford-modes : в„•
clifford-modes = clifford-dimension

-- В§ Fermat-like combinatorial strata
FвӮӮ : в„•
FвӮӮ = suc clifford-modes

FвӮғ : в„•
FвӮғ = suc (clifford-modes * clifford-modes)

-- В§ Discrete Оә: spectral width Г— 2
Оә-discrete : в„•
Оә-discrete = 2 * (degree-K4 + 1)

-- В§ Law 16B-8: Оә = 8
law16B-8-kappa : Оә-discrete вүЎ 8
law16B-8-kappa = refl

-- В§ Hierarchy exponent: VВ·E вҲ’ ПҮ
hierarchy-exponent : в„•
hierarchy-exponent = vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4 вҲё eulerChar-computed

-- В§ Law 16B-9: hierarchy exponent = 22
law16B-9-hierarchy : hierarchy-exponent вүЎ 22
law16B-9-hierarchy = refl

-- В§ Alpha denominator: deg В· (1 + EВІ)
simplex-denom-K4 : в„•
simplex-denom-K4 = degree-K4 * suc (edgeCountK4 * edgeCountK4)

-- В§ Law 16B-10: simplex denominator = 111
law16B-10-simplex-denom : simplex-denom-K4 вүЎ 111
law16B-10-simplex-denom = refl

-- В§ Edge pair count: EВІ
EdgePairCount-early : в„•
EdgePairCount-early = edgeCountK4 * edgeCountK4

-- В§ Law 16B-11: EВІ = 36
law16B-11-edge-pairs : EdgePairCount-early вүЎ 36
law16B-11-edge-pairs = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-12: FвӮӮ = 17
law16B-12-F2-is-17 : FвӮӮ вүЎ 17
law16B-12-F2-is-17 = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-13: FвӮғ = 257
law16B-13-F3-is-257 : FвӮғ вүЎ 257
law16B-13-F3-is-257 = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-14: compactification triple (5, 17, 37)
law16B-14-compactification-triple :
  (suc vertexCountK4 вүЎ 5) Г— ((suc clifford-dimension вүЎ 17) Г— (suc EdgePairCount-early вүЎ 37))
law16B-14-compactification-triple = refl , (refl , refl)
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{K\textsubscript{4} aliases and cycle counts}
\label{sec:k4-constants:aliases-cycle-counts}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

This layer eliminates notation drift by tying all later aliases back to the same forced counts. The cycle data then record the remaining named invariants needed downstream, again with no freedom beyond the tetrahedral combinatorics already fixed.

\begin{code}
-- В§ KвӮ„ aliases for KвӮ„ usage
K4-V K4-E K4-F K4-deg K4-chi : в„•
K4-V   = vertexCountK4
K4-E   = edgeCountK4
K4-F   = faceCountK4
K4-deg = degree-K4
K4-chi = eulerChar-computed

-- В§ Redundant aliases for KвӮ„ invariant fields
KвӮ„-vertices-count KвӮ„-edges-count KвӮ„-degree-count KвӮ„-triangles : в„•
KвӮ„-vertices-count = vertexCountK4
KвӮ„-edges-count    = edgeCountK4
KвӮ„-degree-count   = degree-K4
KвӮ„-triangles      = faceCountK4

-- В§ Simplex evaluation alias for KвӮ„
simplex-eval-K4 : в„•
simplex-eval-K4 = simplex-eval

-- В§ Law 16B-15: simplex evaluation = 137
law16B-15-simplex-eval-bare : simplex-eval-K4 вүЎ 137
law16B-15-simplex-eval-bare = refl

-- В§ Law 16B-15a: ПҮ is the unique minimum of the KвӮ„ invariant basis.
-- The non-trivial invariants are {ПҮ=2, d=3, V=F=4, E=6, Оә=8}.
-- ПҮ is strictly below every other member.
law-chi-below-deg : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-deg
law-chi-below-deg = sвүӨs (sвүӨs (sвүӨs zвүӨn))               -- 3 вүӨ 3

law-chi-below-V : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-V
law-chi-below-V = sвүӨs (sвүӨs (sвүӨs zвүӨn))                  -- 3 вүӨ 4

law-chi-below-F : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-F
law-chi-below-F = sвүӨs (sвүӨs (sвүӨs zвүӨn))                  -- 3 вүӨ 4
