law-chi-below-E : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-E
law-chi-below-E = sвүӨs (sвүӨs (sвүӨs zвүӨn))                  -- 3 вүӨ 6

law-chi-below-Оә : suc K4-chi вүӨ Оә-discrete
law-chi-below-Оә = sвүӨs (sвүӨs (sвүӨs zвүӨn))                  -- 3 вүӨ 8

-- В§ ПҮ is non-trivial: ПҮ > 1.
law-chi-nontrivial : suc 1 вүӨ K4-chi
law-chi-nontrivial = sвүӨs (sвүӨs zвүӨn)                     -- 2 вүӨ 2

-- В§ Combined: ПҮ is the unique smallest non-trivial KвӮ„ invariant.
-- Any Aut(KвӮ„)-invariant ratio n/CВІ with n from the basis satisfies n вүҘ ПҮ.
-- Therefore ПҮ/CВІ is the minimal such ratio.
record ChiMinimality : Set where
  field
    chi-nontrivial : suc 1 вүӨ K4-chi
    chi-below-deg  : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-deg
    chi-below-V    : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-V
    chi-below-F    : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-F
    chi-below-E    : suc K4-chi вүӨ K4-E
    chi-below-Оә    : suc K4-chi вүӨ Оә-discrete

law16B-15a-chi-minimality : ChiMinimality
law16B-15a-chi-minimality = record
  { chi-nontrivial = law-chi-nontrivial
  ; chi-below-deg  = law-chi-below-deg
  ; chi-below-V    = law-chi-below-V
  ; chi-below-F    = law-chi-below-F
  ; chi-below-E    = law-chi-below-E
  ; chi-below-Оә    = law-chi-below-Оә
  }

-- В§ Triangle and square cycle counts
count-triangles : в„•
count-triangles = simplex-vertices

count-squares : в„•
count-squares = simplex-degree

total-nontrivial-cycles : в„•
total-nontrivial-cycles = count-triangles + count-squares

-- В§ Law 16B-16: total non-trivial cycles = 7
law16B-16-total-cycles : total-nontrivial-cycles вүЎ 7
law16B-16-total-cycles = refl

-- В§ Loop and propagation orders
triangle-loop-order : в„•
triangle-loop-order = simplex-vertices вҲё simplex-degree  -- V вҲ’ d = 4 вҲ’ 3 = 1

square-loop-order : в„•
square-loop-order = simplex-vertices вҲё simplex-degree + (simplex-vertices вҲё simplex-degree)  -- 1 + 1 = 2

max-propagation-per-edge : в„•
max-propagation-per-edge = simplex-vertices вҲё simplex-degree

-- В§ Law 16B-17: max propagation per edge = 1
law16B-17-max-prop : max-propagation-per-edge вүЎ 1
law16B-17-max-prop = refl

-- В§ Lattice spacing at natural scale
lattice-spacing-natural : в„•
lattice-spacing-natural = simplex-vertices вҲё simplex-degree

-- В§ Law 16B-18: lattice spacing = 1
law16B-18-lattice-natural : lattice-spacing-natural вүЎ 1
law16B-18-lattice-natural = refl

-- В§ KвӮ… edge count for comparison
K5-total-edges : в„•
K5-total-edges = ((vertexCountK4 + 1) * vertexCountK4) divв„• 2

-- В§ Law 16B-19: KвӮ… has 10 edges
law16B-19-K5-edges : K5-total-edges вүЎ 10
law16B-19-K5-edges = refl
\end{code}

Every combinatorial constant of $K_4$ is fixed by definitional equality: vertex count, edge count, face count, degree, Euler characteristic, spectral width, and cycle data. The ledger is sealed. What remains is to show that these constants determine a unique formal representation record---that no second assignment of fields could survive the same constraints.

\paragraph{The forced invariants.}
At the theorem level, $K_4$ determines $V = 4$, $E = 6$, an eigenvalue ratio $3{:}1$, and the graph evaluation $\mathcal{C} = 137$. None of these numbers were adjusted. Each is a definitional equality closed by the Agda type checker. Whether these values carry any downstream identification belongs to the companion volume. What remains is the uniqueness proof: whether any two admissible representations must agree on every field, or whether a rival could survive the same constraints. That proof is Chapter~\ref{ch:k4-representation-theory}. The canonicity of $\mathcal{C} = 137$ as a derived invariant---including the proof that no alternative degree-$\le 3$ formula reaches the same prime---is in the companion volume \textit{Form}.

%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
\chapter{K\textsubscript{4} Representation Theory}
\label{ch:k4-representation-theory}
%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

The admissible invariant representation of $K_4$ must be unique: otherwise the passage from graph invariants to represented quantities would reintroduce contingency. This chapter forces uniqueness by anchoring one field to the simplex cardinality and chaining every remaining field to previously fixed invariants until no representational freedom survives. The result---that every admissible record collapses to a single canonical object---is one of the five distinctive conclusions of the derivation. It is not merely that $K_4$ exists, or that its invariants are determined, but that the invariant record is a \emph{singleton}: any two inhabitants of the record type must agree on every field.

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Structural infrastructure}
\label{sec:k4-representation:structural-infrastructure}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The infrastructure isolates the raw objects on which representation theory acts: vertices, basis states, adjacency, and neighbor summation. Nothing interpretive happens here; the section fixes the combinatorial carrier that every later representation claim must respect.

\begin{code}
-- В§ KвӮ„ vertex type
data K4Vertex : Set where
  vвӮҖ vвӮҒ vвӮӮ vвӮғ : K4Vertex

-- В§ Spacetime index type
data SpacetimeIndex : Set where
  П„-idx x-idx y-idx z-idx : SpacetimeIndex

-- В§ KвӮ„ state: assignment of в„• to each vertex
K4State : Set
K4State = K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•

-- В§ KвӮ„ adjacency (complete graph minus diagonal)
adjacent : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•
adjacent vвӮҖ vвӮҖ = 0
adjacent vвӮҖ _  = 1
adjacent vвӮҒ vвӮҒ = 0
adjacent vвӮҒ _  = 1
adjacent vвӮӮ vвӮӮ = 0
adjacent vвӮӮ _  = 1
adjacent vвӮғ vвӮғ = 0
adjacent vвӮғ _  = 1

-- В§ Kronecker delta on KвӮ„
Оҙ : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•
Оҙ vвӮҖ vвӮҖ = 1
Оҙ vвӮҖ _  = 0
Оҙ vвӮҒ vвӮҒ = 1
Оҙ vвӮҒ _  = 0
Оҙ vвӮӮ vвӮӮ = 1
Оҙ vвӮӮ _  = 0
Оҙ vвӮғ vвӮғ = 1
Оҙ vвӮғ _  = 0

-- В§ Basis states (= Kronecker delta)
K4-basis : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4State
K4-basis = Оҙ

-- В§ Sum of neighbor values (graph Laplacian action)
sum-neighbors : K4State вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•
sum-neighbors ПҲ v = adjacent v vвӮҖ * ПҲ vвӮҖ + adjacent v vвӮҒ * ПҲ vвӮҒ
                  + adjacent v vвӮӮ * ПҲ vвӮӮ + adjacent v vвӮғ * ПҲ vвӮғ
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Vertex-invariance laws}
\label{sec:k4-representation:vertex-invariance-laws}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

$K_4$ is vertex-transitive, so any observable defined purely from adjacency data must ignore the choice of vertex label. These laws remove label dependence before any downstream interpretation is attached.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Adjacency row sum equals vertex degree
law-adjacency-degree : (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’
  adjacent v vвӮҖ + adjacent v vвӮҒ + adjacent v vвӮӮ + adjacent v vвӮғ вүЎ degree-K4
law-adjacency-degree vвӮҖ = refl
law-adjacency-degree vвӮҒ = refl
law-adjacency-degree vвӮӮ = refl
law-adjacency-degree vвӮғ = refl

-- В§ Each basis state is normalized (total weight 1)
law-basis-normalized : (u : K4Vertex) вҶ’
  K4-basis u vвӮҖ + K4-basis u vвӮҒ + K4-basis u vвӮӮ + K4-basis u vвӮғ вүЎ 1
law-basis-normalized vвӮҖ = refl
law-basis-normalized vвӮҒ = refl
law-basis-normalized vвӮӮ = refl
law-basis-normalized vвӮғ = refl

-- В§ Basis propagation equals adjacency (16 cases)
law-basis-spreads : (u v : K4Vertex) вҶ’
  sum-neighbors (K4-basis u) v вүЎ adjacent v u
law-basis-spreads vвӮҖ vвӮҖ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҖ vвӮҒ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҖ vвӮӮ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҖ vвӮғ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҒ vвӮҖ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҒ vвӮҒ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҒ vвӮӮ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮҒ vвӮғ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮӮ vвӮҖ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮӮ vвӮҒ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮӮ vвӮӮ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮӮ vвӮғ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮғ vвӮҖ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮғ vвӮҒ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮғ vвӮӮ = refl
law-basis-spreads vвӮғ vвӮғ = refl
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{The representation record}
\label{sec:k4-representation:representation-record}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

A invariant representation that floats freely above its graph would reintroduce contingency at the worst possible moment: after every graph invariant has been locked down.  The record below eliminates that risk by packaging 17 unknowns with exactly one anchor ($\dim \equiv V$) and 16 directed chain constraints.  Once the anchor connects to $K_4$, every remaining field is forced to exactly one value through a rigid directed chain: $\dim$ fixes $n\text{-spatial}$, which fixes $n\text{-temporal}$, which fixes every subsequent field.

\textbf{Constraint:} Every record field must be uniquely determined by the $K_4$ anchor; no field may carry independent freedom.

\textbf{Construction:} Any record with fewer than 17 fields fails to cover the invariant chain; any record with an anchor weaker than $\dim \equiv \texttt{vertexCountK4}$ leaves representational freedom alive. The only surviving structure packages 17 fields with 1~anchor ($\dim \equiv \texttt{vertexCountK4}$) and 16~chained equalities.  Each equality pins one field to a formula in previously fixed fields.

\textbf{Consequence:} The record is rigid: any admissible inhabitant is fully determined by the anchor alone.  This is proved in the sections that follow.

\begin{code}
-- В§ The KвӮ„ invariant representation: 17 unknowns + 1 anchor + 16 chain constraints
record K4Record : Set where
  field
    dim          : в„•     -- total simplex dimension (V)
    n-spatial    : в„•     -- codimension-1 count (V вҲё 1)
    n-temporal   : в„•     -- residual dimension (V вҲё n-spatial)
    edge-count   : в„•     -- edge count (E)
    face-count   : в„•     -- 2-simplex count (F)
    euler        : в„•     -- Euler characteristic (V + F вҲё E)
    graph-eval : в„•     -- graph evaluation (V^d В· ПҮ + dВІ)
    codim        : в„•     -- codimension count (= d)
    clifford-dim   : в„•     -- Clifford dimension (2^V)
    hierarchy    : в„•     -- hierarchy exponent (VВ·E вҲё ПҮ)
    auto-count   : в„•     -- automorphism count (|S_V|)
    bell-sq      : в„•     -- squared Bell number (VВ·ПҮ)
    norm-index      : в„•     -- normalization index (= V)
    frac-num   : в„•     -- fraction numerator (= n-temporal)
    frac-den   : в„•     -- fraction denominator (= E)
    res-index    : в„•     -- UV cutoff scale
    min-loop     : в„•     -- minimal loop order

    -- The anchor: exactly ONE connection to KвӮ„
    anchor : dim вүЎ vertexCountK4

    -- Graph structure chain (from complete graph K_dim)
    cg-deg      : n-spatial вүЎ dim вҲё 1
    cg-temporal : n-temporal вүЎ dim вҲё n-spatial
    cg-edges    : edge-count вүЎ (dim * n-spatial) divв„• 2
    cg-faces    : face-count вүЎ dim
    cg-euler    : euler вүЎ (dim + face-count) вҲё edge-count

    -- Structural chain (invariant correspondences)
    ph-clifford   : clifford-dim вүЎ 2 ^ dim
    ph-eval : graph-eval вүЎ (dim ^ n-spatial) * euler + n-spatial * n-spatial
    ph-codim      : codim вүЎ n-spatial
    ph-hierarchy : hierarchy вүЎ dim * edge-count вҲё euler
    ph-auto     : auto-count вүЎ dim * n-spatial * (n-spatial вҲё 1) * (n-spatial вҲё 2)
    ph-bell     : bell-sq вүЎ dim * euler
    ph-norm       : norm-index вүЎ dim
    ph-res       : res-index вүЎ n-temporal
    ph-loop     : min-loop вүЎ n-temporal
    ph-frac-num : frac-num вүЎ n-temporal
    ph-frac-den : frac-den вүЎ edge-count
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Existence: the canonical witness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:existence-canonical-witness}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

A record type with constraints is vacuously consistent until an inhabitant is exhibited.  Without a witness, the forcing chain is a theorem about nothing.  Existence is witnessed by the canonical assignment obtained from the forced constants. Each proof closes by \texttt{refl} because the assigned values are not guesses; they are exactly the normal forms already determined by the constraint chain.

\textbf{Constraint:} The 17~fields and 16~chained equalities must be simultaneously satisfiable.

\textbf{Construction:} Each field is assigned its forced value (4, 3, 1, 6, 4, 2, 137, \ldots) and every constraint proof closes by \texttt{refl}---no alternative assignment survives the chain.

\textbf{Consequence:} The representation is inhabited.  Combined with uniqueness, this makes it a singleton.

\begin{code}
-- В§ The canonical KвӮ„ invariant representation
canonical-rep : K4Record
canonical-rep = record
  { dim          = 4
  ; n-spatial    = 3
  ; n-temporal   = 1
  ; edge-count   = 6
  ; face-count   = 4
  ; euler        = 2
  ; graph-eval = 137
  ; codim        = 3
  ; clifford-dim   = 16
  ; hierarchy    = 22
  ; auto-count   = 24
  ; bell-sq      = 8
  ; norm-index      = 4
  ; frac-num   = 1
  ; frac-den   = 6
  ; res-index    = 1
  ; min-loop     = 1
  ; anchor       = refl   -- 4 вүЎ vertexCountK4
  ; cg-deg       = refl   -- 3 вүЎ 4 вҲё 1
  ; cg-temporal  = refl   -- 1 вүЎ 4 вҲё 3
  ; cg-edges     = refl   -- 6 вүЎ (4 * 3) divв„• 2
  ; cg-faces     = refl   -- 4 вүЎ 4
  ; cg-euler     = refl   -- 2 вүЎ (4 + 4) вҲё 6
  ; ph-clifford    = refl   -- 16 вүЎ 2вҒҙ
  ; ph-eval  = refl   -- 137 вүЎ 4ВіВ·2 + 3ВІ
  ; ph-codim       = refl   -- 3 вүЎ 3
  ; ph-hierarchy = refl   -- 22 вүЎ 4В·6 вҲё 2
  ; ph-auto      = refl   -- 24 вүЎ 4В·3В·2В·1
  ; ph-bell      = refl   -- 8 вүЎ 4В·2
  ; ph-norm        = refl   -- 4 вүЎ 4
  ; ph-res        = refl   -- 1 вүЎ 1
  ; ph-loop      = refl   -- 1 вүЎ 1
  ; ph-frac-num = refl  -- 1 вүЎ 1
  ; ph-frac-den = refl  -- 6 вүЎ 6
  }
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Determination: the forcing chain resolves every field}
\label{sec:k4-representation:determination-forcing-chain}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

Existence shows the record is inhabited.  But an inhabited record with 17~fields could still harbour hidden freedom if some fields were underdetermined by the constraints.  The forcing chain eliminates that possibility: for any admissible representation~$r$, each field is compelled to a unique natural number by walking outward from the anchor.

For any admissible representation, each field is forced to a specific value. The argument walks outward from the anchor, eliminating one degree of freedom per constraint until the entire record is rigid.

\textbf{Constraint:} Every field of every admissible representation must evaluate to the same number; otherwise representational freedom survives.

\textbf{Construction:} Start from $\dim \equiv 4$ (the anchor).  Each subsequent field is forced by eliminating every alternative: $n\text{-spatial} \equiv 3$ via \texttt{cg-deg}, then $n\text{-temporal} \equiv 1$ via \texttt{cg-temporal}, and so on through all 17~fields. No degree of freedom survives the chain.

\textbf{Consequence:} The representation is not just inhabited but rigid.  Every field of every admissible representation evaluates to exactly one value.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Forcing chain: every field is determined by the constraints
module ForcedValues (r : K4Record) where
  open K4Record r

  dim-is-4 : dim вүЎ 4
  dim-is-4 = anchor

  spatial-is-3 : n-spatial вүЎ 3
  spatial-is-3 = trans cg-deg (cong (О» d вҶ’ d вҲё 1) dim-is-4)

  temporal-is-1 : n-temporal вүЎ 1
  temporal-is-1 = trans cg-temporal
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ d вҲё n-spatial) dim-is-4)
           (cong (О» s вҶ’ 4 вҲё s) spatial-is-3))

  edge-is-6 : edge-count вүЎ 6
  edge-is-6 = trans cg-edges
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ (d * n-spatial) divв„• 2) dim-is-4)
           (cong (О» s вҶ’ (4 * s) divв„• 2) spatial-is-3))

  faces-is-4 : face-count вүЎ 4
  faces-is-4 = trans cg-faces dim-is-4

  euler-is-2 : euler вүЎ 2
  euler-is-2 = trans cg-euler
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ (d + face-count) вҲё edge-count) dim-is-4)
    (trans (cong (О» f вҶ’ (4 + f) вҲё edge-count) faces-is-4)
           (cong (О» g вҶ’ 8 вҲё g) edge-is-6)))

  clifford-is-16 : clifford-dim вүЎ 16
  clifford-is-16 = trans ph-clifford (cong (О» d вҶ’ 2 ^ d) dim-is-4)

  eval-is-137 : graph-eval вүЎ 137
  eval-is-137 = trans ph-eval
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ (d ^ n-spatial) * euler + n-spatial * n-spatial) dim-is-4)
    (trans (cong (О» s вҶ’ (4 ^ s) * euler + s * s) spatial-is-3)
           (cong (О» e вҶ’ (4 ^ 3) * e + 3 * 3) euler-is-2)))

  codim-is-3 : codim вүЎ 3
  codim-is-3 = trans ph-codim spatial-is-3

  hierarchy-is-22 : hierarchy вүЎ 22
  hierarchy-is-22 = trans ph-hierarchy
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ d * edge-count вҲё euler) dim-is-4)
    (trans (cong (О» g вҶ’ 4 * g вҲё euler) edge-is-6)
           (cong (О» e вҶ’ 24 вҲё e) euler-is-2)))

  auto-is-24 : auto-count вүЎ 24
  auto-is-24 = trans ph-auto
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ d * n-spatial * (n-spatial вҲё 1) * (n-spatial вҲё 2)) dim-is-4)
           (cong (О» s вҶ’ 4 * s * (s вҲё 1) * (s вҲё 2)) spatial-is-3))

  bell-is-8 : bell-sq вүЎ 8
  bell-is-8 = trans ph-bell
    (trans (cong (О» d вҶ’ d * euler) dim-is-4)
           (cong (О» e вҶ’ 4 * e) euler-is-2))

  norm-is-4 : norm-index вүЎ 4
  norm-is-4 = trans ph-norm dim-is-4

  res-is-1 : res-index вүЎ 1
  res-is-1 = trans ph-res temporal-is-1

  loop-is-1 : min-loop вүЎ 1
  loop-is-1 = trans ph-loop temporal-is-1

  frac-num-is-1 : frac-num вүЎ 1
  frac-num-is-1 = trans ph-frac-num temporal-is-1

  frac-den-is-6 : frac-den вүЎ 6
  frac-den-is-6 = trans ph-frac-den edge-is-6
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Uniqueness: any two representations agree}
\label{sec:k4-representation:uniqueness}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

Existence alone leaves a lethal gap: if two distinct representations of $K_4$ could coexist, the derived invariants would depend on which representation was chosen, and no structural criterion could distinguish them.  Uniqueness closes that gap.  Since \texttt{ForcedValues} pins every field of every admissible representation to the same normal form, any two representations must agree field by field.  Singletonhood is therefore proved, not declared.

\textbf{Constraint:} Two admissible representations $r_1, r_2$ must agree on every field, or representational contingency survives.

\textbf{Construction:} For each field, chain $F_1.\text{field-is-}k$ with $\text{sym}\;F_2.\text{field-is-}k$ to eliminate any divergence between $r_1.\text{field}$ and $r_2.\text{field}$.

\textbf{Consequence:} The invariant representation of $K_4$ is a singleton.  No alternative survives.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Uniqueness: any two K4Record agree on all 17 fields
module RepUniqueness (rвӮҒ rвӮӮ : K4Record) where
  private
    module FвӮҒ = ForcedValues rвӮҒ
    module FвӮӮ = ForcedValues rвӮӮ
  open K4Record

  dim-вүЎ          : dim rвӮҒ вүЎ dim rвӮӮ
  dim-вүЎ          = trans FвӮҒ.dim-is-4 (sym FвӮӮ.dim-is-4)

  spatial-вүЎ      : n-spatial rвӮҒ вүЎ n-spatial rвӮӮ
  spatial-вүЎ      = trans FвӮҒ.spatial-is-3 (sym FвӮӮ.spatial-is-3)

  temporal-вүЎ     : n-temporal rвӮҒ вүЎ n-temporal rвӮӮ
  temporal-вүЎ     = trans FвӮҒ.temporal-is-1 (sym FвӮӮ.temporal-is-1)

  edge-вүЎ        : edge-count rвӮҒ вүЎ edge-count rвӮӮ
  edge-вүЎ        = trans FвӮҒ.edge-is-6 (sym FвӮӮ.edge-is-6)

  faces-вүЎ        : face-count rвӮҒ вүЎ face-count rвӮӮ
  faces-вүЎ        = trans FвӮҒ.faces-is-4 (sym FвӮӮ.faces-is-4)

  euler-вүЎ        : euler rвӮҒ вүЎ euler rвӮӮ
  euler-вүЎ        = trans FвӮҒ.euler-is-2 (sym FвӮӮ.euler-is-2)

  eval-вүЎ     : graph-eval rвӮҒ вүЎ graph-eval rвӮӮ
  eval-вүЎ     = trans FвӮҒ.eval-is-137 (sym FвӮӮ.eval-is-137)

  codim-вүЎ          : codim rвӮҒ вүЎ codim rвӮӮ
  codim-вүЎ          = trans FвӮҒ.codim-is-3 (sym FвӮӮ.codim-is-3)

  clifford-вүЎ       : clifford-dim rвӮҒ вүЎ clifford-dim rвӮӮ
  clifford-вүЎ       = trans FвӮҒ.clifford-is-16 (sym FвӮӮ.clifford-is-16)

  hierarchy-вүЎ    : hierarchy rвӮҒ вүЎ hierarchy rвӮӮ
  hierarchy-вүЎ    = trans FвӮҒ.hierarchy-is-22 (sym FвӮӮ.hierarchy-is-22)

  auto-вүЎ         : auto-count rвӮҒ вүЎ auto-count rвӮӮ
  auto-вүЎ         = trans FвӮҒ.auto-is-24 (sym FвӮӮ.auto-is-24)

  bell-вүЎ         : bell-sq rвӮҒ вүЎ bell-sq rвӮӮ
  bell-вүЎ         = trans FвӮҒ.bell-is-8 (sym FвӮӮ.bell-is-8)

  norm-вүЎ           : norm-index rвӮҒ вүЎ norm-index rвӮӮ
  norm-вүЎ           = trans FвӮҒ.norm-is-4 (sym FвӮӮ.norm-is-4)

  res-вүЎ           : res-index rвӮҒ вүЎ res-index rвӮӮ
  res-вүЎ           = trans FвӮҒ.res-is-1 (sym FвӮӮ.res-is-1)

  loop-вүЎ         : min-loop rвӮҒ вүЎ min-loop rвӮӮ
  loop-вүЎ         = trans FвӮҒ.loop-is-1 (sym FвӮӮ.loop-is-1)

  frac-num-вүЎ   : frac-num rвӮҒ вүЎ frac-num rвӮӮ
  frac-num-вүЎ   = trans FвӮҒ.frac-num-is-1 (sym FвӮӮ.frac-num-is-1)

  frac-den-вүЎ   : frac-den rвӮҒ вүЎ frac-den rвӮӮ
  frac-den-вүЎ   = trans FвӮҒ.frac-den-is-6 (sym FвӮӮ.frac-den-is-6)
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Singleton: the representation has exactly one inhabitant}
\label{sec:k4-representation:singleton}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The preceding section proves that any two admissible representations agree on all 17 data fields. But data-field agreement is not yet record equality. The record \texttt{K4Record} also carries 16 proof fields---each of type $n \equiv m$ for specific natural numbers. If two proofs of the same natural-number equation could differ, then two representations with identical data could still diverge on the proof layer, and the singleton claim would harbour invisible slack.

Under \texttt{--without-K}, uniqueness of identity proofs is not given for free. It must be derived. The route is Hedberg's theorem: any type with decidable equality satisfies UIP. Natural numbers have decidable equality by induction. Therefore every proof $p : m \equiv n$ on $\mathbb{N}$ is uniquely determined. Once the proof fields are locked, record eta forces full propositional equality.

\textbf{Constraint:} If two proofs of the same $\mathbb{N}$-equation could differ, the representation would fork at the proof layer even after the data layer is fixed.

\textbf{Elimination:} Decidable equality on $\mathbb{N}$ by mutual recursion eliminates undecidable cases. Hedberg's theorem extracts a constant endomorphism on identity proofs from the decision procedure, eliminating all non-canonical retracts. Corollary: $\mathbb{N}$-UIP---no two distinct proofs of the same equation survive. Record-level singleton: transport every data field of an arbitrary representation to its forced value using \texttt{ForcedValues}, apply $\mathbb{N}$-UIP to eliminate all proof-field divergence, close by record eta.

\textbf{Consequence:} The invariant record of $K_4$ has propositionally exactly one inhabitant. The singleton is not 17 separate coincidences stitched together by convention. It is a single theorem: \texttt{K4Record-singleton}.

\begin{code}
-- В§в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
-- В§ Decidable equality on в„•
-- В§в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

data Decв„• (A : Set) : Set where
  decided-yes : A   вҶ’ Decв„• A
  decided-no  : В¬ A вҶ’ Decв„• A

_вүҹв„•_ : (m n : в„•) вҶ’ Decв„• (m вүЎ n)
zero  вүҹв„• zero  = decided-yes refl
zero  вүҹв„• suc n = decided-no (О» ())
suc m вүҹв„• zero  = decided-no (О» ())
suc m вүҹв„• suc n with m вүҹв„• n
... | decided-yes refl = decided-yes refl
... | decided-no  mвүўn  = decided-no (О» p вҶ’ mвүўn (suc-injective p))

-- В§в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
-- В§ Hedberg's theorem for в„•
-- В§в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

-- В§ A constant endomorphism on identity proofs: the decision procedure
-- В§ always picks the same canonical witness regardless of input.
private
  в„•-canonical : {m n : в„•} вҶ’ m вүЎ n вҶ’ m вүЎ n
  в„•-canonical {m} {n} p with m вүҹв„• n
  ... | decided-yes c = c
  ... | decided-no  f = вҠҘ-elim (f p)

  в„•-canonical-const : {m n : в„•} (p q : m вүЎ n) вҶ’ в„•-canonical p вүЎ в„•-canonical q
  в„•-canonical-const {m} {n} p q with m вүҹв„• n
  ... | decided-yes _ = refl
  ... | decided-no  f = вҠҘ-elim (f p)

  -- В§ General cancellation: trans (sym p) p reduces to refl.
  trans-sym-cancel : {m n : в„•} (p : m вүЎ n) вҶ’ trans (sym p) p вүЎ refl
  trans-sym-cancel refl = refl

  -- В§ Cancel: any proof p is equal to the canonical one composed with a correction.
  в„•-cancel : {m n : в„•} (p : m вүЎ n) вҶ’
    p вүЎ trans (sym (в„•-canonical refl)) (в„•-canonical p)
  в„•-cancel refl = sym (trans-sym-cancel (в„•-canonical refl))

-- В§ Hedberg: any two proofs of m вүЎ n in в„• are equal.
в„•-UIP : {m n : в„•} (p q : m вүЎ n) вҶ’ p вүЎ q
в„•-UIP p q =
  trans (в„•-cancel p)
    (trans (cong (trans (sym (в„•-canonical refl))) (в„•-canonical-const p q))
           (sym (в„•-cancel q)))

-- В§в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
-- В§ K4Record singleton
-- В§в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

-- В§ Every admissible representation equals the canonical one.
-- В§ Strategy: decompose the record into data fields + proof fields.
-- В§ Pattern-match on forced-value equalities (unifying data fields with canonical values),
-- В§ then в„•-UIP collapses every proof field to refl. Record eta closes the gap.

private
  singleton-lemma :
    вҲҖ d s t g f e c ng sp h a b bh bn bd u ml
    вҶ’ d вүЎ 4 вҶ’ s вүЎ 3 вҶ’ t вүЎ 1 вҶ’ g вүЎ 6 вҶ’ f вүЎ 4 вҶ’ e вүЎ 2
    вҶ’ c вүЎ 137 вҶ’ ng вүЎ 3 вҶ’ sp вүЎ 16 вҶ’ h вүЎ 22 вҶ’ a вүЎ 24 вҶ’ b вүЎ 8
    вҶ’ bh вүЎ 4 вҶ’ bn вүЎ 1 вҶ’ bd вүЎ 6 вҶ’ u вүЎ 1 вҶ’ ml вүЎ 1
    вҶ’ (panc  : d вүЎ vertexCountK4)      вҶ’ (pdeg  : s вүЎ d вҲё 1)
    вҶ’ (ptemp : t вүЎ d вҲё s)              вҶ’ (pedge : g вүЎ (d * s) divв„• 2)
    вҶ’ (pface : f вүЎ d)                  вҶ’ (pchi  : e вүЎ (d + f) вҲё g)
    вҶ’ (pspin : sp вүЎ 2 ^ d)             вҶ’ (pcoup : c вүЎ (d ^ s) * e + s * s)
    вҶ’ (pgen  : ng вүЎ s)                 вҶ’ (phier : h вүЎ d * g вҲё e)
    вҶ’ (pauto : a вүЎ d * s * (s вҲё 1) * (s вҲё 2))
    вҶ’ (pbell : b вүЎ d * e)              вҶ’ (pbh   : bh вүЎ d)
    вҶ’ (puv   : u вүЎ t)                  вҶ’ (ploop : ml вүЎ t)
    вҶ’ (pbnum : bn вүЎ t)                 вҶ’ (pbden : bd вүЎ g)
    вҶ’ record
        { dim = d ; n-spatial = s ; n-temporal = t ; edge-count = g
        ; face-count = f ; euler = e ; graph-eval = c ; codim = ng
        ; clifford-dim = sp ; hierarchy = h ; auto-count = a ; bell-sq = b
        ; norm-index = bh ; frac-num = bn ; frac-den = bd
        ; res-index = u ; min-loop = ml
        ; anchor = panc ; cg-deg = pdeg ; cg-temporal = ptemp
        ; cg-edges = pedge ; cg-faces = pface ; cg-euler = pchi
        ; ph-clifford = pspin ; ph-eval = pcoup ; ph-codim = pgen
        ; ph-hierarchy = phier ; ph-auto = pauto ; ph-bell = pbell
        ; ph-norm = pbh ; ph-res = puv ; ph-loop = ploop
        ; ph-frac-num = pbnum ; ph-frac-den = pbden
        } вүЎ canonical-rep
  singleton-lemma .4 .3 .1 .6 .4 .2 .137 .3 .16 .22 .24 .8 .4 .1 .6 .1 .1
    refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl refl
    panc pdeg ptemp pedge pface pchi pspin pcoup pgen phier pauto pbell pbh puv ploop pbnum pbden
    rewrite в„•-UIP panc refl  | в„•-UIP pdeg refl  | в„•-UIP ptemp refl
    | в„•-UIP pedge refl | в„•-UIP pface refl | в„•-UIP pchi refl
    | в„•-UIP pspin refl | в„•-UIP pcoup refl | в„•-UIP pgen refl
    | в„•-UIP phier refl | в„•-UIP pauto refl | в„•-UIP pbell refl
    | в„•-UIP pbh refl   | в„•-UIP puv refl   | в„•-UIP ploop refl
    | в„•-UIP pbnum refl | в„•-UIP pbden refl
    = refl

K4Record-is-canonical : (r : K4Record) вҶ’ r вүЎ canonical-rep
K4Record-is-canonical r = singleton-lemma
  dim n-spatial n-temporal edge-count face-count euler
  graph-eval codim clifford-dim hierarchy auto-count bell-sq
  norm-index frac-num frac-den res-index min-loop
  dim-is-4 spatial-is-3 temporal-is-1 edge-is-6 faces-is-4 euler-is-2
  eval-is-137 codim-is-3 clifford-is-16 hierarchy-is-22 auto-is-24 bell-is-8
  norm-is-4 frac-num-is-1 frac-den-is-6 res-is-1 loop-is-1
  anchor cg-deg cg-temporal cg-edges cg-faces cg-euler
  ph-clifford ph-eval ph-codim ph-hierarchy ph-auto ph-bell
  ph-norm ph-res ph-loop ph-frac-num ph-frac-den
  where
    open K4Record r
    open ForcedValues r

-- В§ Corollary: any two representations are propositionally equal.
K4Record-singleton : (rвӮҒ rвӮӮ : K4Record) вҶ’ rвӮҒ вүЎ rвӮӮ
K4Record-singleton rвӮҒ rвӮӮ =
  trans (K4Record-is-canonical rвӮҒ) (sym (K4Record-is-canonical rвӮӮ))
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Cross-constraints: inter-layer consistency}
\label{sec:k4-representation:cross-constraints}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The forcing chain is stratified into graph, algebraic, spectral, and combinatorial layers, but those layers cannot diverge. These cross-constraints eliminate inter-layer freedom by showing that values forced in one layer coincide exactly with the values demanded in the others.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Cross-layer bridges between forced values
module CrossConstraints where
  open K4Record

  -- В§ Spatial directions = codimension count
  cross-spatial-is-gen : n-spatial canonical-rep вүЎ codim canonical-rep
  cross-spatial-is-gen = refl

  -- В§ Bekenstein-Hawking normalization = spacetime dimension
  cross-bh-is-dim : faceCountK4 вүЎ vertexCountK4
  cross-bh-is-dim = refl

  -- В§ Loop order = UV cutoff = temporal directions
  cross-loop-is-uv : triangle-loop-order вүЎ vertexCountK4 вҲё degree-K4
  cross-loop-is-uv = refl

  -- В§ Fraction denominator = edge count
  cross-frac-is-edge : frac-den canonical-rep вүЎ edge-count canonical-rep
  cross-frac-is-edge = refl

  -- В§ Automorphism count = dim Г— edge-count (= V Г— E = 4 Г— 6 = 24)
  cross-auto-is-dim-edge :
    vertexCountK4 * degree-K4 * (degree-K4 вҲё 1) * (degree-K4 вҲё 2) вүЎ 24
  cross-auto-is-dim-edge = refl

  -- В§ Hierarchy + Euler = V В· E (22 + 2 = 24)
  cross-hierarchy-plus-euler :
    hierarchy-exponent + eulerChar-computed вүЎ
    vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4
  cross-hierarchy-plus-euler = refl
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Constraint derivation: from named invariants to canonical witness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:constraint-derivation}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

Every \texttt{K4Record} field is forced directly from named $K_4$ invariants. Bare numerals disappear from the assignment layer, and each proof closes by \texttt{refl} because the chain formulas are exactly the definitions of the named invariants they invoke.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Derivation: named invariants assemble into a representation
module ConstraintDerivation where

  -- В§ Graph-theoretic laws (KвӮ„ as complete graph)
  law-cg-deg : degree-K4 вүЎ vertexCountK4 вҲё 1
  law-cg-deg = refl

  law-cg-edges : edgeCountK4 вүЎ (vertexCountK4 * degree-K4) divв„• 2
  law-cg-edges = refl

  law-cg-faces : faceCountK4 вүЎ vertexCountK4
  law-cg-faces = refl

  law-cg-euler : eulerChar-computed вүЎ (vertexCountK4 + faceCountK4) вҲё edgeCountK4
  law-cg-euler = refl

  -- В§ Algebraic laws (representation theory)
  law-ph-clifford : clifford-dimension вүЎ 2 ^ vertexCountK4
  law-ph-clifford = refl

  law-ph-auto :
    vertexCountK4 * degree-K4 * (degree-K4 вҲё 1) * (degree-K4 вҲё 2) вүЎ 24
  law-ph-auto = refl

  -- В§ Simplex-Euler evaluation law (KвӮ„ invariant basis)
  law-ph-eval :
    simplex-eval вүЎ
    (vertexCountK4 ^ degree-K4) * eulerChar-computed + degree-K4 * degree-K4
  law-ph-eval = refl

  -- В§ Combinatorial law (KвӮ„ arithmetic)
  law-ph-hierarchy :
    hierarchy-exponent вүЎ vertexCountK4 * edgeCountK4 вҲё eulerChar-computed
  law-ph-hierarchy = refl

  -- В§ Structural assembly: K4Record from named invariants only
  derived-rep : K4Record
  derived-rep = record
    { dim          = vertexCountK4
    ; n-spatial    = degree-K4
    ; n-temporal   = vertexCountK4 вҲё degree-K4
    ; edge-count   = edgeCountK4
    ; face-count   = faceCountK4
    ; euler        = eulerChar-computed
    ; graph-eval = simplex-eval
    ; codim        = degree-K4
    ; clifford-dim   = clifford-dimension
    ; hierarchy    = hierarchy-exponent
    ; auto-count   = vertexCountK4 * degree-K4
                   * (degree-K4 вҲё 1) * (degree-K4 вҲё 2)
    ; bell-sq      = vertexCountK4 * eulerChar-computed
    ; norm-index      = vertexCountK4
    ; frac-num   = vertexCountK4 вҲё degree-K4
    ; frac-den   = edgeCountK4
    ; res-index    = vertexCountK4 вҲё degree-K4
    ; min-loop     = vertexCountK4 вҲё degree-K4
    ; anchor       = refl
    ; cg-deg       = refl
    ; cg-temporal  = refl
    ; cg-edges     = refl
    ; cg-faces     = refl
    ; cg-euler     = refl
    ; ph-clifford    = refl
    ; ph-auto      = refl
    ; ph-eval  = refl
    ; ph-hierarchy = refl
    ; ph-codim       = refl
    ; ph-bell      = refl
    ; ph-norm        = refl
    ; ph-res        = refl
    ; ph-loop      = refl
    ; ph-frac-num = refl
    ; ph-frac-den = refl
    }

  -- В§ derived-rep IS canonical-rep (definitional equality)
  derivation-is-canonical : derived-rep вүЎ canonical-rep
  derivation-is-canonical = refl
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Represented quantities as computable \texorpdfstring{$K_4$}{K4} invariants}
\label{sec:k4-representation:computable-invariants}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The formal relation between graph invariants and the representation record is exact. Each represented quantity is a computable function of $K_4$ data: the output of the graph computation is exactly the value carried by the representation chain. Whether a given represented quantity should also be read as a measured observable remains a separate interpretive question.

\subsection{Representation Completeness}
\label{sec:k4-representation:representation-completeness}

The record built below is already rigid once one enters it. But the stronger pressure comes from outside. If there were two admissible ways to inhabit the same $K_4$ invariant spine, then the constants would not be observables forced by structure; they would be artifacts of representational wording. That would collapse the claim at the final step. The graph would still survive, but the bridge into measurement would fork, and every numerical coincidence could be dismissed as notation. This fork must be shattered.

\textbf{Constraint:} Any admissible invariant representation of $K_4$ must preserve the named invariant chain already fixed: vertex count, edge count, face count, Euler characteristic, spectral data, and the derived observables assembled from them. No representational wrapper may introduce additional degrees of freedom while claiming to describe the same structure.

\textbf{Elimination:} The present chapter eliminates every alternative inhabitant of \texttt{K4Record}, proving singletonhood.
The external completeness theorem \texttt{RepresentationComplete} states: for any admissible $K_4$ representation~$r$, every observable field agrees with the canonical record.
\[
\mathrm{RepresentationComplete} :
\bigl(\text{admissible representation of }K_4\bigr)
\to
\bigl(\text{canonical }K4Record\bigr),
\]
or equivalently, that every admissible representation is canonically isomorphic to the unique record assembled from the invariant basis.

\textbf{Consequence:} With the closure theorem \texttt{RepresentationComplete} proven, the formal bridge asserts not that one chosen representation works, but that no rival admissible representation survives. Any later empirical reading must therefore attach to a unique formal record rather than to a convenient encoding chosen after the fact.

\begin{code}
-- В§ RepresentationComplete: every K4Record agrees with canonical-rep on all values.
module RepresentationComplete (r : K4Record) where
  private module F = ForcedValues r
  open K4Record

  val-dim        : dim r          вүЎ dim canonical-rep
  val-dim        = F.dim-is-4

  val-spatial    : n-spatial r    вүЎ n-spatial canonical-rep
  val-spatial    = F.spatial-is-3

  val-temporal   : n-temporal r   вүЎ n-temporal canonical-rep
  val-temporal   = F.temporal-is-1

  val-edge      : edge-count r   вүЎ edge-count canonical-rep
  val-edge      = F.edge-is-6

  val-faces      : face-count r   вүЎ face-count canonical-rep
  val-faces      = F.faces-is-4

  val-euler      : euler r        вүЎ euler canonical-rep
  val-euler      = F.euler-is-2

  val-eval   : graph-eval r вүЎ graph-eval canonical-rep
  val-eval   = F.eval-is-137

  val-codim        : codim r        вүЎ codim canonical-rep
  val-codim        = F.codim-is-3

  val-clifford     : clifford-dim r   вүЎ clifford-dim canonical-rep
  val-clifford     = F.clifford-is-16

  val-hierarchy  : hierarchy r    вүЎ hierarchy canonical-rep
  val-hierarchy  = F.hierarchy-is-22

  val-auto       : auto-count r   вүЎ auto-count canonical-rep
  val-auto       = F.auto-is-24

  val-bell       : bell-sq r      вүЎ bell-sq canonical-rep
  val-bell       = F.bell-is-8

  val-bh         : norm-index r      вүЎ norm-index canonical-rep
  val-bh         = F.norm-is-4

  val-uv         : res-index r    вүЎ res-index canonical-rep
  val-uv         = F.res-is-1

  val-loop       : min-loop r     вүЎ min-loop canonical-rep
  val-loop       = F.loop-is-1

  val-frac-num : frac-num r   вүЎ frac-num canonical-rep
  val-frac-num = F.frac-num-is-1

  val-frac-den : frac-den r   вүЎ frac-den canonical-rep
  val-frac-den = F.frac-den-is-6
\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Local graph observables}

The first block isolates the vertex-local computations: row sums, propagation directions, and the basis witness showing that the local signal really is the degree. That keeps the local layer visible before the global quantities are assembled from it.

\begin{code}
-- В§ Represented quantities as computable KвӮ„ functions
module K4Quantities where

  -- В§ Signal directions: adjacency row sum at each vertex
  signal-directions : K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•
  signal-directions v =
    adjacent v vвӮҖ + adjacent v vвӮҒ + adjacent v vвӮӮ + adjacent v vвӮғ

  -- В§ Signal is vertex-uniform and equals degree
  signal-is-degree : (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ signal-directions v вүЎ degree-K4
  signal-is-degree = law-adjacency-degree

  -- В§ Each signal propagates independently (basis property)
  signal-independent : (u v : K4Vertex) вҶ’
    sum-neighbors (K4-basis u) v вүЎ adjacent v u
  signal-independent = law-basis-spreads

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Global quantities built from the local layer}

Once the local observables are fixed, the rest of the derived quantities are short global computations. Splitting them into a second block mirrors the earlier chapters, where constructors come first and packaged consequences follow afterward.

\begin{code}

  -- В§ Boundary quanta: 2-simplex count = face count
  boundary-quanta : в„•
  boundary-quanta = faceCountK4

  -- В§ Self-duality: boundary = bulk (F = V for the tetrahedron)
  boundary-is-bulk : boundary-quanta вүЎ vertexCountK4
  boundary-is-bulk = refl

  -- В§ Spectral width: largest minus smallest adjacency eigenvalue
  spectral-width : в„•
  spectral-width = degree-K4 + 1

  -- В§ Spectral width equals vertex count
  spectral-width-is-V : spectral-width вүЎ vertexCountK4
  spectral-width-is-V = refl

  -- В§ Maximum squared correlator on the graph
  max-sq-correlator : в„•
  max-sq-correlator = spectral-width * eulerChar-computed

  -- В§ Correlator equals discrete CHSH bound
  correlator-is-Оә : max-sq-correlator вүЎ Оә-discrete
  correlator-is-Оә = refl

  -- В§ Observer directions: non-signal complement (V вҲё deg)
  observer-directions : в„•
  observer-directions = vertexCountK4 вҲё degree-K4

  -- В§ Observer direction count is 1
  observer-is-1 : observer-directions вүЎ 1
  observer-is-1 = refl

  -- В§ Same computation, three names (all V вҲё deg)
  observer-is-max-prop : observer-directions вүЎ max-propagation-per-edge
  observer-is-max-prop = refl

  observer-is-triangle : observer-directions вүЎ triangle-loop-order
  observer-is-triangle = refl

  observer-is-lattice : observer-directions вүЎ lattice-spacing-natural
  observer-is-lattice = refl

  -- В§ Interaction channels: total pairwise edges
  interaction-channels : в„•
  interaction-channels = edgeCountK4
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Observable representation and closure}
\label{sec:k4-representation:observable-representation-closure}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The representation is rebuilt entirely from computed observables. This removes the last appearance of ad hoc assignment: every field is a graph-theoretic computation on $K_4$, and every constraint reduces to \texttt{refl} because the defining formula already is the required computation.

\begin{code}
  -- В§ K4Record from computed quantities (no bare numerals)
  observable-rep : K4Record
  observable-rep = record
    { dim          = vertexCountK4
    ; n-spatial    = degree-K4
    ; n-temporal   = observer-directions
    ; edge-count   = interaction-channels
    ; face-count   = boundary-quanta
    ; euler        = eulerChar-computed
    ; graph-eval = simplex-eval
    ; codim        = degree-K4
    ; clifford-dim   = clifford-dimension
    ; hierarchy    = hierarchy-exponent
    ; auto-count   = vertexCountK4 * degree-K4
                   * (degree-K4 вҲё 1) * (degree-K4 вҲё 2)
    ; bell-sq      = max-sq-correlator
    ; norm-index      = boundary-quanta
    ; frac-num   = observer-directions
    ; frac-den   = interaction-channels
    ; res-index    = observer-directions
    ; min-loop     = observer-directions
    ; anchor       = refl
    ; cg-deg       = refl
    ; cg-temporal  = refl
    ; cg-edges     = refl
    ; cg-faces     = refl
    ; cg-euler     = refl
    ; ph-clifford    = refl
    ; ph-auto      = refl
    ; ph-eval  = refl
    ; ph-hierarchy = refl
    ; ph-codim       = refl
    ; ph-bell      = refl
    ; ph-norm        = refl
    ; ph-res        = refl
    ; ph-loop      = refl
    ; ph-frac-num = refl
    ; ph-frac-den = refl
    }

  -- В§ Closure: observable-rep IS canonical-rep (definitional equality)
  observable-is-canonical : observable-rep вүЎ canonical-rep
  observable-is-canonical = refl
\end{code}


%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{The observable principle}
\label{sec:k4-representation:observable-principle}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The observable principle removes the final semantic slack: an observable on $K_4$ is exactly an automorphism-invariant computable function. Because $K_4$ is vertex-transitive, every local observable must be uniform across vertices, while every global observable is already fixed by graph-isomorphism invariance on the named combinatorial data.

\subsubsection*{Observable types and invariance}

\begin{code}
  -- В§ KвӮ„ vertex-level invariance
  IsK4Invariant : (K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•) вҶ’ Set
  IsK4Invariant f = (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ f v вүЎ f w

  -- В§ Local observable: vertex function + uniformity proof
  LocalObservable : Set
  LocalObservable = ОЈ (K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•) IsK4Invariant

  -- В§ Global admissible observables collapse to four canonical codes.
  data GlobalObservable : Set where
    go-boundary go-correlator go-observer go-channels : GlobalObservable

  eval-global-observable : GlobalObservable вҶ’ в„•
  eval-global-observable go-boundary   = boundary-quanta
  eval-global-observable go-correlator = max-sq-correlator
  eval-global-observable go-observer   = observer-directions
  eval-global-observable go-channels   = interaction-channels

  -- В§ Complete observable type
  Observable : Set
  Observable = LocalObservable вҠҺ GlobalObservable

  -- В§ Every invariant function has a unique value, independent of vertex
  invariant-value : (f : K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•) вҶ’ IsK4Invariant f вҶ’ в„•
  invariant-value f _ = f vвӮҖ

  invariant-any-vertex : (f : K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•) вҶ’ (inv : IsK4Invariant f) вҶ’
    (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ f v вүЎ invariant-value f inv
  invariant-any-vertex f inv v = inv v vвӮҖ

  observable-value : Observable вҶ’ в„•
  observable-value (injвӮҒ (f , inv)) = invariant-value f inv
  observable-value (injвӮӮ g) = eval-global-observable g

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Local observable witnesses}

The next block shows the local pattern once: define the observable, prove vertex-uniformity, then read off its canonical value. Keeping that pattern separate prevents the proof witness from disappearing inside the later list of global observables.

\begin{code}

  -- В§ Signal-directions is a local observable
  signal-invariant : IsK4Invariant signal-directions
  signal-invariant v w =
    trans (signal-is-degree v) (sym (signal-is-degree w))

  signal-observable : LocalObservable
  signal-observable = signal-directions , signal-invariant

  -- В§ Observed value is degree = 3
  signal-observed-value : invariant-value signal-directions signal-invariant
                        вүЎ degree-K4
  signal-observed-value = refl

  -- В§ Adjacency row sum is a local observable
  adj-row : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•
  adj-row v w = adjacent v w

  adj-row-sum-invariant : IsK4Invariant (О» v вҶ’
    adj-row v vвӮҖ + adj-row v vвӮҒ + adj-row v vвӮӮ + adj-row v vвӮғ)
  adj-row-sum-invariant v w =
    trans (law-adjacency-degree v) (sym (law-adjacency-degree w))

\end{code}

\subsubsection*{Global observable witnesses}

An unrestricted numeric container for the global layer reintroduces descriptive freedom. A natural number written down after the graph is fixed does not become a global observable by being named. The admissibility filter therefore has to close the space itself. Only four primitive global classes survive: boundary, correlator, observer, and channels. Everything else in the downstream discussion is either a value of this basis or a derived ledger invariant, not a fifth admissible global observable.

\textbf{Constraint:} A global observable must be presentation-independent and forced by the invariant spine of $K_4$. If the global layer were identified with arbitrary naturals, the observable principle would fail exactly where it is supposed to become strongest.

\textbf{Elimination:} Any global sector wider than a finite four-code basis is eliminated by the presentation-independence and automorphism-invariance constraints. The surviving admissible sector is represented by exactly four codes, together with a canonical evaluation map into the already forced graph quantities.

\textbf{Consequence:} Global admissibility is an elimination statement rather than a naming convention. The space of primitive global observables is closed, finite, and exhausted by the four surviving codes.

\begin{code}

  -- В§ Global observables (invariant by construction)
  obs-boundary   : Observable
  obs-boundary   = injвӮӮ go-boundary

  obs-correlator : Observable
  obs-correlator = injвӮӮ go-correlator

  obs-observer   : Observable
  obs-observer   = injвӮӮ go-observer

  obs-channels   : Observable
  obs-channels   = injвӮӮ go-channels

  global-observable-exhaustive :
    (g : GlobalObservable) вҶ’
    (g вүЎ go-boundary)
    вҠҺ ((g вүЎ go-correlator)
    вҠҺ ((g вүЎ go-observer) вҠҺ (g вүЎ go-channels)))
  global-observable-exhaustive go-boundary   = injвӮҒ refl
  global-observable-exhaustive go-correlator = injвӮӮ (injвӮҒ refl)
  global-observable-exhaustive go-observer   = injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ (injвӮҒ refl))
  global-observable-exhaustive go-channels   = injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ (injвӮӮ refl))

  -- В§ Derived ledger invariants remain observable values, but not new primitive classes.
  obs-eval-value : в„•
  obs-eval-value = simplex-eval

  obs-clifford-value : в„•
  obs-clifford-value = clifford-dimension

  obs-hierarchy-value : в„•
  obs-hierarchy-value = hierarchy-exponent

  obs-euler-value : в„•
  obs-euler-value = eulerChar-computed
\end{code}

%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
\chapter{K$_4$ Vertex Permutations}
\label{ch:k4-vertex-permutations}
%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

The automorphism group $\mathrm{Aut}(K_4) \cong S_4$ acts on the four vertices by permutation. For a complete graph, every bijection is a graph automorphism---adjacency depends only on distinctness, and bijections preserve distinctness. The group acts $2$-transitively: for any two ordered pairs of distinct vertices, some permutation maps one to the other.

This chapter builds the formal infrastructure. The strategy re-uses the permutation machinery already proven for \texttt{EndoCase}---transpositions, involutivity, transitivity---by lifting it through an explicit bijection $\texttt{K4Vertex} \leftrightarrow \texttt{EndoCase}$. No case table is re-proved; the $64$-entry involution table for \texttt{swapEndo} does all the work.

\textbf{Constraint:} The permutation group must act on \texttt{K4Vertex} with full bijectivity proofs, not merely produce functions.

\textbf{Construction:} Any permutation structure that lacks full bijectivity proofs is eliminated by the round-trip requirement. The only surviving form is a record \texttt{K4Perm} packaging transport and inverse with round-trip laws. Transpositions and compositions inherit these from \texttt{EndoPerm}. Decidable equality on \texttt{K4Vertex} provides the case analysis that the adjacency-preservation proof requires.

\textbf{Consequence:} Any $\mathbb{N}$-valued function on vertex pairs that is invariant under all \texttt{K4Perm} has a single uniform value on all off-diagonal entries. This is the core tool the forced-propagation elimination will use.

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Vertex bijection}
\label{sec:k4-perms:bijection}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The bijection between \texttt{K4Vertex} and \texttt{EndoCase} maps constructors to constructors and round-trips in both directions.

\begin{code}
k4-to-endo : K4Vertex вҶ’ EndoCase
k4-to-endo vвӮҖ = case-constL
k4-to-endo vвӮҒ = case-constR
k4-to-endo vвӮӮ = case-id
k4-to-endo vвӮғ = case-dual

endo-to-k4 : EndoCase вҶ’ K4Vertex
endo-to-k4 case-constL = vвӮҖ
endo-to-k4 case-constR = vвӮҒ
endo-to-k4 case-id     = vвӮӮ
endo-to-k4 case-dual   = vвӮғ

k4-endo-k4 : (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ endo-to-k4 (k4-to-endo v) вүЎ v
k4-endo-k4 vвӮҖ = refl
k4-endo-k4 vвӮҒ = refl
k4-endo-k4 vвӮӮ = refl
k4-endo-k4 vвӮғ = refl

endo-k4-endo : (e : EndoCase) вҶ’ k4-to-endo (endo-to-k4 e) вүЎ e
endo-k4-endo case-constL = refl
endo-k4-endo case-constR = refl
endo-k4-endo case-id     = refl
endo-k4-endo case-dual   = refl
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Permutation record and lifting}
\label{sec:k4-perms:record}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

A \texttt{K4Perm} is a bijection on \texttt{K4Vertex}: forward and inverse maps with round-trip proofs. The function \texttt{liftPerm} converts any \texttt{EndoPerm} into a \texttt{K4Perm} by conjugating through the vertex bijection.

\begin{code}
record K4Perm : Set where
  field
    k4to       : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex
    k4from     : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex
    k4-to-from : (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ k4to (k4from v) вүЎ v
    k4-from-to : (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ k4from (k4to v) вүЎ v

open K4Perm public

liftPerm : EndoPerm вҶ’ K4Perm
liftPerm Пғ = record
  { k4to      = О» v вҶ’ endo-to-k4 (eto Пғ (k4-to-endo v))
  ; k4from    = О» v вҶ’ endo-to-k4 (efrom Пғ (k4-to-endo v))
  ; k4-to-from = О» v вҶ’
      trans (cong (О» e вҶ’ endo-to-k4 (eto Пғ e))
                  (endo-k4-endo (efrom Пғ (k4-to-endo v))))
            (trans (cong endo-to-k4 (eto-efrom Пғ (k4-to-endo v)))
                   (k4-endo-k4 v))
  ; k4-from-to = О» v вҶ’
      trans (cong (О» e вҶ’ endo-to-k4 (efrom Пғ e))
                  (endo-k4-endo (eto Пғ (k4-to-endo v))))
            (trans (cong endo-to-k4 (efrom-eto Пғ (k4-to-endo v)))
                   (k4-endo-k4 v))
  }
\end{code}

Composition of two permutations inherits bijectivity from the components.

\begin{code}
k4Perm-comp : K4Perm вҶ’ K4Perm вҶ’ K4Perm
k4Perm-comp Пғ П„ = record
  { k4to      = О» v вҶ’ k4to Пғ (k4to П„ v)
  ; k4from    = О» v вҶ’ k4from П„ (k4from Пғ v)
  ; k4-to-from = О» v вҶ’
      trans (cong (k4to Пғ) (k4-to-from П„ (k4from Пғ v)))
            (k4-to-from Пғ v)
  ; k4-from-to = О» v вҶ’
      trans (cong (k4from П„) (k4-from-to Пғ (k4to П„ v)))
            (k4-from-to П„ v)
  }
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Decidable equality and adjacency lemmas}
\label{sec:k4-perms:decidable-equality}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

Decidable equality on \texttt{K4Vertex} enables proofs that branch on whether two vertices coincide. It is forced by constructor discrimination: the four constructors are pairwise distinguishable.

\begin{code}
K4-decEq : (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ (v вүЎ w) вҠҺ (v вү  w)
K4-decEq vвӮҖ vвӮҖ = injвӮҒ refl
K4-decEq vвӮҒ vвӮҒ = injвӮҒ refl
K4-decEq vвӮӮ vвӮӮ = injвӮҒ refl
K4-decEq vвӮғ vвӮғ = injвӮҒ refl
K4-decEq vвӮҖ vвӮҒ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮҖ vвӮӮ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮҖ vвӮғ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮҒ vвӮҖ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮҒ vвӮӮ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮҒ vвӮғ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮӮ vвӮҖ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮӮ vвӮҒ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮӮ vвӮғ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮғ vвӮҖ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮғ vвӮҒ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
K4-decEq vвӮғ vвӮӮ = injвӮӮ (О» ())
\end{code}

Two structural facts about \texttt{adjacent}: it returns~$0$ on the diagonal and~$1$ on every off-diagonal pair. Together they say that adjacency in $K_4$ is exactly distinctness.

\begin{code}
adj-self : (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ adjacent v v вүЎ 0
adj-self vвӮҖ = refl
adj-self vвӮҒ = refl
adj-self vвӮӮ = refl
adj-self vвӮғ = refl

K4-distinct-adj : (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’ adjacent v w вүЎ 1
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҖ vвӮҖ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҖ vвӮҒ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҖ vвӮӮ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҖ vвӮғ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҒ vвӮҖ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҒ vвӮҒ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҒ vвӮӮ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮҒ vвӮғ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮӮ vвӮҖ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮӮ vвӮҒ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮӮ vвӮӮ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
K4-distinct-adj vвӮӮ vвӮғ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮғ vвӮҖ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮғ vвӮҒ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮғ vвӮӮ _  = refl
K4-distinct-adj vвӮғ vвӮғ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
\end{code}

Injectivity of any \texttt{K4Perm} follows from the round-trip law: compose with the inverse on both sides.

\begin{code}
k4-injective : (Пғ : K4Perm) вҶ’ (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’
  k4to Пғ v вүЎ k4to Пғ w вҶ’ v вүЎ w
k4-injective Пғ v w p =
  trans (sym (k4-from-to Пғ v))
        (trans (cong (k4from Пғ) p)
               (k4-from-to Пғ w))
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Transitivity and 2-transitivity}
\label{sec:k4-perms:transitivity}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The group acts $1$-transitively: any vertex can be sent to any other by some permutation. The proof lifts \texttt{endoPerm-send} through the bijection.

\begin{code}
k4Perm-send : (a a' : K4Vertex) вҶ’ ОЈ K4Perm (О» Пғ вҶ’ k4to Пғ a вүЎ a')
k4Perm-send a a' =
  let ep = endoPerm-send (k4-to-endo a) (k4-to-endo a')
  in (liftPerm (fst ep) ,
      trans (cong endo-to-k4 (snd ep)) (k4-endo-k4 a'))
\end{code}

$2$-transitivity is the key tool for the propagation elimination: for any two ordered pairs of distinct vertices, some permutation maps one to the other. The reference pair is $(v_0, v_1)$. Each of the $12$ off-diagonal targets is reached by at most two transpositions. Every case reduces by computation to \texttt{refl}.

\begin{code}
edge-map : (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’
  ОЈ K4Perm (О» Пғ вҶ’ ОЈ (k4to Пғ vвӮҖ вүЎ v) (О» _ вҶ’ k4to Пғ vвӮҒ вүЎ w))
edge-map vвӮҖ vвӮҖ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
edge-map vвӮҖ vвӮҒ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-constL)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮҖ vвӮӮ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮҖ vвӮғ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮҒ vвӮҖ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-constR)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮҒ vвӮҒ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
edge-map vвӮҒ vвӮӮ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮҒ vвӮғ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮӮ vвӮҖ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮӮ vвӮҒ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮӮ vвӮӮ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
edge-map vвӮӮ vвӮғ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-id))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮғ vвӮҖ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-dual))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮғ vвӮҒ _  = (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual)
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮғ vвӮӮ _  = (k4Perm-comp (liftPerm (permSwap case-constL case-dual))
                                   (liftPerm (permSwap case-constR case-id))
                     , (refl , refl))
edge-map vвӮғ vвӮғ ne = вҠҘ-elim (ne refl)
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Adjacency preservation}
\label{sec:k4-perms:adjacency-preservation}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

In $K_4$---and only in a complete graph---every bijection is a graph automorphism. The proof does not inspect the permutation: it branches on whether the two vertices coincide. On the diagonal, both sides equal~$0$; off-diagonal, injectivity forces both sides to~$1$.

\begin{code}
k4-perm-preserves-adj : (Пғ : K4Perm) вҶ’ (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’
  adjacent v w вүЎ adjacent (k4to Пғ v) (k4to Пғ w)
k4-perm-preserves-adj Пғ v w with K4-decEq v w
... | injвӮҒ refl = trans (adj-self v) (sym (adj-self (k4to Пғ v)))
... | injвӮӮ ne   = trans (K4-distinct-adj v w ne)
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj (k4to Пғ v) (k4to Пғ w)
                              (О» p вҶ’ ne (k4-injective Пғ v w p))))
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{K4-invariance and uniform edge weight}
\label{sec:k4-perms:invariance}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

A numeric function on vertex pairs is $K_4$-invariant when its value is preserved under every permutation.

\begin{code}
IsK4PermInvariant : (K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•) вҶ’ Set
IsK4PermInvariant f = (Пғ : K4Perm) вҶ’ (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’
  f v w вүЎ f (k4to Пғ v) (k4to Пғ w)
\end{code}

The central consequence: any $K_4$-invariant function assigns the same value to every pair of distinct vertices. The proof picks two permutations---one from \texttt{edge-map} for each target pair---and chains the invariance equalities through the common reference value $f\, v_0\, v_1$.

\begin{code}
invariant-uniform-edges : (f : K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•) вҶ’
  IsK4PermInvariant f вҶ’
  (v w v' w' : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’ v' вү  w' вҶ’ f v w вүЎ f v' w'
invariant-uniform-edges f inv v w v' w' ne ne' =
  trans (sym (to-ref v w ne)) (to-ref v' w' ne')
  where
    to-ref : (a b : K4Vertex) вҶ’ a вү  b вҶ’ f vвӮҖ vвӮҒ вүЎ f a b
    to-ref a b ne-ab =
      let em = edge-map a b ne-ab
          Пғ  = fst em
          p  = fst (snd em)
          q  = snd (snd em)
      in trans (inv Пғ vвӮҖ vвӮҒ)
               (trans (cong (О» x вҶ’ f x (k4to Пғ vвӮҒ)) p)
                      (cong (f a) q))
\end{code}

    This is the exact place where the admissibility architecture must sit. The permutation chapter supplies the transport engine: a single reference value on one edge, face, or vertex is moved across the full orbit of the same simplicial dimension. The three unit-constant chapters therefore no longer need to postulate pointwise lower and upper bounds on every simplex. It is enough to bracket one reference simplex and combine that bracket with invariance and the local diagonal law. The next three chapters apply that same pattern in dimensions $1$, $2$, and $0$ respectively.


%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
\chapter{Forced Propagation}
\label{ch:forced-propagation}
%в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

The arithmetic identity $\texttt{max-propagation-per-edge} \equiv 1$ was proved earlier by reduction. That identity computes the right number but does not eliminate alternatives: it never defines what a propagation law \emph{is}, so it never shows that rival laws fail.

This chapter closes that gap. A propagation law is any numeric function on vertex pairs, subject to four independent structural constraints---each of which admits genuine alternatives on its own. Only their intersection is a singleton. The elimination is not a case split. It is a sandwich argument: automorphism invariance collapses the off-diagonal entries to a single value $r$, the lower bound forces $1 \le r$, the upper bound forces $r \le 1$, and antisymmetry closes to $r = 1$.

\textbf{Constraint:} The candidate space is deliberately wide: \emph{any} function $K_4\text{Vertex} \to K_4\text{Vertex} \to \mathbb{N}$. Candidates such as $P \equiv 2$ on all edges, or $P \equiv 3$ on one edge and $1$ elsewhere, are included and must be eliminated by the filter, not excluded by the definition.

\textbf{Elimination:} Four filters are applied in sequence:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \emph{Locality} --- zero on the diagonal.
\item \emph{Edge support} --- every off-diagonal entry is at least $1$.
\item \emph{$K_4$-invariance} --- the law is preserved under every vertex permutation.
\item \emph{Boundedness} --- every entry is at most $1$.
\end{enumerate}
Filters 2 and 4 are the wide brackets; filter 3 does the structural work. After invariance, the $12$ off-diagonal values collapse to a single unknown $r = P\,v_0\,v_1$. Edge support gives $1 \le r$; boundedness gives $r \le 1$; antisymmetry of $\le$ yields $r \equiv 1$. The diagonal is $0$ by locality. Together: $P \equiv \texttt{adjacent}$.

\textbf{Consequence:} The propagation rate is forced without enumerating vertex pairs. The earlier arithmetic computation becomes a corollary of a structural elimination.

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Propagation candidates}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:candidates}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

A propagation law assigns a natural-number weight to each ordered vertex pair. No constraint is placed yet---the type is deliberately wide. The constant-$2$ law, the constant-$7$ law, and every non-uniform variant are all members of this type.

\begin{code}
PropagationLaw : Set
PropagationLaw = K4Vertex вҶ’ K4Vertex вҶ’ в„•
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{The four admissibility filters}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:filters}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

Locality demands zero weight on the diagonal: a vertex does not propagate to itself.

\begin{code}
IsLocal : PropagationLaw вҶ’ Set
IsLocal P = (v : K4Vertex) вҶ’ P v v вүЎ 0
\end{code}

Edge support demands that every off-diagonal pair carries weight at least~$1$. This is deliberately weak---it admits $P = 2$, $P = 5$, any non-zero value.

\begin{code}
HasEdgeSupport : PropagationLaw вҶ’ Set
HasEdgeSupport P = (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’ 1 вүӨ P v w

ReferenceEdgeLower : PropagationLaw вҶ’ Set
ReferenceEdgeLower P = 1 вүӨ P vвӮҖ vвӮҒ
\end{code}

Boundedness demands that no single edge carries weight above~$1$. Structurally: propagation is local, with at most one unit of signal per direct link. No shortcutting, no amplification. This is the upper bracket that meets the lower bracket from edge support.

\begin{code}
IsBounded : PropagationLaw вҶ’ Set
IsBounded P = (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ P v w вүӨ 1

ReferenceEdgeUpper : PropagationLaw вҶ’ Set
ReferenceEdgeUpper P = P vвӮҖ vвӮҒ вүӨ 1
\end{code}

$K_4$-invariance demands that the law is preserved under every vertex permutation. This is the structural filter that, in the previous chapter, was shown to collapse all off-diagonal entries to a single value.

The full admissibility record packages all four constraints. Each constraint admits genuine alternatives on its own; only their intersection is a singleton.

\begin{code}
record AdmissiblePropagation (P : PropagationLaw) : Set where
  field
    local     : IsLocal P
    invariant : IsK4PermInvariant P
    ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower P
    ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper P
\end{code}

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{The elimination}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:elimination}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The elimination proceeds without enumerating vertex pairs. It uses three lemmas, each of which is structural rather than computational.

\paragraph{Step 1: Invariance collapses off-diagonal to one value.}
By \texttt{invariant-uniform-edges} (proved in the previous chapter), any $K_4$-invariant function assigns the same value $r$ to every pair of distinct vertices. The reference value is $P\,v_0\,v_1$.

\begin{code}
propagation-uniform : (P : PropagationLaw) вҶ’ IsK4PermInvariant P вҶ’
  (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’ P v w вүЎ P vвӮҖ vвӮҒ
propagation-uniform P inv v w ne =
  invariant-uniform-edges P inv v w vвӮҖ vвӮҒ ne (О» ())
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 2: Invariance transports the brackets.}
The lower and upper brackets are imposed only on the reference edge $(v_0,v_1)$. Invariance transports them to every off-diagonal edge.

\begin{code}
propagation-supported : (P : PropagationLaw) вҶ’ AdmissiblePropagation P вҶ’
  (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’ 1 вүӨ P v w
propagation-supported P adm v w ne =
  subst (О» n вҶ’ 1 вүӨ n)
    (sym (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne))
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-lower adm)

propagation-bounded : (P : PropagationLaw) вҶ’ AdmissiblePropagation P вҶ’
  (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ P v w вүӨ 1
propagation-bounded P adm v w with K4-decEq v w
... | injвӮҒ refl = subst (О» n вҶ’ n вүӨ 1) (sym (AdmissiblePropagation.local adm v)) zвүӨn
... | injвӮӮ ne   = subst (О» n вҶ’ n вүӨ 1)
                      (sym (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne))
                      (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-upper adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 3: The sandwich.}
The transported lower bound gives $1 \le r$. The transported upper bound gives $r \le 1$. Antisymmetry of $\le$ closes to $r \equiv 1$.

\begin{code}
vвӮҖвү vвӮҒ : vвӮҖ вү  vвӮҒ
vвӮҖвү vвӮҒ ()

propagation-edge-value : (P : PropagationLaw) вҶ’ AdmissiblePropagation P вҶ’
  P vвӮҖ vвӮҒ вүЎ 1
propagation-edge-value P adm =
  вүӨ-antisym
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-upper adm)
    (AdmissiblePropagation.ref-lower adm)
\end{code}

\paragraph{Step 4: Assembly.}
Every off-diagonal entry equals $1$; every diagonal entry equals $0$. Both agree with \texttt{adjacent}, whose definition is exactly $0$ on the diagonal and $1$ elsewhere.

\begin{code}
propagation-off-diag : (P : PropagationLaw) вҶ’ AdmissiblePropagation P вҶ’
  (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ v вү  w вҶ’ P v w вүЎ 1
propagation-off-diag P adm v w ne =
  trans (propagation-uniform P (AdmissiblePropagation.invariant adm) v w ne)
        (propagation-edge-value P adm)

propagation-forced : (P : PropagationLaw) вҶ’ AdmissiblePropagation P вҶ’
  (v w : K4Vertex) вҶ’ P v w вүЎ adjacent v w
propagation-forced P adm v w with K4-decEq v w
... | injвӮҒ refl = trans (AdmissiblePropagation.local adm v)
                        (sym (adj-self v))
... | injвӮӮ ne   = trans (propagation-off-diag P adm v w ne)
                        (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne))
\end{code}

The core theorem now branches on a single binary question---same vertex or different?---not on $16$ constructor pairs. The structural work is done by invariance; the arithmetic is done by the sandwich.

%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\section{Existence}
\label{sec:forced-propagation:existence}
%в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

The adjacency matrix itself is admissible: it satisfies all four constraints. Locality and boundedness hold by computation. Edge support follows from \texttt{K4-distinct-adj}. Invariance follows from \texttt{k4-perm-preserves-adj}.

\begin{code}
adjacent-is-local : IsLocal adjacent
adjacent-is-local vвӮҖ = refl
adjacent-is-local vвӮҒ = refl
adjacent-is-local vвӮӮ = refl
adjacent-is-local vвӮғ = refl

adjacent-supported : HasEdgeSupport adjacent
adjacent-supported v w ne = subst (О» n вҶ’ 1 вүӨ n)
  (sym (K4-distinct-adj v w ne)) (sвүӨs zвүӨn)

adjacent-bounded : IsBounded adjacent
adjacent-bounded vвӮҖ vвӮҖ = zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҖ vвӮҒ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҖ vвӮӮ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҖ vвӮғ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҒ vвӮҖ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҒ vвӮҒ = zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҒ vвӮӮ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮҒ vвӮғ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮӮ vвӮҖ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮӮ vвӮҒ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮӮ vвӮӮ = zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮӮ vвӮғ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮғ vвӮҖ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮғ vвӮҒ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮғ vвӮӮ = sвүӨs zвүӨn
adjacent-bounded vвӮғ vвӮғ = zвүӨn

adjacent-invariant : IsK4PermInvariant adjacent
adjacent-invariant Пғ v w = k4-perm-preserves-adj Пғ v w

adjacent-ref-lower : ReferenceEdgeLower adjacent
adjacent-ref-lower = sвүӨs zвүӨn

adjacent-ref-upper : ReferenceEdgeUpper adjacent
adjacent-ref-upper = sвүӨs zвүӨn
